title: Attention-Guided Hybrid Learning for Tree Species Classification in Mining Restoration Areas Using UAV Hyperspectral and LiDAR Data
authors: Guo Wang, Cheng Wang, Yuncheng Deng, Jinliang Wang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Accurate tree species classification in open-pit mining regions is crucial for assessing ecological restoration effectiveness. However, it remains challenging due to extreme surface reflectance, dust contamination, and spectral whitening effects prevalent. To address these challenges, this letter proposes an Attention-Guided Misclassification-Aware Hybrid Learning (AG-MAHL) framework for tree species classification. Unlike existing fusion methods that simply concatenate features or ignore hard samples, our framework introduces (i) an attention-guided fusion strategy to align spectral-structural features under high-reflectance conditions, and (ii) a misclassification-aware learning mechanism that dynamically reweights difficult samples. Experiments conducted at the Haikou Phosphogypsum Tailings Reservoir in Kunming, China, demonstrate that the proposed AG-MAHL framework achieves an overall accuracy (OA) of 0.8571, a weighted F1-score of 0.8423, and a balanced accuracy of 0.8402. This performance surpasses that of conventional machine learning methods and representative deep learning baselines. The results indicate that the proposed attention-guided hyperspectral–LiDAR fusion, coupled with misclassification-aware learning, offers an effective solution for tree species classification in spectrally challenging open-pit mining restoration environments.

title: Experimental Study on Failure Mechanism of 4H-SiC MOSFETs Under Unclamped Inductive Switching (UIS) Stress Conditions
authors: Amir Shahzad, Na Ren, Liang Qiao, Xinke Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Device and Materials Reliability
abstract: This paper discusses a comprehensive reliability evaluation of a 1200-V 4H-SiC planar MOSFET fabricated in-house for research purposes, subjected to Unclamped Inductive Switching (UIS) stress under a wide temperature range from 25°C to 175°C. Avalanche current, breakdown voltage, and avalanche energy were recorded in a systematic way to measure temperature-dependent ruggedness. The examination of post-stress failure by ultrasonic scanning, decapsulation, OBIRCH/EMMI, and FIB-SEM showed that a series of degradation processes occur, starting with early charge trapping and slight shifts of leakage, then through the formation of localized hot-spots, weakening of the gate-oxide, and the erosion of metallization. Current crowding around the source region linked by a single bond wire was the cause of catastrophic failures, which caused a short between gate and source and between drain and source once thermal runaway and parasitic BJT activation had taken place. It was demonstrated that temperature is a strong accelerator of degradation, decreasing the avalanche capability and increasing structural damage. The SEM cross-section shows that the hotspot that develops at the corner of the p+ region where the highest accumulation of electric field takes place, causes p-well degradation during early failure. These results provide new insight into the thermal-electrical overstress limits of SiC MOSFETs and highlight the critical influence of packaging-dependent current distribution on avalanche reliability.

title: Hybrid YOLO Feature Extraction with RF–AdaBoost Classifier for Accurate Breast Tumor Detection
authors: Peramalasetty Ramesh, Suneetha Rikhari, Jayaram Boga, Seeniappan Kaliappan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The malignancy is the highest diagnosed breast cancer in India with one out of every 25.8 being breast cancer. The problem of tumor stages is a critical factor in treatment planning and improving patients survival rates due to accurate and early diagnoses. In this paper, there is a hybrid Cancer Classification Model (CCM) that uses ultrasound images which have been processed to detect and classify early on cancerous tumours as benign or malignant with estimation of the stages of disease progression and probability of survival.The proposed CCM uses YOLO to rapidly and precisely localize the tumors and uses a hybrid Random Forest (RF)-AdaBoost ensemble classifier to classify the tumors using robust features, and the result is to have high diagnostic accuracy at low computational complexity. Combining the feature extraction with deep learning and the ensemble learning enhances the quality of classification and supports clinical decision-making.The experiment conducted between the YOLO + RF–AdaBoost system and the traditional classifiers like Naive-Bayes and Decision Tree classifiers. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is proved by experimental results showing a high level of accuracy of 96 per cent, which proves the authoritative usage in detecting breast cancer at the initial early stages (I and II).

title: Implementation of ML based Routing Algorithms for 5G Wireless Networks Architectures
authors: Kandala Kalyana Srinivas, K Jyostna, Chevella Anil Kumar, G Varshini, D Karthik, G L V Ramakrishna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Performance is based on routing efficiency and adaptability regardless of the system architecture.In this paper we are going to compare three broadly known routing algorithms: Enhanced Opportunistic Routing with Learning (EORL), Open Shortest Path First (OSPF), Software Defined Networking with Q-learning (SDN-Q).The parameters used for testing are throughput ,packet delivery ratio, performance benchmarks, and overall scalability. This paper provides through insights into each algorithm’s advantages and disadvantages in contemporary networks focusing on adaptability and autonomous smart-decision making system. These findings are useful for tailoring methods for effective routing in the networks ranging from legacy systems to AI-controlled and programmable networks.

title: Design and Simulation of a Compact Axial and Radial Corrugated Horn Antenna for Ku-Band Defense and Aerospace Applications
authors: Rakshitha M, Monikka P, Mrunal A Marihal, P. Raja Ramana Rao, Subrata Kumar Datta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a compact axial and radial corrugated horn antenna for Ku-Band defense and aerospace applications. The antenna employs a combination of axial and radial corrugations to achieve efficient mode conversion into the hybrid HE<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> mode, resulting in improved beam quality with low cross-polarization and reduced sidelobe levels. An axially corrugated profile shortens the horn length while maintaining high gain and radiation efficiency, making it suitable for defense and aerospace systems where size and performance are critical. The antenna was designed and analyzed using CST Microwave Studio. Simulation results show that the antenna achieves gain above 20 dBi with low sidelobe levels across the Ku-Band, demonstrating its potential for next-generation defense and aerospace platforms.

title: Simulation of a Dual-Parameter Optical Fiber Sensor Based on OptiSystem Modeling
authors: Anis Farisha Alias, Nur Najahatul Huda Saris, Azura Hamzah, Nurul Ashikin Daud, Nadiatulhuda Zulkifli, Marinah Othman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: This paper presents the modeling of a Fiber Bragg Grating (FBG)-based sensing system for dual-parameter temperature and strain measurement using OptiSystem simulation. Conventional FBG sensors are commonly limited to single-parameter sensing, resulting in cross-sensitivity issues in applications that require simultaneous temperature and strain monitoring. The proposed model analyzes wavelength shift responses under varying strain and temperature conditions. Optimization is carried out by adjusting the grating period, thermal expansion coefficient, and grating length, followed by the selection of optimal values based on reliability analysis. Performance evaluation using coefficient of variation indicates values exceeding 10 %, suggesting instability under the selected configuration. Linearity assessment conducted by independently varying strain and temperature under the optimized setup yields linearity values of 0.4495 and 0.2272, respectively. These results indicate that further refinement of structural parameters is necessary to improve the reliability and linearity of the proposed dual-parameter FBG sensing system.

title: KrishnaVani: Transformer-Based Conversational AI Trained on the Bhagavad Gita for Spiritual Dialogue Generation
authors: Shubh Rastogi, Jai Batra, Madhumita Mandal, Prashant Srivastava, Gurpreet Kaur, Vinayak Majhi, Sujata Pandey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This paper presents KrishnaVani, a transformer-based conversational AI system fine-tuned on the Bhagavad Gita to enable contextually relevant and spiritually coherent dialogue generation. Leveraging the GPT-2 Large architecture and a curated dataset of scripture verses with aligned English translations, the system was trained to emulate the linguistic style and philosophical depth characteristic of Krishna’s teachings. The fine-tuning process achieved a validation perplexity of 3.96, with qualitative assessments confirming improvements in thematic alignment, response fluency, and cultural authenticity over baseline models. A web-based user interface was developed to facilitate interactive engagement, supporting real-time responses and multi-turn conversations. The proposed system demonstrates the potential of domain-specific transformer models in preserving and disseminating philosophical literature through AI-driven conversational agents. Future work will extend the corpus to include multilingual versions of the Bhagavad Gita and incorporate advanced alignment techniques such as reinforcement learning with human feedback to further improve response accuracy and ethical alignment.

title: Generative AI–Enabled OCPP Intelligence: A Predictive Framework for Autonomous EV Charger Diagnostics and Self-Healing Networks
authors: Pradeep Anjuru
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The rapid expansion of EV charging networks has made reliability and automation critical, yet global studies show 23–27% of fast chargers remain non-operational each month due to communication failures, connector faults, and power-module issues. Existing OCPP-based monitoring is largely reactive, relying on threshold rules with no predictive or self-healing capability. This paper introduces GA–OCPP Intelligence, a generative-AI diagnostic and autonomous-healing framework integrated with OCPP 1.6/2.0.1. Using a VAE–Transformer model, it learns the joint probability distribution of OCPP messages, charger telemetry, and grid context. Omnet++ simulations calibrated with real reliability data show major improvements: AUC 0.98 vs. 0.94 (LSTM) and 0.88 (thresholds), and a reduction in downtime from 5.2 to 2.4 hours per charger monthly. The system also resolves up to 35% of faults automatically through OCPP Reset, Change Availability, and firmware-update commands—surpassing the industry’s 20–30% self-healing range.

title: A Hybrid ECM–Particle Filter Framework with Machine Learning Enhancement for Accurate SOC/SOH Estimation in Lithium-Ion Battery Management Systems
authors: Nirmala M, Mukund V, Nehal M, Nigin S, Shyam Michael R
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries have is one of the main energy storage technologies in industrial applications, renewable energy systems, and electric vehicles because of their efficiency, long cycle life, and energy density. To ensure safety and dependability, accurate estimation of battery systems, such as State of Charge (SOC) and State of Health (SOH), is crucial. Various algorithms which use electrical measurements and computational models is must as these states cannot be directly measured with sensors. Coulomb counting and Kalman filter are methods which are examples of conventional SOC estimation techniques that have accumulated errors and perform poorly in nonlinear and dynamic operating conditions. Similarly, conventional SOH estimation techniques are not ideal for real-time applications as they rely on offline capacity tests and internal resistance measurements. The proposed system uses a hybrid Battery Management System (BMS) framework along with a CatBoost machine learning algorithm for long-term SOH and Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction with a physics-based Equivalent Circuit Model (ECM) with a Particle Filter (PF) for reliable real-time SOC estimation. While the PF offers probabilistic estimation under non-Gaussian noise and model uncertainty, the ECM captures nonlinear battery dynamics. Using historical battery data, the CatBoost model efficiently learns degradation patterns. The results which are produced using this system in MATLAB SIMULINK shows us that the proposed system outperforms the existing traditional estimation models

title: Simulation and Modeling of Biodegradable Capacitor
authors: Anjana J.G, Abhinav K.L, Ananthu A.C, B S Vishnu Chasanth, Dheeraj M, Unnikrishnan Jayan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Biodegradable capacitors (also referred to as green capacitors) are a trend to replace regular components in short-lived and disposable electronics. In this work, the TCAD was used to simulate the shapes of a MOS, MSM, and parallel plate capacitor, verify their electrical behavior with silicon dioxide, zinc oxide, and hafnium oxide materials and palladium, silver, or titanium electrodes. Depletion and Schottky barriers contributed to the strong variation in capacitance of MOS and MSM capacitors when voltage varied such that capacitors are useful in sensing but not in long-term storage. The parallel plate capacitors exhibited minimal variations to bias and remained constant thus they can be used in biodegradable capacitor designs. Such findings provide a starting point for selecting the most appropriate dielectrics and shapes of devices to be made in the future.

title: AI-Powered Finance Management Platform
authors: Deepthi C G, Jithesh J Shetty, I B Shreeya Jain, Lakshmi C R, Kiran Kumar D R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Introducing the AI-Powered Finance Management Platform: an integrated system for automating personal finance using modern cloud services and AI/ML. The platform includes real-time expense categorization, budget forecasting, fraud detection and alerts, savings and investment optimization, and a conversational financial assistant. It is built on a serverless, microservices-based architecture with technologies like Next.js (React) on the frontend, Clerk for authentication, Supabase (PostgreSQL) in the backend database layer, and Inngest for task orchestration. The key AI models currently in use include tree-based classifiers like XGBoost and Random Forest for transaction categorization and fraud detection, time series models like ARIMA and LSTM for budgeting and forecasting, and large language models like Google Gemini or GPT-4 for natural language advice. Explainable AI techniques such as SHAP are also used to make the predictions interpretable. The data flows from integrations with banks and users, via preprocessing such as parsing merchants and feature extraction into the ML services. Key objectives of this work include automated insights on budgeting, fraud anomaly alerts, personalized recommendations, and easy-to-use conversational access. Key objectives of the work are automated budgeting insights, fraud anomaly alerts, personalized recommendations, and easy conversational access. Evaluation covers classification metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and confusion matrices, forecasting error measured by MAPE, system latency indicated by API response times, and user feedback. Early results indicate high accuracy on test datasets-including 98% fraud detection accuracy with XGBoost-and low-latency real-time performance. Secure deployment is based on OAuth and JWT flows with end-to-end TLS and AES encryption. Further improvements will be related to federated learning for privacy, blockchain audit trails, real-time market data integration, and gamification. This platform resolves the fragmentation of outdated finance tools and helps users manage their finances in a much smarter, more personalized, and scalable way.

title: SHCF: Synergistic Hybrid Clustering Framework for Personalized Nutrition Recommendation and Endocrine Homeostasis using K-Means and Gaussian Mixture Models
authors: S Akshaya, S Jeevitha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The methodologies of nutrition recommendation over the past years have more or less centered on generalized eating habits with little regard to personal health conditions and hormonal levels. The nutritional value of foods is a major criterion in most of the existing methods of categorizing foods despite the fact that important factors like age, gender, and other physiological changes like puberty, pre and post menopause and andropause are not well considered. An intelligent nutrition recommendation system is suggested to classify people according to their age, gender, hormonal levels, and health issues like diabetes, high blood pressure, and cholesterol level levels using unsupervised machine learning methods. The suggested Synergistic Hybrid Clustering Framework (SHCF) is based on K-Means clustering, and Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM) to represent hard and probabilistic cluster boundaries. The data is preprocessed with encoding of labels, normalization of data and dimensionality reduction through Principal Component Analysis (PCA) keeping 95 percent of the data variance. Four clusters are distinguished with the help of both the individual models and the hybrid approach that are connected with various hormonal stages. The hybrid model incorporates the good features of K-Means and GMM, enhancing the stability of clusters, the accuracy and that which represents integration of nutritional patterns. According to the end cluster work, this system can create individual nutrition plans based on hormonal age and health history. The SHCF framework has better personalized and clustered performance in comparison to a single model. The findings and results show that combining hormonal and health determinants with hybrid machine learning methods is effective in intelligent data-based nutrition planning.

title: Simulation-Based Design of a Multimodal Vision-Guided Smart Wheelchair for Secure and Energy-Efficient Mobility
authors: Kavitha N. Pillai, Chinthala Ramesh, Anju Das, Margaret Mercy V, Deepthi Mariam John
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Individuals with severe mobility impairments often require continuous assistance to navigate indoor environments safely. Although smart wheelchairs have introduced automated control and sensing, many existing designs rely on a single input modality, lack integrated security measures, or overlook energy efficiency. This paper presents a simulation-based multimodal smart wheelchair framework that combines vision-guided navigation with eye-gaze, gesture, and EEG-assisted intent inputs. The architecture integrates virtual ultrasonic and infrared sensing for obstacle avoidance and a biometric-inspired authorization layer for secure operation. An energy-aware motion model, including simulated solar-assisted power contribution, enables evaluation of long-term sustainability. The system is implemented in a Python-based simulation using a differential-drive wheelchair and 2D occupancy maps. Experimental results show improved navigation stability, reduced collision likelihood, higher command adaptability, and lower estimated power usage compared to single-modality baselines. Overall, the proposed framework achieves up to a 24% improvement in navigation success and a 32–45% reduction in energy consumption compared to a baseline controller.

title: Design and Implementation of TriSense-DL for Intelligent Malware Detection
authors: M. Prem Nazeer, J. Relin Francis Raj, N. Deepa, C Gayathri, K. Muthulakshmi, S. Murali
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The rapid evolution of malware has rendered traditional signature-based security mechanisms ineffective against sophisticated and zero-day cyber threats. This work presents the design and implementation of TriSense-DL, an intelligent hybrid deep learning framework for advanced malware detection. The proposed system integrates static analysis, dynamic behavior analysis, and anomaly detection to achieve robust and accurate classification of malicious software. TriSense-DL employs Convolutional Neural Networks to extract structural features from executable files, Recurrent Neural Networks with Long Short-Term Memory units to model runtime behavioral patterns, and Autoencoders to identify anomalous deviations associated with previously unseen attacks. The outputs of these models are fused and processed by a Deep Neural Network classifier to generate final detection decisions. The framework is implemented as a modular engineering architecture that enables scalability, adaptability, and effective threat intelligence integration. Extensive preprocessing and model optimization techniques are applied to enhance generalization and reduce false positives. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that TriSense-DL outperforms standalone deep learning models in terms of detection accuracy, recall, and resilience to obfuscated malware. The anomaly-aware design significantly improves zero-day malware detection while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for real-world deployment. By combining multi-dimensional learning with feature fusion, TriSense-DL provides a comprehensive and intelligent malware detection solution. The proposed framework contributes to strengthening National Security by safeguarding digital systems and critical assets. Furthermore, it supports advancements in Information and communication technology through intelligent automation, promotes Innovation in cybersecurity engineering, and reinforces secure digital Infrastructure for modern computing environments.

title: An Enhanced Multiple Object Detection and Tracking Framework using YOLOv4-Lite and Kalman Filter for Low-Quality Images and Videos
authors: Vishal A. Aher, Satish R. Jondhale, Sachin V. Chaudhari, Balasaheb S. Agarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The latest advances in deep learning contributed significantly towards the domain of computer vision. The majority of investigations concentrated on objects detection in high resolution images/videos. The complexity of object tracking is increased by factors like image noise, changes in illumination, dynamic object motion, and occlusion of objects. i.e., low-quality video. Video quality is an important challenge for object detection. The main goal of this investigation work is to improve accuracy and making inferences of object detection from poor quality video/images. We developed a new technique for the single stage detector, YOLOv4 tiny model with pruning and Kalman filtering for object tracking in poor quality video. The stages of compression covered by the framework are sparsity induction, choosing the right filters and pruning them. The detector model for detecting object is sparsified in the Sparsity by using an optimum threshold improvement. In the stage of filter choice and pruning, based on filter weight sparse metrics, we select and discard filters in next two subsequent layers used for convolution. As a result, the model is smaller than the majority of compact designs available now on the market. The model with compressed model and Kalman filter which produces good quality image representation on the low-quality image/video. The proposed method has given 20% better accuracy as compared to original model. The Kalman filter help to find the output detected classes in the frames to frame for the tracking the identified object. The proposed YOLOv4-based model with the MobileNet SPP/ResNet152 backbone demonstrates superior performance, achieving 84% mAP, 79% accuracy, 72% F1-score, and the highest inference speed of 48 FPS with 14.87 ms per frame, outperforming the other compared models.

title: Wildfire Occurrence Analysis using Meteorological Data: A Comparative Study of Statistical Methods and State-of-the-Art Learning Models
authors: Anil Kumar, Soham Ghosh, Pooja Gowda
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Wildfires are a serious environmental and social issue that is highly affected by the meteorological fluctuation and fuel state. Models capable of capturing long-term temporal patterns and short-term nonlinear relationships are therefore necessary for accurate wildfire detection. This study is a comparative analysis of statistical time-series modeling and the state-of-the-art learning models to analyze the occurrence of wildfires using multi-source meteorological and fuel related variables. First, ARIMA and SARIMA models are used to describe the high-risk periods over time and identify the periods with statistically significant high-risk of wildfires. Then, event-level wildfire occurrence classification on classical machine learning and deep learning models, such as LSTM, Bi-LSTM, and GRU are used. Accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC -AUC are the metrics of model performance, and the recall-based thresholding is prioritized in order to minimize false wildfires. Experiments indicate that deep learning-based models surpass statistical and classical methods, and the GRU model has a test ROC -AUC of 0.796. Its findings indicate the trade-off between false alarms and detection sensitivity as well as the significance of threshold-aware assessment in wildfire early-warning systems.

title: Low-Light Medical Image Enhancement Using GANs with Clinical Constraints
authors: Sakthivel S, S. Dinakar Jose, V. Keerthiga, K. Balasubramanian, Pooja Vasant Koli, Sajiv G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The loss of visibility in crucial anatomical structures during low-light medical imaging remains a persistent challenge in ultrasound, endoscopy, and low-dose X-ray imaging, negatively impacting interpretive confidence and increasing the chances of errors. Available low-light image enhancement techniques improve global brightness of the images but virtually do not improve the utility of the images in diagnostics since the clinical structures are blurred or noise is amplified. This paper introduces a CC-GAN model that enhances images captured in low-light conditions while retaining anatomical integrity using anatomical prior information and constraints bounded by a radiologist. The model employs attention encoders, U-Net, PatchGAN discriminators, and clinical constraints modules dedicated to the imaging integration. The CC-GAN relies on the illumination-aware encodings to impose structural and edge accuracy decomposition. Evaluation of the model using a mixed-modality dataset containing 6240 low-light images yields a PSNR of dB 32.81, SSIM of 0.962, LPIPS of 0.082, and FID of 21.7 while outperforming 3 techniques in the domain. The radiologists reporting on the model noted a 28% improvement in the visibility of the lesion boundary and 22% reduction in artifact amplification relative to baseline GAN techniques. The CC-GAN model exhibited accurate anatomical reconstruction of the images through low-light enhancement. Thus, it can be embedded with image and CAD systems.

title: Bull and Bear Phase Detection of BankNifty using the convolution of LSTM and ADF
authors: Gouranga Duari, Bhupendera Kumar, Homi Shivanshu Purbey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Anyone familiar with the stock market is aware of the recurring bull and bear phases. However, the key question is whether traders and investors actually factor these phases into their decision to buy, sell or remain invested. This paper presents a comprehensive empirical analysis of the NIFTY BANK index by identifying and evaluating bull and bear market phases using statistical techniques. We utilized the Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) test to assess seasonality and stationarity of the time series data, and the Rate of Change (ROC) indicator to detect market momentum and trend shifts. An interactive Streamlit web interface was developed, enabling users to upload CSV datasets, visualize trends, and analyze market cycles year-wise. The project also includes performance evaluation metrics such as precision, recall, and accuracy for both ADF and ROC-based detections, validated against actual market phase data. The outcomes support better understanding of market dynamics and provide valuable tools for investment strategy development. [1]

title: Deep Generative Steganography for Multimodal Data: Integrating NLP and GANs for Secure Image-based Communication
authors: Veera Swamy Pittala, Maradani Chandra Naga Deep, Mekapothula Bhagya Lakshmi, Shaik Abzal
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The ever-rising use of digital communication platforms has placed significant importance on the need to have a private and secured transmission of secretly and safely embedded data, as traditional cryptography methods find it difficult to preserve secrecy and confidentiality of data to be transmitted, particularly where data may be text, voice, and so on. The challenge placed on cryptography methods has, therefore, encouraged the need to develop and construct a smart image steganography system that guaranteeably and securely embeds text and voice data into digital image files through deep learning models. The method combines generative adversarial learning to develop data embedded imperceptibly and incorporates Natural Language Processing to optimize text data embedded into the image system. The digital image data is coded through a generative adversarial network, hence promoting imperceptible data to be embedded and recovered from their embedded forms without causing distortion. This method can be supplemented with a language-driven summarization technique to summarize vital data to be embedded, particularly where data is of a lengthy text nature. The speech inputs are recognized and typed, and the typing is then encrypted to support dual inputs. The proposed method is implemented with the help of an interactive tool, which has the capability to do the decryption as well as the encryption. Near-negligible pixel-wise error correction is achieved on the experimental platform. Values of 35.96 and 30.55 are obtained on the secret and cover image errors, respectively, on the experimental platform with high precision related to data extraction.

title: Harnessing Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Generative AI Techniques for Social Media Sentiment Analysis with Text Classification
authors: Bhulakshmi Makkena
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Social media text and posts are analyzed through advanced computing and artificial intelligence systems to determine user sentiments. The word intensity and activities involved in their use are analyzed to determine user sentiments. Sentiment140 is used in this study with a corpus of 1.6 million labelled tweets and proposes a systematic way of classifying sentiments. The suggested algorithm entails preprocessing of data, tokenization, min-max normalization, feature extraction through TF-IDF, and class balancing using SMOTE. The processed data is trained with a DistilBERT model to learn contextual dependencies between and semantic structure of textual data. The experimental assessment proves that the suggested model is 98% accurate, 96% precise, with a recall of 96% and an F1-score of 98%, which is far more accurate and precise than the existing models, including Random Forest and Decision Tree and the BERT baseline. The results show that the proposed system works and is efficient, therefore it may be used for social media sentiment analysis on a big scale. This paper forms an excellent basis for future extensions, such as multilingual, multimodal and explainable sentiment classification systems.

title: A Co-Simulation Framework for Integrated Powertrain Modeling Using Predictive Control Algorithms in 48 V xEV Systems
authors: Sreedhar Reddy Aemalla, Subbarao Mopidevi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The increasing adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) necessitates accurate predictive modeling for enhanced energy efficiency, drivetrain performance, and thermal management. Existing studies often focus on simplified vehicle dynamics or isolated motor-drivetrain components, neglecting system-level interactions, torque distribution, and realistic operating conditions such as aerodynamic drag, rolling resistance, and road gradients. Such limitations reduce predictive accuracy, particularly under transient conditions and diverse drive cycles. To address these gaps, this work presents an integrated predictive control framework combining longitudinal vehicle dynamics, drivetrain modeling, and switched reluctance motor (SRM) performance within a MATLAB/Simulink co-simulation environment. The model incorporates differential, fixed gear, and drive shaft dynamics, and is validated across standard drive cycles (MIDC, UDDS, HWFET, US06). Results show high fidelity, with acceleration prediction errors (RMSE) below 0.043 m/s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, drivetrain torque deviations within 25 Nm, and SRM efficiency exceeding 90% in the optimal torque-speed region. This approach supports real-time predictive control and provides a robust foundation for drivetrain optimization and energy management in 48V electric vehicles

title: Empirical Comparison of LSTM and GRU for Bitcoin Price Prediction
authors: Agrata Gupta, Sivakumar R, Arul Kumar Natarajan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: As the cryptocurrency market exhibits remarkable volatility, there is an escalating demand for precise price prediction mechanisms. Many studies have examined the prediction of Bitcoin prices using various deep-learning algorithms. To anticipate Bitcoin prices, this study employs recurrent neural network (RNN) architectures, particularly Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) models. The primary objective of this research is to empirically compare the performance of these two deep learning models in forecasting Bitcoin prices. Experiments were conducted using historical Bitcoin data from 2013 to 2023, and the models were evaluated using standard regression metrics, including Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Mean Squared Error (MSE), and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE). The experimental results show that the GRU model achieved lower prediction errors (RMSE = 3434 USD) than the LSTM model (RMSE = 8238 USD), indicating that the GRU provides better forecasting performance for Bitcoin price prediction. The findings demonstrate that both deep learning models can effectively capture nonlinear patterns in cryptocurrency data, while GRU offers improved predictive accuracy due to its simpler architecture and efficient training. These results provide valuable insights for investors and traders seeking reliable cryptocurrency forecasting tools. Furthermore, future studies may incorporate additional explanatory variables, such as social media sentiment, macroeconomic indicators, and blockchain activity, to further improve predictive performance.

title: Unified Joint Optimization of Noise Suppression and Feature Selection for Robust Intelligent Job Matching on Websites
authors: Li Ning
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: To address the cross-source heterogeneous structure and multi-layer semantic noise issues in job descriptions and user resumes on online recruitment platforms, this study proposes a Unified Joint Optimization (UJO) framework. This method is based on a dual-tower semantic representation structure, introducing hierarchical encoding mechanisms in both the job tower and the user tower, including word-level HR-BERT encoding, sentence-level attention-weighted aggregation, and paragraph-level multi-head self-attention modeling. During the representation learning stage, an equivalent semantic noise energy function is constructed, jointly characterizing text noise intensity through semantic entropy, redundancy coefficients, and token diversity. Simultaneously, differential symmetry constraints are used to weaken common-mode interference. Furthermore, a cross-source entity alignment strategy is designed, fusing normalized edit distance and vector cosine similarity. This study constructs a weighted matching function and introduces a graph convolutional propagation mechanism on the three-layer heterogeneous graph of "job-skill-user" to achieve structural information fusion. To avoid redundant features introduced by multi-layer propagation, a learnable sparse gating module is further constructed. This module also incorporates L1 regularization for structural compression. In the matching stage, bilinear interaction modeling and binary cross-entropy loss are used for point-level optimization. At the same time, a sorting loss based on NDCG is introduced to implement list-level constraints. The experiment constructed three test sets with different noise intensities based on 48,732 job postings, 126,415 resumes, and 1,250,736 labeled samples. Results show that on the Clean dataset, the UJO model achieves an AUC of 0.957, NDCG@10 reaches 0.897, and MAP reaches 0.886. Under high noise (Noise-H) conditions, the AUC is 0.934, only 2.3% lower than Clean. Noise energy validation experiments show that the average equivalent noise energy for Clean, Noise-L, and Noise-H data are 0.87, 1.94, and 3.26, respectively. After introducing the noise suppression module, the variance of the representation vector decreased from 1.482 to 0.963. Sparse gating analysis shows that approximately 48% dimensionality compression can be achieved within the λ = 0.003– 0.005 range while maintaining an AUC above 0.954. Graph ablation experiments showed that removing graph convolutions reduced NDCG@10 by 2.1% and Recall@20 by 2.8%. Joint optimization of the ranking layer improved NDCG@10 from 0.864 to 0.897 and Top-3 hit rate from 0.732 to 0.781.

title: Adaptive PWM Control using Machine Learning and RISC-V
authors: Abhinav Chalil, Vivek Ashok Nair, Rajesh Kannan Megalingam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This paper presents the design and virtual implementation of an Adaptive Pulse Width Modulation - PWM control system using Machine Learning and RISC-V architecture. Instead of actual physical hardware, the system is simulated using QEMU, BRISC-V, and EDA Playground. A machine learning Linear Regression model predicts the PWM duty cycle from parameters such as temperature,humidity and speed. The trained model runs on a RISC-V emulator which updates the duty cycles dynamically, while PWM In Verilog, waveform verification is done using GTKWave. Results confirm that adaptive PWM can be achieved entirely. accuracy of results and low latency in software, providing a cost-effective approach for intelligent control in embedded and IoT applications.

title: AI-based Noise Cancellation and Speech Enhancement for Early Parkinson’s Disease Detection
authors: Kavitha K, Pyreddy Bhagirath Reddy, Kathri Gujarathi Arun, Putluru Sashi Vardhan Reddy, Pavuluru Muni Venkata Joshith
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Non-invasive diagnosis of Parkinson's disease (PD) has been proposed as one of the most promising methods of conducting speech analysis to detect the disease early. nevertheless, acoustic heterogeneity in reality and external noise has a strong negative impact on classification effects. The paper is an attempt at developing a strong noise-resistant system incorporating signal improvement, acoustic feature derivation, and machine learning in order to enhance PD detection. A spectral subtraction and Wiener filtering with spectral enhancement using short time fourier transform (STFT) to minimize additive noise and preserve pathological speech characteristics are proposed. Using the refined cues, Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCCs) and features of phonatory significance to a clinician like jitter, shimmer, fundamental frequency, and harmonic to noise ratio are derived and used to categorize a support vector machine (SVM). The literature reviews two benchmark datasets (UCI Parkinson and PC-GITA corpus) on the conditions of controlled noise. Experimental findings show that the enhancement strategy suggested is more effective than noisy speech inputs in enhancing classification strength and has an average accuracy of 96.4, sensitivity, specificity, and area under the ROC curve. The results have shown that noise suppression is a potent tool in increasing the discriminative ability of acoustic biomarkers to detect PD. This paper shows the relevance of speech improvement methods to automated diagnostics pipelines and demonstrates that the noise-conscious preprocessing can enhance reliability in the field of practical screening. The suggested framework provides a computationally effective and interpretable approach to creating user-friendly, speech-based, and initial detection equipment of Parkinson disease.

title: SmartCode AI: A Hybrid Static Analysis and Ensemble Transformer Framework for Secure Code Review and Automated Remediation
authors: N. Uma Maheswari, A.K. Prabhakaran, R. Raja Vignesh, T. Ram Prakash, S. Sanju
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Application security vulnerabilities continue to dominate the threat landscape, contributing to nearly seventy percent of reported security breaches. The growing volume of software applications, combined with increasing architectural complexity, has significantly expanded the attack surface. Traditional Static Application Security Testing (SAST) tools remain widely adopted; however, existing approaches suffer from critical limitations. High false positive rates, often ranging between sixty and eighty percent, create alert fatigue and reduce developer trust. Pattern-based detection mechanisms lack contextual understanding of business logic, leading to misclassification of safe code as vulnerable. The absence of automated remediation further compounds the issue, as developers must manually interpret findings and design secure fixes. This manual effort increases remediation time, delays deployment cycles, and introduces inconsistency in patch quality. Limited scalability and integration flexibility also hinder continuous security monitoring across large repositories. The proposed SmartCode AI methodology addresses these challenges through a hybrid architecture integrating rule-based static analysis with an ensemble of transformer-based deep learning models. Initial candidate detection ensures broad vulnerability coverage, while contextual verification performed by multiple models significantly reduces false positives. Consensus-driven validation enhances reliability and minimizes inconsistent predictions. Automated patch generation further transforms vulnerability management from detection-only to detection-and-repair. Multi-layer validation ensures syntactic correctness, security compliance, and functional preservation before presenting fixes. A scalable microservices deployment model supports concurrent analysis of large repositories, reducing remediation time from days to hours. Benchmark evaluations demonstrate substantial improvement in precision, recall, F1-score, and patch success rate, validating the effectiveness of the integrated framework.

title: High speed Semiconductor based control circuit for Battery Equalization used in Electric vehicle Battery management system
authors: Femy P. H., Jayakumar J.
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: This paper details the development of a high speed control circuit based on semiconductors for active battery equalization in electric vehicle (EV) battery management systems. The proposed method combines an inductor-based battery equalization topology with a VLSI control circuit for real-time state-of-charge (SoC) monitoring and fast switching. The control mechanism considers SoC deviation as the basic parameter for equalization decision criteria. It applies high-frequency PWM technique to ensure controlled inductor current and efficient energy transfer between cells. The proposed method gives emphasis on low switching losses, reduced device stress, and scalability for large battery packs. The VLSI controller provides rapid equalization response compared to conventional microcontroller-based systems. Simulation results show higher balancing speed, better current regulation, and high equalization efficiency under varying load conditions. The proposed system provides an energy-efficient, and scalable solution for next-generation integrated EV battery management systems.

title: Analysis of Electromagnetic Field and Thermal Coupling in Cable Faults
authors: Guoqiang Gao, Qianran Zhao, Xin Zhao, Jiaojiao Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: New energy distributed power generation through the substation after boosting for power transmission, high-voltage power cables as the key carrier of the transmission link, and different faults will have a great impact on the steady state of the transmission line. Defects in faulty cables destroy the internal physical structure, resulting in increased magnetic leakage, insulation failure to ground, instability of the electric field distribution, causing local temperature rise and tip discharge, and it is very important for cable fault diagnosis to use the temperature change and magnetic field change in the faulty section as the physical quantities to be monitored. Therefore, this paper investigates the electromagnetic-thermal coupled field changes of $\mathbf{3 5 k V}$ MV cables under fault conditions, considering the effects of cable fault types and laying methods on the results, respectively. A three-core cable model is established by the finite element method, and several types of faults, such as short circuits, open circuits, and partial discharges, are analyzed. The measurements provide data on temperature, magnetic, and electric field variations associated with faults and validate the effectiveness of the numerical method based on the coupled-field approach and internal and surface boundary conditions. The method provides highprecision diagnostics and powerful visualization capabilities. The results show that the temperature and electromagnetic fields of similar cables behave differently under different fault conditions, while the laying method has a significant impact on the service life of the cable.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Network Intrusion Detection Using TabTransformer and CatBoost
authors: Soujanya Thirunagari, P.V.N. Rajeswari, Viyyapu Pushpa, Uma Maheswari Rayudu, G.Jose Moses, Naladi Ram Babu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The rapid growth of networked systems and internet- connected devices has significantly increased the volume and complexity of cyberattacks targeting modern infrastructures. Traditional signature-based intrusion detection systems (IDS) are insufficient against evolving threats such as DDoS, PortScan, Web Attacks, and infiltration attempts, as they rely on predefined rules and struggle to generalize to unseen patterns. Machine learning-based IDS solutions have emerged as a scalable alternative, yet their performance is limited by the heterogeneous nature of network traffic, high-dimensional tabular features, and the imbalance among attack categories. To provide a more reliable IDS, this study evaluates a wide spectrum of classical ML classifiers and a deep-learning baseline model using the CIC-IDS2017 dataset. A hybrid deep tabular learning framework is then introduced, combining TabTransformer for categorical feature representation with CatBoost for robust gradient-boosted classification. Experimental analysis shows that baseline ML models achieve strong foundational accuracy, while the ANN model further improves performance. The proposed hybrid model delivers the highest accuracy, F1-score, and attack-specific detection rates, demonstrating its effectiveness in learning complex tabular feature interactions and improving generalization across diverse intrusion categories. These findings indicate that transformer-driven tabular embeddings combined with boosted decision trees provide a powerful and scalable approach for modern intrusion detection.

title: From Metric-Driven Assessment to Ethics-Embedded Evaluation: A Computer-Aided AHP Framework for Restorative Design in Traditional Craft Villages

title: Edge Feature Enhancement and Multi-scale Convolutional Kernel Selection Aggregation for Fine-grained Ship Detection
authors: Yaxiong Chen, Shuo Gao, Qi Wu, Zhong Sai, GuiRong Feng
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: In recent years, with the continuous advancement of high-resolution remote sensing imaging technology, the capability of perceiving maritime ship targets under complex sea conditions has been significantly improved. This holds substantial theoretical value and practical significance for enhancing maritime traffic situational awareness, strengthening the supervision of fishery activities, and safeguarding the marine environment as well as maritime security. However, due to the single imaging perspective of remote sensing images, fine-grained ship detection still faces challenges. such as large intra-class scale variations caused by varying imaging heights and ambiguous inter-class boundaries for visually similar ship types.To address this issue, we propose the Edge-Aware Feature Enhancement Module (EFEM) to strengthen the fusion of edge details and spatial contextual information of ships in remote sensing images, and design the Multi-Scale Kernel Aggregation Module (MSKAM) to improve the model’s ability to perceive ships of varying sizes. These modules effectively solve the problem of extracting discriminative features of ships. Finally, experiments conducted on the third hierarchy of the ShipRSImageNet dataset and the FAIR1M dataset demonstrate that, compared with the benchmark model, the proposed fine-grained ship detection model achieves improvements of 1.87 % and 1.72 % in the mAP50 metric, respectively. Moreover, comparative validations on the two datasets further confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method in enhancing the accuracy of fine-grained ship detection.
