title: Semantic-Aware 5G Communication Framework with Task-Oriented Encoding for Efficient Data Transmission
authors: Rajidi Sahithi, Kandula Sri Nithya, Mudapaka Sai Usha Sri, Rampuram Srija, Yadavalli Lakshmi Tirupatamma
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Semantic intelligence communication has become a bright paradigm in the efficient and intelligent 5G network of the next generation. In this paper, a 5G communication framework is introduced, which is semantically aware that is using task-oriented encoding, with decisions regarding transmission being informed by the significance of information to the target task instead of bit-level accuracy. The suggested framework combines the edge intelligence with profound semantic encoders and decoders to discover compact, task-relevant representations in the dynamic network settings. An evaluation mechanism of semantic importance is presented that helps to prioritize the important information to make a selective transmission and adaptive use of the resources. Simulation outcomes prove the suggested method to be a much better bandwidth user with a high level of task success and robustness in comparison to traditional and compressed 5G policies, especially during low and moderate SNR. The results of the study show the promise of semantic-aware, task-oriented communication as a useful approach to supporting intelligent, low-latency, and resource-efficient 5G and future wireless networks.

title: Vision Transformer based Defect Detection System for Tyre Mould Surfaces
authors: H. Mohammed Thaha, S. Vishnu Priya, K. Mouliswaran, D. Gayathri, B. Ramasubramanian, S. Mirdula
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The surface condition of tyre molds considerably affects the wear, quality and safety of produced tyres. In the production, moulds suffer thermal stress, mechanical load and chemical corrosion, to a variety of defects such as scratches, pits, micro crack and local wear. These blemishes may carry over into tyre treads, resulting in irregular tread wear and early failure. Traditional inspection is based on visual check by human eyes, which is subjective and unstable, while classical image-processing techniques are sensitive to light changing and textures. Through the use of patch embedding and multi-head self-attention, the model is able to establish global contextual connectivity in the mould surface for recognition of fine localised defects. The experiment results show that the proposed method outperforms other works, obtaining 92.56% accuracy, 98.85% precision, 92.50% recall and 95.57 F1-score. A balanced accuracy over 92.72% and MCC above 0.7403, indicate the classification is reliable in condition of class imbalance. Experimental evaluation demonstrates obvious superiority over traditional blob detection approaches. The experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of transformer based global feature learning in intelligent tyre mould surface inspection.

title: Analysis of RF Performance in Negative Capacitance FETs for Low-Power Circuits
authors: Pearly Princess. J, D. S. Shylu Sam, Harish Kathiravan M, Blessy Evangeline. S
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: One potential architecture of a device that could achieve very low-power operation at levels that are beyond the conventional limits of standard MOSFETs is the negative capacitance field-effect transistor (NCFET). This paper discusses a three dimensional vertical silicon negative capacitance FET (Si-NCFET) through the use of Sentaurus TCAD simulations in detail, both in terms of DC and radio-frequency (RF) operation. The proposed dual-gate topology allows strong electrostatic control of the channel, which effectively suppresses short-channel effects and increases current drivability. A detailed parametric analysis is conducted to obtain the best device performance in terms of the outer gate length and the thickness of the gate oxide. Transfer characteristics, cutoff frequency, transconductance as well as voltages capitance behavior have been studied extensively as well as the key important electrical and RF metrics. The optimized Si-NCFET has a big potential in the low-power and high-frequency circuit application with a steep subthreshold swing 38 mV/dec, a high Ion/Ioff ratio, improved cutoff frequency, and transconductance. These results prove the fact that vertical Si-NCFETs can be adapted to the next-generation energy-efficient RF and mixed-signal systems.

title: Decentralized AI Model Marketplace: A Blockchain-Powered Platform for Secure Model Selling, Ownership, and Provenance
authors: Vishal Subhash Chavan, Suyash Sanjay Kotkar, Prajakta Shamrao Waghmode, Prathamesh Satyavan Ligade, Jambukeshwar Pujari, Sheetal Gaikwad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The widespread adoption of artificial intelligence and machine learning (AI/ML) has significantly increased the need for reliable and high-performance models. However, the absence of secure, transparent, and equitable platforms for distributing and monetizing these models presents a critical bottleneck. This paper introduces a blockchain-based decentralized marketplace tailored for AI/ML model exchange, aiming to resolve challenges related to model ownership, licensing, and royalty automation. The proposed platform empowers developers to publish their models with verifiable ownership, leveraging smart contracts to automate licensing and ensure fair revenue distribution. Buyers can confidently acquire models with validated authenticity, supported by secure file-type verification methods that avoid disclosing sensitive content. The blockchain’s immutable ledger guarantees full traceability of transactions, mitigating risks such as intellectual property misuse and unauthorized redistribution. Moreover, integrated analytics modules offer actionable insights on model usage patterns and demand trends, enabling data-driven decision-making for both creators and consumers. By combining blockchain integrity, AI capabilities, and market intelligence, this work proposes a transformative solution for establishing a trustworthy, efficient, and incentivized ecosystem for AI/ML model sharing.

title: A Transfer Learning-based Deep Vision Framework for Multi-Class Detection of Tea Leaf Diseases in Precision Agriculture
authors: Dolores L. Montesines, May M. Garcia, Reggie Campomanes, Alvin Sarraga Alon
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Early and accurate detection of tea leaf diseases is essential for maintaining crop productivity and ensuring sustainable agricultural management. This study presents a transfer learning–based deep vision framework for multi-class detection of tea leaf diseases using an object detection approach. A curated dataset comprising 5,180 annotated images across six classes—Algal Leaf Spot, Brown Blight, Gray Blight, Helopeltis, Red Leaf Spot, and Healthy leaves—was utilized, with images captured under diverse illumination conditions and backgrounds to enhance robustness. The model demonstrates stable training convergence and strong detection capability across standard evaluation metrics. Quantitative results show that the proposed framework achieves a precision of 0.986 and recall of 0.988, indicating effective suppression of false positives while maintaining high detection sensitivity. The model further attains a mAP@50 of 0.988 and mAP@50–95 of 0.914, reflecting reliable object classification and accurate spatial localization under both moderate and stringent intersection-over-union thresholds. Training dynamics analysis reveals stable optimization behavior, with classification loss converging early while localization-related losses continue refining bounding box precision throughout later epochs. Qualitative inference on unseen samples confirms the model’s ability to accurately localize and distinguish multiple tea leaf disease categories with minimal misclassification. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed framework, highlighting its potential for practical deployment in automated tea leaf disease monitoring systems for precision agriculture.

title: IoT-Enabled Biometric Access Control System for Secure Smart Classrooms
authors: Karen Joy D. Cadiz, Norman Avery F. Santos, Alwyn James G. Meora
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: This paper describes the design, development, and implementation of a classroom automation system using the Internet of Things technology that is expected to improve the security of access and the unnecessary usage of energy in laboratory classrooms. The automation system uses the DOST-IoTPOD platform, which enables fingerprint authentication access, motion-controlled lighting through a passive infrared sensor, relay-controlled loading, and decision-making through a microcontroller. This system is designed to be automatically initiated depending on the occupancy of the room and the authorized entrance. A developmental methodology was used in the research investigation, wherein the performance of the automation system using DOST-IoTPOD was determined by the comparison between the automatic system designed using the platform and the present manual system. In the data analysis, ANOVA, Pearson Correlation Coefficient, Mean Absolute Error, and Mean Absolute Percentage Error were used in the statistical test, which determines the system's response, accuracy, and stability regarding the occupancy patterns observed. Results indicate that the system developed improves the efficiency of the operation by lowering the unnecessary usage of lighting, securing access, and maintaining stability in the system response. From these results, it becomes obvious that the DOST-IoTPOD automation solution is a trusted and scalable solution for a smart-classroom setting and thus a solid ground for further innovations in intelligent energy and building automation.

title: Quantum-Inspired approaches to Multi-robot Scheduling and Control using QAOA and VQE
authors: Ramaraj S, Jaiganesh M, Mercy S, Bodla. Ashwin Kumar, Senthur Balaji K, Lathika M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Quantum computation injection into robot automation opens up new avenues for boosting multi-robot complicated task scheduling and real-time adaptive control. Conventional optimization methods barely keep up with the challenges of scalability and efficiency in dynamic environments, thus becoming one of the bottlenecks for successful robotic coordination. This paper puts forward two leading quantum algorithms, Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) and Variational Quantum Eigen solver (VQE), as a remedy to these shortcomings. Through the use of quantum parallelism and hybrid quantum-classical architectures, the intended solution is to elevate task assignment effectiveness and interactive response capacity in multi-robotic systems. Among other things, the proposed work aims at setting up a quantum inspired optimization model to schedule a task, an adaptive and real-time decision-support control framework, and the experimental demonstration of the algorithm's capability to handle complexity and high volume in robotic environments. Computer-based simulations alongside actual experiments, as a matter of fact, will be able to outperform regular optimizations, hence proving the transformative potentials of quantum algorithms on robotic automation.

title: Transfer Learning–based Object Detection for Joint Head and Tail Localization in Precision Pig Farming
authors: Priscilla S. Bator, Mary Joy D. Vinas, Mhona Liza N. Paragas, Alvin Sarraga Alon
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Accurate localization of fine-grained anatomical regions is essential for vision-based livestock monitoring systems in precision pig farming. This study proposes a transfer learning–based object detection framework for the joint localization of pig head and tail regions from RGB images collected in commercial farm environments. The approach adapts a pretrained deep detection model to simultaneously identify head and tail regions within a unified architecture, enabling structured anatomical representation while maintaining computational efficiency. The dataset consists of 46,077 annotated images with balanced head and tail class distributions, augmented to enhance robustness under occlusion, varying illumination, and high animal density. Experimental results demonstrate stable convergence between training and validation phases and strong generalization capability. The model achieves a peak mAP@50 of 0.990 and mAP@50–95 of 0.775, with corresponding precision and recall values of 0.983 and 0.978, respectively. Confidence–F1 analysis indicates reliable confidence calibration, with an optimal F1-score of 0.97 obtained at a moderate confidence threshold. Qualitative evaluations further confirm accurate and anatomically consistent localization across diverse and challenging farm scenes.

title: A Tri-Coupled Transformer-Based Ku-Band Low-Noise Amplifier With 1.9-dB NF and 30-dB Gain
authors: Tuanjie Ding, Tao Hou, Binbin Lv, Hao Zheng, Fang Lu
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a tri-coupled transformer (XFMR)-based low-noise amplifier (LNA) implemented in a 55-nm CMOS process, which achieves a low noise figure (NF), high gain, and high output power for Ku-band satellite communication. The tri-coupled XFMR is implemented using the top two thick metal layers, and a strong coupling coefficient is attained through same-layer metal coupling, thereby enabling effective noise cancellation and significant Gm-boosting. Measured results show that the proposed LNA achieves a peak gain of 30 dB and an ultralow noise of 1.9–2.6 dB from 10.6 to 13.6 GHz within a 1-dB bandwidth.

title: Machine Learning-Assisted Circuit-Network-Field Co-Optimization for Metal-Bezel Smartphone Antenna Design
authors: Wenjian Gong, Qi Wu, Weiqi Chen, Libin Sun, Hanyang Wang, Haiming Wang, Wei Hong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: To meet advanced performance requirements, modern smartphone antennas frequently adopt circuit-network-field (CNF) co-design, which introduces challenges such as strong variable coupling, high sensitivity, and vast search spaces. Traditional machine learning-assisted optimization (MLAO) methods, typically focused on geometric structures, exhibit limited capability in addressing these complexities. This paper proposes a machine learning-assisted circuit-network-field co-optimization (MLA-CNFCO) method incorporating microwave network analysis. First, logarithmic sampling is applied to circuit variables to reshape the sample space, alleviating issues related to nonlinear impedance and high sensitivity. A proportion-modeling approach is then introduced to enhance the prediction accuracy of radiation pattern surrogate models. To accelerate the process, a microwave network analytical solution (MNAS) is employed to calculate reflection coefficients directly, enabling the rapid identification of superior initial solutions. These coefficients are integrated as constraints, significantly narrowing the search space. Finally, the co-design problem is effectively decoupled by embedding microwave network-based circuit optimization within potential-based geometry optimization. The effectiveness of the MLA-CNFCO framework is validated through the design, fabrication, and measurement of a high-performance smartphone antenna.

title: Realistic Human Image Animation with Dynamic Camera Effects
authors: Minseok Kang, Jihyun Kim, ChangHee Yang, Kyeongbo Kong, Suk-Ju Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Human image animation transfers a sequence of pose guidance to a character in an image to generate a dynamic video. In this paper, we enhance the realism of human image animation by introducing viewpoint-conditioned synthesis under moderate camera motion, enabling dynamic camera effects in addition to articuated human motion. However, simply employing off-the-shelf human image animation or view synthesis models in a cascaded manner leads to artifacts, because body motion and viewpoint transformation are handled independently wwithout preserving consistent depth ordering and occlusion. As such, we propose a method which jointly learns to perform both tasks within a shared 3D representation. Specifically, our framework obtains an inpainted background image and isolates the human figure from animated frames. Most importantly, we propose a background depth reconstruction module (BDRM), composed of a depth inpainter and a depth scaler, to obtain an aligned background depth. With the depth information, we convert the background and the foreground into 3D point clouds which are then rendered from target views. For the joint training, we suggest a T-shaped training method which resolves the time-consuming and costly process of collecting animated videos for each different view. In this paper, we prove the effectiveness of our background depth reconstruction module in preventing occlusions, and the superiority of our method over cascade approaches quantitatively and qualitatively. Our Project Page is available at https://excellent-wizard-cf6.notion. site/ieee-tcsvt-dynamic-camera-human-image-animation.

title: Text-Visible/Infrared Person Retrieval: Attribute-Guided Feature Decoupling and Collaborative Alignment and A Unified Benchmark
authors: Chenglong Li, Ziheng Xu, Yifei Deng, Aihua Zheng, Jin Tang
abstract: research on text-to-image person retrieval primarily focuses on visible images, which are not suitable under low-light scenarios. Infrared imaging becomes necessary in many visual systems, and matching text with both visible and infrared images is required. However, visible and infrared images are heterogeneous with different visual characteristics, so matching text with them in a unified framework is very challenging. In this work, we design a new task called Text-Visible/Infrared person retrieval and contribute a novel approach and a unified benchmark to promote the research and development of this field. On one hand, we propose a novel Attribute-guided feature decoupling and Collaborative Alignment Network (ACANet) that pursues accurate alignment from the text modality to both visible and infrared modalities in a unified framework according to the texture and color attribute information of text descriptions. In particular, we decouple the color features of visible images supervised by the text labels and integrate them into the infrared features to eliminate the impact of the absence of color information in infrared images during cross-modal collaborative alignment. Moreover, we also decouple the texture information from visible images supervised by the text labels and perform the collaborative alignment of texture and infrared features with a fusion agent. In addition, we extend conventional masked language modeling to a cross-modal paradigm to help ACANet learn uniform fine-grained alignment in multiple image modalities. On the other hand, we contribute a unified high-quality MM01LLCM-Text dataset, which provides person images in both visible and infrared modalities paired with fine-grained text descriptions. Experimental results show that the proposed ACANet outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on MM01LLCM-Text dataset.

title: Intelligence, Learning, and Artificial Life Models: Evolution and Systematic Review
authors: Dante Vargas, David Mauricio, Luis Rivera
abstract: Artificial Life (ALife) seeks to understand and recreate the fundamental processes of living systems using synthetic models capable of robust, adaptive, and autonomous behavior. Despite significant advances, the field still lacks an integrated perspective that jointly examines modeling, intelligence, and learning, three key aspects for the study of ALife. This study presents a systematic review of scientific literature indexed in Scopus, Web of Science, and the ACM Digital Library, complemented by secondary sources, following the methodology proposed by Kitchenham and Charters. A total of 694 records were initially identified, from which 52 studies were selected through a rigorous multi-stage filtering process. The review identifies 45 ALife models classified according to Heudin’s typology into Soft ALife (37), Hard ALife (7), and Wet ALife (1), highlighting the predominance of computational approaches. With respect to intelligence, 62 instances were identified across 44 articles, corresponding to limited-memory, cognitive, self-organizing, emergent, and evolutionary intelligence, revealing a growing tendency toward hybrid multi-intelligence architectures. In terms of learning, eight learning paradigms were identified, including supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement, self-organized, continuous, evolutionary, probabilistic, and static learning, corresponding to a total of 77 algorithms. Overall, the findings indicate that ALife models are becoming increasingly adaptive and cognitively sophisticated through the integration of diverse learning mechanisms and forms of artificial intelligence. This review constitutes the first systematic effort to jointly examine model typologies, intelligence categories, and learning mechanisms in ALife, offering an integrative and evolutionary perspective on the progressive enhancement of adaptive and cognitive capabilities in artificial living systems.

title: SPIM RSMA: Prototyping and Experimental Validation of its Superiority Over Traditional RSMA
authors: Sagnik Bhattacharyya, Shiv Kumar, Sam Darshi, Keshav Singh
abstract: In this letter, we develop and implement a transceiver prototype for split-packet interference management in an uplink rate-splitting multiple access (SPIM RSMA) framework using a software-defined radio (SDR) platform. The primary objective is to experimentally validate the performance superiority of the SPIM RSMA framework over conventional uplink RSMA and non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) schemes. Experimental results demonstrate that SPIM RSMA achieves significantly improved bit error rate (BER) and throughput performance compared to traditional RSMA and NOMA. These gains are primarily attributed to the effective mitigation of split packet interference (SPI), which preserves the inherent advantages of rate splitting. Furthermore, the experimental BER results are corroborated through comparisons with simulation-based results, thus validating the proposed transceiver architecture.

title: High-Current Regulation System Based on an Inductance with Local Saturations: Study of Magnetic Coupling Between the Power Circuit and the Command Coils
authors: Jilali Imaankaf, Cristian Demian, Raphael Romary, Daniel Roger, Mehdi Zmirli, Philippe Bernard
abstract: High-current regulation systems designed for metallurgy and glass industries can use a tunable inductance controlled by a DC source. The principle of such a tunable inductance is based on the natural nonlinearity of the magnetic core. This device is placed in series with the primary winding of a transformer. It provides fast electrical adjustments. In such structures a magnetic coupling naturally appears between the power circuit and the command coils. This paper highlights this coupling through experimental and numerical results and analyzes how it depends on electrical operating conditions of the system.

title: High-PAE Gold-Free AlGaN/GaN HEMT With Al–Si Alloy Gate Fabrication Process for 5G Applications
authors: Long Chen, Hao Lu, Longge Deng, Yuechao Du, Mengqi Liu, Ling Yang, Bin Hou, Meng Zhang, Mei Wu, Xiaohua Ma, Yue Hao
abstract: In this article, we present gold-free AlGaN/GaN high electron mobility transistors (HEMTs) fabricated on SiC substrates using an Al–Si alloy gate fabrication process. The proposed devices incorporate a two-step deposited (TSD) Ti/Al/Ti/TiN metal stack with high-dose ion-implanted ohmic regions and implement an ohmic trench etching technique. This design achieves an ultralow ohmic contact resistance (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${R}_{\text {C}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.18~\Omega~\cdot $</tex-math> </inline-formula> mm and a highly uniform surface morphology with a root-mean-squared (rms) roughness of 2.33 nm. In addition, the gate electrode employs a Ti/AlSi metal stack that is fully compatible with Si-based CMOS technology. Owing to the superior ohmic contact properties, the fabricated HEMTs exhibit a peak transconductance (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${g}_{\text {m}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 302.2mS/mm and a saturation drain current (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\text {D, {max}}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 1.26 A/mm. Small-signal radio frequency (RF) measurements yield a cutoff frequency (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${f}_{\text {T}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 23.9 GHz and a maximum oscillation frequency (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${f}_{\max }\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 38.9 GHz. Furthermore, the TSD HEMT demonstrates an output power density (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${P}_{\text {sat}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 3.9 W/mm and a peak power-added efficiency (PAE) of 63.1% at 3.6GHz. These findings highlight the potential of this approach in advancing high-performance and low-cost Au-free GaN HEMTs for sub-6 GHz RF applications.

title: Time-Series Disturbance Observer-Based Multi-Rate Predictive Control of High-Switching-Frequency Power Converters
authors: Rongkui Mei, Dehong Zhou, Xin Liu, Jianxiao Zou, Zewei Shen
abstract: Wide-bandgap devices enable high-frequency power converters but pose significant computational challenges for digital control systems. Multi-rate model predictive control (MR-MPC) tackles this challenge by decoupling sampling and switching operations, but its multi-step prediction mechanism leads to prediction error propagation within sampling intervals. However, conventional disturbance observers can only perform disturbance rejection at sampling instants, failing to provide suppression for the multiple time-series control inputs within each sampling interval. To this end, a time-series disturbance observer-based MR-MPC (TDMR-MPC) is proposed to enhance robustness against parameter mismatches. The proposed scheme utilizes a generalized proportional-integral observer to estimate time-series disturbances and embeds them into the predictive model for real-time suppression at each control point. Additionally, Lagrangian interpolation is proposed to deal with the dimensional mismatch between reference trajectories and control inputs. The stability of the system is rigorously proven, and experimental results on a silicon-carbide-based motor drive system validate the effectiveness of the scheme.

title: A High-Isolation Self-Packaged Filter Array Using a 3D Hybrid Integrated Shielding Cavity
authors: Shaohui Liu, Xiaozhi Liu, Yongping Xiao, Yaya Liang, Pingan Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: A self-packaged and 3D hybrid integrated shielding cavity filter array that achieves > 50 dB adjacent-channel isolation in an ultra-compact multilayer PCB footprint (39.60 mm × 39.60 mm × 1.82 mm) is presented, eliminating the need for bulky external metal shielding cavities. The design integrates four X/Ku-band bandpass filters (centered at 7.75 GHz, 8.75 GHz, 9.75 GHz, and 10.75 GHz) using a 3D hybrid isolation strategy that synergistically combines orthogonal polarization, vertical layer segregation, a gold-ribbon shielding fence, and a central metallic island for cavity-mode suppression. Measured results demonstrate that the in-band insertion loss is below 7.0 dB, the stopband rejection exceeds 40 dBc across all channels, and the inter-channel isolation is better than 50 dB over 7.5-8 GHz, 8.5-9 GHz, 9.5-10 GHz, and 10.5-11 GHz ranges. Full-wave simulations corroborate the measurements, validating the efficacy of the proposed architecture. This approach offers a scalable, low-cost, and highly integrable solution for dense microwave front-ends in next-generation phased-array radars and software-defined radio systems.

title: Biomedical Knowledge Graph Alignment with GPT-Augmented Similarity Feature Construction via Tree-based Particle Swarm Optimization and Adaptive Fitness Optimization
authors: Xingsi Xue, Xingwang Li, Himanshu Dhumras
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Accurate biomedical Knowledge Graph (KG) alignment is critical for integrating heterogeneous biomedical information and enabling reliable downstream applications, such as clinical decision support, biomedical search, and personalized healthcare. However, variations in terminologies, KG structures, and semantic granularity across biomedical sources introduce substantial heterogeneity, making the construction of high-quality Similarity Features (SFs) a major challenge. Although the Generative Pre-trained Transformer (GPT) has shown strong potential in capturing nuanced biomedical semantics, each GPT-augmented SF typically reflects only one semantic perspective, and combining multiple SFs in a meaningful manner remains non-trivial because of the expanded symbolic search space and potential feature conflicts. To address these challenges, we propose a novel Tree-based Particle Swarm Optimization with Adaptive Fitness Optimization (T-PSO-AFO) framework for GPT-augmented SF construction. First, a GPT-based semantic feature construction method is introduced to extract expressive and context-aware SFs that better capture biomedical entity equivalence. Then, a tree-based symbolic representation within a PSO framework is developed to explore diverse and complex SF combinations more effectively. Finally, an adaptive fitness landscape optimization mechanism is proposed to dynamically reshape the symbolic search space during evolution, improving the convergence stability and alignment performance. Experiments on OAEI's LargeBio and Disease and Phenotype datasets demonstrate that T-PSO-AFO significantly outperforms state-of-the-art biomedical entity matching approaches, validating its robustness, effectiveness, and scalability in aligning heterogeneous biomedical KGs.

title: Noise-Induced Geometric Deformation of PCA Representations in Magnetically Split Mössbauer Spectra
authors: Sonia Sharmin, Chiharu Mitsumata, Eiji Kita, Hideto Yanagihara
abstract: Reliable extraction of hyperfine parameters from Mössbauer spectra becomes challenging when measurements are affected by significant noise. Under such conditions, nonlinear least-squares (NLLS) fitting may produce apparently acceptable spectral fits while the estimated physical parameters deviate substantially from their true values. In this work, we investigate the effect of measurement noise on the geometric structure of Mössbauer spectral data using principal component analysis (PCA). A large synthetic ensemble of magnetically split <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"/>57 Fe sextet spectra is generated and analyzed using a fixed-basis PCA representation trained on clean spectra. Measurement noise is introduced over a controlled signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) range, and the resulting geometric distortion of the PCA representation is quantified through global subspace drift and local score-space distortion metrics. The results show that increasing noise induces systematic global subspace drift and local score-space dispersion in the PCA representation of the spectral manifold, and that these geometric changes reveal the onset of parameter instability earlier than conventional residual-based fitting diagnostics. These findings demonstrate that PCA-based geometric analysis provides a useful complementary tool for assessing the reliability of Mössbauer parameter extraction under noisy measurement conditions.

title: Residual Prototypical Contrastive Self-Supervised Learning for UAV Shadow Removal
authors: Jian Dong, Zhi He, Chengle Zhou, Li Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) imagery shadow removal is a crucial process that aims to restore the surface information of key regions obscured by shadows in optical imagery. State-of-the-art UAV shadow removal methods are typically built on either supervised or unsupervised paradigms. However, these methods fundamentally rely on strong priors, such as paired shadow-free references or cycle-consistency constraints. To address these limitations, we propose a novel residual prototypical contrastive learning framework, termed ConShadow, for self-supervised UAV shadow removal. Our newly developed approach first designs a Retinex-based shadow synthesis augmentation (RSSA) strategy, which generates diverse shadowing variations while preserving scene-identity consistency to facilitate ideal representation learning. Subsequently, a residual prototypical contrastive self-supervised learning framework is proposed, which is enhanced by cooperative-adversarial prototypes training, hard negative mining, and false negative mitigation. This paradigm effectively guides the model in learning discriminative correction patterns while preserving the integrity of non-shadowed regions. We conduct extensive experiments on two benchmark UAV shadow removal datasets and an aerial remote sensing shadow removal dataset. Our results demonstrate that the proposed ConShadow achieves competitive accuracy and superior visual quality compared to state-of-the-art supervised schemes, while requiring significantly lower GPU memory than unsupervised counterparts.

title: Multi-Charging Station Pricing Strategy for Real-Time Congestion Management in Coupled Traffic-Power Networks
authors: Gaojunjie Li, Siqi Bu, Edward Chung, Fuzhang Wu, Ruoheng Wang, Yang Zhang
abstract: In high-density cities, coordinating charging station (CS) real-time pricing to mitigate traffic congestion is considered a promising approach. However, the increasing complexity of transportation systems and multi-player dynamic interactions limit the effectiveness of existing pricing strategies in real-time congestion management. To address these, a real-time coordinated pricing framework for multiple CSs in coupled traffic-power networks based on Simulation of Urban MObility (SUMO) simulation is proposed in this paper. First, based on the established CS selection model for EVs, SUMO is utilized to simulate the real-time vehicle and road conditions of the transportation system under given charging prices, providing feedback on charging demand as well as individual and global traffic congestion costs. Next, an Adversarial Diverse Ensemble (ADE) surrogate-based traffic state prediction model is developed to overcome the time-intensive nature of SUMO and integrate it into the multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) framework. Then, a Graph Attention Network-based bi-level multi-agent Soft Actor-Critic (GAT-BLMASAC) algorithm is proposed to solve the coordinated pricing problem in a bi-level architecture, where the upper level optimizes bidding strategies of generation companies (GenCos) as price-makers and the lower level coordinates pricing among CSs as price-takers. Finally, simulations on a real-world Hong Kong transport network demonstrate that the pricing strategies derived from the proposed framework significantly mitigate traffic congestion while enhancing the total profit of CSs. The proposed algorithm exhibits robust convergence in the multi-leader-multi-follower game, offering a scalable solution for coordinated CS pricing in high-density urban settings.

title: MAGIC: Meta-Ability Guided Interactive Chain-of-Distillation for Effective-and-Efficient Vision-and-Language Navigation
authors: Liuyi Wang, Zongtao He, Mengjiao Shen, Jingwei Yang, Chengju Liu, Qijun Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Despite the remarkable developments of recent large models in Embodied Artificial Intelligence (E-AI), their integration into robotics is hampered by their excessive parameter sizes and computational demands. Towards the Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) task, a core task in E-AI, this paper reveals the great potential of using knowledge distillation for obtaining lightweight student models by proposing a Meta-Ability Guided Interactive Chain-of-distillation (MAGIC) method. Specifically, a Meta-Ability Knowledge Distillation (MAKD) framework is proposed for decoupling and refining the necessary meta-abilities of VLN agents. A Meta-Knowledge Randomization Weighting (MKRW) and a Meta-Knowledge Transferable Determination (MKTD) module are incorporated to dynamically adjust aggregation weights at the meta-ability and sample levels, respectively. Moving beyond the traditional one-step unidirectional distillation, an Interactive Chain-of-Distillation (ICoD) learning strategy is proposed to allow students to give feedback to teachers, forming a new multi-step teacher-student co-evolution pipeline. Remarkably, on the R2R test unseen public leaderboard, our smallest model, MAGIC-S, with only 5% (11M) of the teacher's size, outperforms all previous methods under the same training data. Additionally, our largest model, MAGIC-L, surpasses the previous state-of-the-art by 5.84% in SPL and 3.18% in SR. Furthermore, a new dataset was collected and annotated from our living environments, where MAGIC-S demonstrated superior performance and real-time efficiency. Our code is publicly available on https://github.com/CrystalSixone/VLN-MAGIC.

title: Adaptive Multi-UAV Relay Deployment Framework in Satellite Aerial Ground Integrated Systems
authors: Bhola, Yu-Jia Chen, Ashutosh Balakrishnan, Swades De, Li-Chun Wang
abstract: The sixth-generation (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathbf {6}$</tex-math></inline-formula>G) communication networks are expected to provide high data rates, ultra-reliable communication, and massive connectivity, especially in challenging environments such as dense urban areas and disaster-affected regions. However, traditional terrestrial-only networks face significant challenges in these scenarios, including signal blockages from high-rise buildings, traffic congestion, and dynamic user distributions. To address these limitations, we propose the adaptive multi-UAV deployment (AMUD) framework within satellite air-ground integrated networks (SAGINs). The AMUD framework dynamically deploys amplify-and-forward multiple unmanned aerial vehicle relay (UAVr) in with low Earth orbit (LEO) satellites to improve coverage, alleviate congestion, and ensure reliable communication in non-line-of-sight and high-demand conditions. We formulate an optimization problem that aims to jointly maximize the energy efficiency of the total network and the total capacity while ensuring the fairness of the total capacity and satisfying the user's requirements. The simulation results demonstrate that AMUD improves the total capacity of the network, improves the total energy efficiency, and increases the fairness of the capacity compared to traditional LEO satellite and ground base station only systems.

title: Differential Force Sensing for Human-Artificial Muscle Interaction in Wearable Robots
authors: Seran Lee, Sujin Park, Seong Jun Park, Cheol Hoon Park, Uikyum Kim
abstract: The differentiation between the wearer’s intention and the robot’s assistance has been insufficiently considered in wearable robot systems. However, decoupling these forces is essential to enhance control precision, efficiency, and safety. This study proposes a differential force sensor that simultaneously measures human intention force and actuator assistive force. Its structural design distinguishes these individual forces from their combined interaction force within a single module. The sensor consists of a board with differential sensing cells, plates for force separation, and connecting lugs. Specifically, a lamina emergent torsional flexure is integrated into the top plate to provide anisotropic stiffness with respect to these two forces. This flexure enables tunable stiffness and measurement range through parameter selection. The proposed sensor achieves a compact form with a diameter of 40 mm and a weight of 37.6 g, while supporting loads of up to 100 N. With net human torque defined as the wearer’s intention, the sensor data were processed using a deep learning-based calibration algorithm. This resulted in estimation accuracies of 94.29% for net human torque and 98.14% for actuator force. Finally, the sensor was verified through performance experiments. The feasibility of differential force sensing was demonstrated using an artificial muscle wearable suit.

title: The Evolution of Tomographic Sensors
authors: Marco Da Silva
abstract: Tomography entered the public imagination as a "scanner" i.e., a machine that takes a picture of the inner body. In engineering terms, however, tomography is better understood as a family of tomographic sensors: distributed sensors arranged so that their measurements, once processed and fused, become spatial information of an object that no single sensor element can provide on its own. Tomography has repeatedly reinvented itself when moving from medical to industrial applications: the foundations may stay familiar, but the sensor constraints such as speed, geometry, robustness, and computation change drastically.

title: A Survey on the Resilience of Supply Chain Networks
authors: Ebenezer Quayson, Jacques Bou Abdo, Rebekah Michael
abstract: Globalization and technological advancements have evolved supply chains from a linear connection of components into intricate, interconnected networks, rendering them vulnerable to disruptions from events like natural disasters, pandemics as well as targeted Cyber attacks. This systematic review investigates resilience of supply chain networks from the perspective of network topology characteristics, modeling them as complex adaptive systems (CAS). The study seeks to address the following research questions; (1) how topological characteristics influence resilience to cascading failures and shocks, (2) what methodologies are used to model resilience of supply chain networks as a property of complex adaptive system and (3) how do multi-stage or multilayer graph-based approaches contribute to enhancing supply chain network resilience models. Findings revealed that supply chain networks were implemented with topologies such as scale-free, random topolgy, small-world, multilayer or multiplex topology as well as specialized structures like multipolar or community, non-planar, core periphery and block diagonal. Structural metrics, such as connectivity, centrality, and clustering, underscore vulnerabilities, while redundancy and alternative paths mitigate cascades. CAS modeling utilized agent-based simulations, Nodes-Edges (NK) frameworks, and evolutionary models to capture adaptive behaviors such as rewiring and trust-building, transforming static robustness into dynamic recovery. Multilayer techniques considered interdependencies across layers, enabling precise risk tracing and optimization, albeit often limited by static assumptions.

title: Fixed-Time Consensus of Multi-Agent Systems in Asymmetric Signed Networks With Application to UAV Swarms
authors: Yujiang Zhong, Zhizhong Liu, Xiaoyue Jin, Litong Fan, Zhen Wang
abstract: A fixed-time consensus protocol for multi-agent systems in asymmetric signed networks is proposed by integrating evolutionary game theory with a dual-mechanism incentive approach. Three key challenges are addressed through novel mechanisms. First, asymmetric interactions among agents in signed networks are characterized through a modified evolutionary game framework that captures both cooperative and competitive relationships. Second, an adaptive consensus protocol is proposed, enabling the system to achieve fixed-time convergence despite time-varying network topologies. Third, a DQN-optimized hierarchical external incentive mechanism is designed to accelerate consensus. Cooperation among agents is effectively promoted by combining structural importance-based stratification with dynamic reward allocation. Incentive parameters are dynamically adjusted based on network states and agent behaviors through the DQN optimization framework, resulting in significant convergence efficiency improvements. Convergence is guaranteed precisely within a fixed time bound through a comprehensive Lyapunov-based stability analysis, regardless of initial conditions. The theoretical fixed-time convergence is verified through collaborative search mission simulations with UAV swarms, demonstrating the ability to achieve consensus in asymmetric signed networks with both cooperative and antagonistic relationships.

title: FIR Filter Implementation in XilinxISE for Radar Signal Processing
authors: Smitha N, Manjula Vasant Kiresur, Shreya M, Shreeja, Shreya V, Neha S M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This paper presents a radar signal processing using Finite Impulse Response (FIR) filters. Linear Frequency Modulated (LFM) chirp signals are employed to improve range resolution via pulse compression. FIR filters, chosen for their inherent stability and linear phase response, effectively suppress clutter and noise. MATLAB is used for precise filter coefficient design and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) analysis, facilitating filter optimization. The optimized filters are implemented on Xilinx Spartan-6 FPGAs using the ISE environment, exploiting the FPGA’s parallel structure to enhance processing efficiency while reducing power consumption. Experimental results demonstrate improved radar signal clarity and enhanced detection performance. This work highlights the benefits of integrating MATLAB-based design tools with FPGA platforms, offering a scalable and efficient approach to high-performance radar signal processing.

title: Real-Time Intrusion Detection for IoT Networks Using Machine Learning
authors: Suresh K, Pola Bala Venkata Ganesh, Kattamuri Sai Siddhardha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The usage of Internet of Things (IOT) devices in different fields such as healthcare, smart homes, transportation and industrial automation has made digital networks susceptible to cyber attacks. Although IOT devices are extremely convenient, they usually lack the computing capabilities, do not have standard security regulations and ineffective update options, which is why conventional security practices are not sufficient. The threats have become more complex, and there is the need of a simple, scalable and intelligent security system capable of identifying and preventing attacks fast. The proposed project is an intrusion detection system (IDS) tailored to IOT networks, created and built in real-time, deployed on the machine learning algorithm and network data collected with Wireshark.The IDS is configured on a test network of IOT devices communicating over a local Wi-Fi, which ensures that the system could be tested in real-life.

title: HAMNet: Hierarchical Artifact Memory Network for Interpretable Deepfake Audio Detection
authors: Nithya Shankar S, Sai Raman D, M. Chandralekha, Rahul Raj M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Audio deepfakes pose a significant security threat in current digital ecosystems. State-of-the-art detection systems are not interpretable and fail to generalize when new synthesis methods are introduced. This work introduces HAMNet, Hierarchical Artifact Memory Network: a deep learning model architecture for interpretable deepfake audio detection. The architecture is based on three major innovations that include: i) hierarchical multi-resolution analysis processing audio at fine, midand coarse temporal levels for artifact detection at multiple time-scales; ii) an artifact memory bank that uses learnable prototype vectors for explicit identification of artifacts, few-shot learning; iii) spatial attention maps illustrating the exact position of artifacts in spectrograms. We transform model decisions to forensic evidence using rich visualizations. The testing of the developed system on the Fake-or-Real (FoR) dataset is performed, achieving 91.3% test accuracy with 93.3% confidence on synthetic audio and AUC-ROC of 0.98. An interpretable design enables its application in forensic investigations and other legal procedures. The general approach, adopted from memory mechanisms, enables rapid updates for new deepfake generative techniques without full model re-training.

title: Enhancing Independent Mobility for the Visually Impaired Through Real-Time Vision-based Obstacle Detection and Audio Guidance
authors: Shravan Shanker, Radhu Krishna R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Reliable real-time navigation assistance is essential for visually impaired individuals navigating complex urban environments that contain diverse static and dynamic obstacles. An assistant system that is based on vision is created by combining lightweight YOLOv8n object detection and dense optical flow-based motion analysis to improve the perception of the environment and motion awareness under realistic conditions. The system has been organized into five synchronized units, which include systems of preprocessing, obstacle detection, detection handling, movement detection and audio feedback. Ego-motion compensation is done through homography transformation and parallax adjustment to ensure that motion interpretation is robust when the user is in continuous motion and the camera is moving, rather than moving the object being observed. The lightweight model architecture enables real-time performance on small, constrained resource, hardware platforms. The system identifies and categorizes six classes of objects of interest to navigation such as crosswalks, obstacles, pedestrians, signals, stairs and vehicles and gives real-time audio feedback with the eSpeak text-to-speech engine. On an experimental evaluation of 8,111 annotated cases, the performance of detection is strong with a total performance of 0.800 and 0.768 recall with mAP at 0.5 and mAP at 0.5:0.95, respectively. Critical classes, including vehicles (mAP at 0.5 = 0.919) and pedestrians (mAP at 0.5 = 0.915) are found to have high detection accuracy, which is indicative of good obstacle awareness. These findings demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed system in enhancing the level of safety in navigation, situational awareness and autonomous mobility among the visually impaired users.

title: Integrated RGB-Thermographic Vision System for Individual Calf Identification and Health Monitoring
authors: Aung Si Thu Moe, Pyke Tin, Thi Thi Zin, Masaru Aikawa, Kazuyuki Honkawa
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: The increasing demand for sustainable and welfare-oriented livestock farming has accelerated the use of advanced sensing and computer vision technologies. This study presents an integrated RGB-thermographic vision system for individual calf identification and automatic body temperature estimation during feeding at a milk-drinking station. Calves are highly vulnerable to infectious diseases, and traditional health monitoring methods are labor-intensive and inefficient. The proposed system employs a Detectron2 Mask R-CNN with a ResNeXt-101 backbone for calf body region detection and a ResNet-101-based transfer learning model for individual identification. Simultaneously, thermal images are analyzed to detect the calf's eye and extract temperature data for health evaluation. By combining RGB and thermal modalities, this approach enables contactless, automated, and continuous monitoring, reducing manual intervention and supporting early disease detection in precision livestock farming.

title:
Design and Evaluation of FA/MA-Graded FA
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    MA
                    <sub>1−x</sub>
                    SnI
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Absorber for High-Efficiency, Lead-Free Perovskite Solar Cells
authors: S. Hemanandh, S. Saravanan, R. Nithiswaran, J. Charles Pravin
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: In this paper, a lead-free perovskite solar cell with a compositionally graded absorber layer FAxMA1−xSnI is numerically simulated using SCAPS-1D software. The structure under study includes the layers FTO/SnO/FAxMA1−xSnI/CuI/Carbon, where the material characteristics are based on previous studies. For more accurate simulation, the effect of interface trap states on the properties of the device was also considered. In particular, the proposed absorber layer design includes a gradient in composition, which allows for increasing the electric field inside the material and, therefore, improving the generation of photoelectrons. It should be noted that the gradient consists of two regions: a part enriched in MA at the contact with the electron transport layer and another one enriched in FA at the contact with the hole transport layer. A comprehensive analysis of key cell characteristics such as doping concentration, defect density, and interface quality was conducted to evaluate their influence on the efficiency of the proposed cell. Thus, under optimal conditions, the maximum efficiency reached 30.87%. These findings, although being obtained for ideal conditions, clearly demonstrate the potential of compositionally graded absorbers for further development of lead-free perovskites.

title: Power System Anomaly Data Generation and Adversarial Learning Methods Based on Diffusion Models
authors: Xiangna Li, Fang Xu, Xuedong Li, Shufeng Kou, Bin Ma, Zhenxiang Pan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of power system operation, traditional anomaly detection methods face performance limitations under conditions of insufficient samples and time series non-stationarity. This study introduces a diffusion-generative model and adversarial learning to construct an anomaly generation and detection method for multivariate time series data in power systems. The experiments show that the generated abnormal data exhibit excellent performance in terms of distribution consistency and temporal structure preservation. The Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) has decreased to 0.098, the Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) distance is 9.37, the variable correlation deviation has dropped to 0.046, and the diversity index has increased to 0.88. In terms of detection performance, the abnormal detection delay has been shortened to 4.58 seconds, the false alarm rate has decreased to 3.12%, the missed detection rate has been reduced to 2.94%, the abnormal score separability has improved to 3.07, and the stability index has reached 0.83. This is significantly superior to the comparison methods. This method can effectively alleviate the problem of insufficient anomaly samples, improve the accuracy and stability of power system anomaly detection, and provide new technical support for the safe operation of complex power systems.

title: RTL to GDSII Implementation of Lightweight IOT Authentication Hardware using Multifunction Residue-Based Cryptographic Architectures
authors: Gavaskar K, Paramasivam K, Sneka S, Vimal R, Sandhyavathi K, Thilagavathy K P
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Internet of Things (IOT) devices need to be secure and should be of low energy consumption. In terms of security, a lightweight hardware-based cryptography method is used. In the existing methods, it consumes more power due to continuous clock switching. This paper proposes a low-power hardware authentication encoder. In order to reduce dynamic power consumption due to unwanted clock switching in the system, clock gating is used at the architectural level. This clock gating uses an enable signal to reduce clock switching. This enable signal will be active only when necessary. This clock will propagate and reduce the power consumption in the system. The proposed design includes an ALU, a 63-bit LFSR, and a carry look-ahead adder. The design is verified, and it shows that the design uses low power consumption compared to other existing designs.

title: Enhancing Ethernet Security through Cyber Physical Systems with Real Time Monitoring, MAC Spoofing Detection and Automated Intrusion Alerts
authors: Priya Lakshmi, Srimathi S, Karan Aylu P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: In recent years, both universities and corporate settings have increasingly encountered issues with system theft and unauthorized access to devices within wired Ethernet networks. Traditional network monitoring tools and switch logs typically focus on recognizing logical disconnections but do not effectively detect the physical removal of Ethernet cables or real-time MAC spoofing attempts. To tackle these issues, this document suggests an Ethernet-Based Physical Theft Detection and Cyber Intrusion Prevention System that continually oversees all connected systems from a central server. The system employs ICMP ping requests, ARP table examination, and MAC address verification to effectively distinguish between regular shutdowns and potentially fraudulent physical disconnections or spoofing events. A Threat Classification Engine analyzes network behaviors to generate immediate alerts and preserve records via a web-based dashboard for the IT team. Created to be affordable, expandable, and straightforward to implement with current infrastructure, this system improves the physical and cybersecurity of wired LAN settings without needing extra endpoint hardware.

title: Resilience in Motion - Adaptive Digital Twins and Real-Time Security Strategies
authors: Damien Nicolas, Christophe Feltus
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: As cyber-physical systems become increasingly interconnected and exposed to dynamic threats, the need for resilient and responsive security mechanisms becomes critical. This position paper explores the potential of self-adaptive digital twins as active agents for live security mitigation. By continuously monitoring, analyzing, and adapting to both internal system states and external threat landscapes, these digital counterparts can enhance real-time situational awareness and support decision-making processes. We argue that embedding self-adaptive capabilities into digital twins provides a promising pathway toward greater operational resilience, for instance against zero-day attacks, advanced persistent threats and other unforeseen vulnerabilities. The paper outlines the conceptual foundations, discusses architectural considerations, and highlights the key challenges and research directions needed to realize this vision.

title: AI-based Traffic Flow Optimization for Mixed Autonomous and Human-Driven Vehicles in Malaysia
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The progressive deployment of autonomous vehicles (AVs) in Malaysia will lead to extended periods of mixed traffic conditions, where AVs coexist with human-driven vehicles (HDVs). Such mixed autonomy environments introduce complex, nonlinear traffic dynamics that challenge conventional traffic management and signal control strategies. This paper proposes an AI-based traffic flow optimization framework designed for Malaysian urban road networks, integrating real-time sensing, traffic state prediction, and deep reinforcement learning–based control. The framework explicitly accounts for heterogeneous driving behaviors by incorporating AV penetration rates and stochastic human driving characteristics into the control process. Simulation-based evaluations under representative Malaysian traffic scenarios demonstrate significant reductions in average travel time, vehicle delay, and stop frequency, alongside improved intersection throughput, compared with fixed-time and actuated control methods. The results indicate that AI-enabled traffic optimization can effectively stabilize mixed traffic flows and enhance network efficiency, supporting Malaysia’s transition toward intelligent and autonomous transportation systems.

title: Decentralized E-Voting Platform with Fingerprint Authentication and Blockchain Security
authors: Rahul J, Rajamohan R, Sinthuja P M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: In contemporary democratic regimes, secure, trans-parent, and accessible voting processes are the most important aspect. Conventional paper-based voting is usually plagued by inefficiencies, voter forgery, and non-real-time verification. The proposed Secure Biometric E-Voting System in this project utilizes fingerprint-based biometric authentication, blockchain technology, and cloud integration to fix all those problems. The voter verification is done by the system with the help of their unique biometric information (fingerprints), which prevents impersonation and double voting by only allowing legitimate voters to vote. After verification, the vote is encrypted and stored in a blockchain ledger, which ensures immutability, transparency, and decentralization. In addition, the system incorporates cloud computing infrastructure to enable real-time storage of votes, scalability, and remote access. This project showcases a secure, scalable, and tamper-resistant e-voting system that modernizes the voting process with state-of-the-art technologies to enhance voter confidence, election integrity, and participation levels

title: FPGA-Based Intelligent Traffic Signal Control System Using Fuzzy Logic
authors: Amar Babu Yandrapati, Salomi Naidu Kode, Manish Nagula, Sainadh Varikuti
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Fixed-time traffic signal control systems are a major cause of traffic congestion at urban intersections. Due to these systems’ inability to adjust to current traffic conditions, resulting in longer wait times, higher emissions, and higher fuel usage. A fuzzy logic-based intelligent traffic signal control system is suggested as a solution to this problem. The system dynamically modifies the duration of the green signal based on input parameters such as waiting time and traffic density. Xilinx Vivado is used to synthesize the suggested design and Verilog HDL is used to implement it on a Zynq-7000 FPGA. When compared to traditional fixed-time controllers, simulation and implementation results demonstrate that the suggested system decreases waiting times and increases traffic flow efficiency.

title: Neurochain: A cognitive Blockchain FrameWork for Big Data Intelligence
authors: G.V.Yogeswari, S.Tharun Sakthe, V.Kumara Sundari M E
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The increase in medical data is bringing immense problems in terms of secure storage, sharing, and interoperability of healthcare systems. The traditional methods fail to balance the access to the data and the privacy of the patients, which frequently results in increased expenses and potential security violations. This work introduces a complete, safe, and scalable system architecture of healthcare data management combining data compression, blockchain technology, big data management, and cloud infrastructure. The proposed system decreases the amount of medical information and maintains diagnostic integrity, encrypts the information to fulfill the requirement of privacy control, and it is stored on blockchain that cannot be tampered to ensure support is provided to the process of transactions being tracked. MongoDB will support the management of semi-structured patient records thus resulting and AWS will provide system resilience and real-time availability. The innovation behind this approach is that it has an integrated architecture that works in parallel in a way that optimality in the storage, security as well as interoperability is maintained to an extent that multi-institutional research can be supported without compromising on the integrity of data. The system efficacy would be determined against simulations that would attempt to experiment with system performance, system security and system extensibility in real healthcare applications.

title: Reflective RAG: A Self-Correcting Cognitive Framework for Reducing Hallucinations in AI Research Assistants
authors: S. Janani, Anurag Das
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Artificial intelligence has already become an integral part of lots of modern educational tools, and research assistants are particularly included by the recent emergence of large language models. Despite their answers' speed and clarity, many systems still produce a lot of wrong or unsupported information, making learning less productive. Retrieval-Augmented Generation partially mitigates this issue by anchoring responses in external documents. However, it does not actively validate the accuracy of generated outputs. This paper introduces the Reflective RAG framework, adding in stages for self-evaluation and correction within the RAG pipeline. The suggested system retrieves relevant academic content, makes an initial response, checks the validity of its input with semantic consistency checks, improves the output based on the differences found, and provides a confidence score to give an estimate of its reliability. Experimental observations show improvements in response accuracy and a reduction in hallucinations over traditional RAG baselines. Since the solution is small and can fit on personal computers, it will be contributing to meeting Sustainable Development Goal 4 (Quality Education).

title: HECA-Based Hybrid Forecasting for Precise Weather Temperature Prediction using Deep Learning
authors: Devi T
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Weather temperature prediction has involved research interest from many domains where in recent decades there are unpredicted due to its severity and temperature variations such as agriculture wastages, dry land, water scarcity and so on. Existing techniques used many older models such as support vector machines and other prediction methods. These models suggested only accuracy and statistical results were very expensive in cost. Also, too expensive computation leads to higher demands and conjunctions that were not able to achieve climatic data based on historical ranges. Time series analysis is being implemented and helps to involve reports based on analysis statistically. To improve the selective statistical data and compare the analysis a simplified estimation using Hybrid Exploratory Convolutional architecture (HECA) disaster and agriculture wastages due to weather temperature variations can be avoided. In terms of existing results and performance metrics a comparison based on hourly and monthly records evaluation has been analyzed. Metrics such as Mean square error and loss are used to evaluate the proposed work performance. Predicting future temperatures using HECA and historical climate data is vital for agriculture, tourism, and disaster preparedness that can forecast the applications implementation and enhance the cost reduction to reduce the finance imbalance all over the world.

title: Multi-Access Technology Integration and Intelligent Scheduling Method for New Distribution Communication System
authors: Manchun Lu, Wenjuan Liang, Zhengfeng Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: This paper proposes a method for integrating multi-access technologies and intelligent scheduling in modern distribution network communication systems. It addresses challenges like poor stability, protocol incompatibility, and resource conflicts by designing a multi-access gateway, intelligent scheduling algorithm, and protocol stack virtualization. The method dynamically selects the best access path, optimizes resource allocation, and prioritizes critical tasks. Using deep learning and reinforcement learning, the system adapts to network changes in real-time, enhancing adaptability and robustness. Dynamic network slicing and Quality of Service (QoS) mechanisms further optimize resource allocation and service transmission. Experimental results show that this approach improves stability, efficiency, and flexibility in distribution network communication systems.

title: AI-Powered Dynamic Traffic Signal Control System for Emergency Vehicle Preemption
authors: Kumar V, Nandish Kumar T R, Samprit Nagshetty, Shailendra, Palanivel R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Rapid urbanization and increasing vehicular density have severely impacted the response times of Emergency Medical Services (EMS). Conventional traffic management systems, which operate on fixed-time cycles or simple induction loops, often trap ambulances in gridlock, leading to critical delays in patient care. Existing preemption solutions relying on GPS or RFID require expensive, universal hardware adoption, limiting their scalability in developing economies. This paper proposes a novel, cost-effective AI-Powered Dynamic Traffic Signal Control System that integrates computer vision and acoustic signal processing to autonomously detect and prioritize emergency vehicles. The system leverages a fine-tuned YOLOv8 Nano architecture for visual object detection and a Mel-Spectrogram Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for siren audio classification, achieving a robust "Dual-Sensor" redundancy. Furthermore, we introduce a context-aware intersection management algorithm that estimates real-time traffic density to dynamically adjust green signal duration. A lightweight IoT layer utilizing the MQTT protocol ensures real-time alerts to infrastructure with sub-200ms latency. Experimental results on a custom dataset of 10,647 images and 8,732 audio samples demonstrate a visual mean Average Precision (mAP@50) of 0.97 and an audio classification accuracy of 94.5%. The proposed system effectively reduces intersection wait times for emergency vehicles while minimizing disruption to general traffic.
