title: Deep Transfer Learning for Produce Quality Assessment: Benchmarking DenseNet and EfficientNet on Citrus Images
authors: Naveen Sharma, Hitesh Marwaha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Globally, the consumption and production patterns of citruses, including oranges, lemons, limes, and grapefruits, are dominated by an ever-increasing requirement for healthy produce, making citruses some of the most expensive fruit products in the global market. Moreover, with an ever-growing focus on the adoption and use of automatic grading and quality measurement tools based on global standards and differing levels of post-harvest loss, these consumption and production patterns are also influenced. Moreover, major challenges include overcoming the effects of global warming on production and the limitations imposed on production due to diseases such as citrus greening. Analysis and comparison of various machine learning and deep learning models for multi-class classification problems involving 131 fruit and vegetable products, with extremely high data dimensionality based on images, form the focus and objective of this research. Among various traditional machine learning algorithms, Random Forest stands as a competent benchmark for deep learning, achieving an accuracy rate of 72.8%. This is still low for classification problems based on images, due to limitations in understanding image complexities. The accuracy difference between various traditional algorithms and deep learning algorithms appeared to be very low. Based on compound scaling among various capable architectures, EfficientNet-B3 demonstrated a significant improvement over previous accuracy rates, achieving an accuracy rate of 96.4%. Notably, DenseNet-201, with its capabilities due to dense connections, showed better accuracy and topped the list with an impressive accuracy rate of 97.2% and 97.1% F1-score.

title: Experimental Evaluation of TSN-5G Interworking for Cyclic Industrial Traffic
authors: Kedar Naik, Seyedali Hadian, Manuel Schappacher, Dominik Welte, Andreas Laufer, Axel Sikora
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: This paper presents a measurement-driven evaluation of a TSN-5G interworking prototype following the 3GPP TSN Translator architecture (Device-Side and Network-Side Translators). In the evaluated system, a private 5 G standalone network provides an Ethernet bridging service between two TSN/Ethernet segments. The study investigates two feasibility aspects for cyclic industrial communication: (i) boundary time continuity of IEEE 802.1AS/gPTP across the interworking segment and (ii) the ability to deliver periodic traffic within tens-of-milliseconds cycle budgets during long-duration operation. Measurements are conducted during a continuous two-hour experiment. Two representative cyclic workloads are evaluated: a 32 ms multi-stream TSN-like traffic profile generated using the RTC testbench and a 64 ms industrial PROFINET RT/PROFIdrive workload from a PLC-drive setup. The results show that time continuity is preserved at the TSN boundaries with submicrosecond precision, providing a stable phase reference for cycle-aligned experiments. Median packet delays remain well below the respective cycle budgets for both workloads. However, latency distributions exhibit long-tail behavior and directional asymmetry, resulting in rare but non-zero deadline exceedances. These observations indicate that TSN-5G interworking in a COTS/open-source prototype can provide statistical timeliness for cyclic traffic with tens-of-milliseconds cycles, while strict TSN-grade determinism remains limited by tail latency events in the wireless transport path.

title: Hybrid CNN-Transformer Architecture for Multi-Class Classification of Astrophysical Objects
authors: Richa Nigam, Alok Misra, Ambuj Kumar Agarwal, Raj Gaurang Tiwari, Firdous Sadaf, Nitin Rakesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Standard classification is problematic with the next-generation sky surveys' vast astronomical image data. From the Astrophysical Objects Image Dataset, this research offers a hybrid deep learning architecture that classifies astrophysical objects using CNNs and Transformer encoders. A five-class classification task including stars, galaxies, quasars, supernovae, and gravitational lenses yields 94.7% accuracy using CNN hierarchical feature extraction with Transformer global dependency modeling. CNN (88.2%) and Transformer (85.6%) perform inferiorly for rare object classes than the proposed hybrid model. This study explicitly incorporates astronomical priors like rotational invariance and multi-wavelength correlations into network construction, making it resilient to data shortages and class imbalance in astronomical datasets. The suggested hybrid technique fulfills astronomical machine learning criteria for scalability to large datasets, generalizability beyond training distributions, and data efficiency with few labeled instances. This research prepares automated categorization pipelines for future surveys like the Legacy Survey of Space and Time (LSST) and Euclid, which will record billions of items and require rapid scientific analysis.

title: Real World Performance Evaluation of AI Hardware: Beyond Standardized Benchmarks
authors: Chithik Raja Mohamed Sinnaiya, Mohammed Musallam Al-Mahri, Wasim Haidar SK, Venkateshwaran Radhakrishnan, Devarajan Veerasamy, Ayaz Mohiuddin
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Many commercial sectors are currently transformed by Artificial Intelligence (AI), yet the ultimate speed of Artificial Intelligence (AI) constantly relies on the underlying physical machine components that people use. Our research examines the standard of AI equipment by looking at the primary measurement units alongside the recognized technical tests of the trade and the actual results in practical settings. Various AI hardware such as the Central Processing Unit (CPU) and the General Processing Unit (GPU) and the Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) and different unique AI chips are compared in this study through the use of widely accepted industry benchmarks like MLPerf, image sorting and natural language processing. Through this investigation, it is revealed that even though the manufactured tests provide useful data for a beginning point, the practical speed of AI equipment can change greatly because of the power of the software layers and the memory data speed and the specific qualities of the working position. Practical suggestions for choosing AI equipment according to specific needs are provided at the end of the research.

title: Integrating LSTM Forecasting with NLP Chatbots for Fair Agricultural Markets
authors: G. Jijendhar, R. Dhanush, C. Nallusamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Fluctuations of local vegetable markets tend to lead to unjust pricing, middlemen exploitation and unsteadiness in the finances of farmers, and uneven access of fresh products by the consumers. The paper describes the design of an AI-driven application, which is a combination of Short-term vegetable price forecasting based on Long Short-term memory (LSTM) networks and a Natural Language Processing (NLP) chatbot to promote communication between farmers and customers in real-time. The LSTM model examines past price patterns, seasonal changes, supply-demand patterns and other environmental conditions with a view to coming up with precise and timely price forecasts. At the same time, the NLP chatbot is a multilingual system that allows asking about prices, receiving market data, and storage and sale tips. The proposed system is three-layer architecture, including Data Layer (which is used to get and process the market information), Application Layer (which is used to predict and generate conversational responses), and Presentation Layer (which is used to give the web and mobile interface). Evaluation is done on an experimental basis with better prediction accuracy, communication among stakeholders, less reliance on intermediaries and less post-harvest losses. The platform facilitates fair trade, sustainable practice in agriculture, and stability in farmer’s income, enhancing access and affordability by consumers, through facilitating data-driven decision-making and providing greater transparency. The system offers a viable way of enhancing local agricultural market ecosystems.

title: Multi-View Imaging in Networked Sensing Systems: A Covariance-Based Approach
authors: Junyuan Gao, Weifeng Zhu, Yanmo Hu, Shuowen Zhang, Jiannong Cao, Yongpeng Wu, Giuseppe Caire, Liang Liu
abstract: This paper considers multi-view imaging in a sixth-generation (6G) integrated sensing and communication network, which consists of a transmit base-station (TBS), multiple receive base-stations (RBSs) connected to a central processing unit (CPU), and multiple extended targets. Our goal is to devise an effective multi-view imaging technique that can jointly leverage the echo signals at all the RBSs to precisely construct the image of these targets. To achieve this goal, we propose a two-phase framework. In Phase I, each RBS recovers an individual image of all the targets from its own view, which is obtained via utilizing its received signals’ sample covariance matrix to detect the grids with non-zero effective scattering intensity in the region of interest. Moreover, the shape of each grid is adjusted to conform to target geometries. In Phase II, the CPU fuses the individual images of all the RBSs to construct a higher-quality image of all the targets. To this end, we first design an edge-preserving natural neighbor interpolation (EP-NNI) method and then formulate an optimization problem to fuse the interpolated results. Extensive numerical results show that the proposed scheme significantly enhances imaging performance, facilitating high-quality environment reconstruction for future 6G networks.

title: User Centric Explainable Machine Learning for Financial Fraud Detection
authors: Devyansh Jain, R. Kanagaraj
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The digital revolution has revolutionized the payments and financial transactions sector globally, but has also increased the reach, scale and sophistication of financial fraud. As a result, increasingly complex Automated Fraud Detection Systems have become the need of the hour for the Banking, Financial Services and Insurance (BFSI) industry today. While machine learning models are able to offer superior fraud detection prowess compared to rule-based approaches, such models often function as a black box, with little visibility into its decisions for the fraud analysts and auditors who have to deal with suspicious activity investigations. This reduces the trust and confidence in the prediction system, making it less useful in risky real-world scenarios. This paper proposes a user-centric explainable fraud transaction prediction framework. A supervised fraud transaction prediction model is built using features derived from financial transactions, and model performance is evaluated with metrics that are important for a fraud prediction use case, such as precision, recall, and F-score. The SHAP (SHapley Additive Explanations) technique is used for feature selection and post-hoc explanation of feature importance at a global level as well as for specific transactions. Further, a Natural Language layer is employed to provide human understandable summary explanations about the most important feature(s) that contributed to the decision made by the prediction model. Finally, the robustness of explanations under minor input changes is also analyzed. This framework will help provide better decision support for real-world financial fraud analysis.

title: Machine Learning Framework for Early Detection of Suicidal Ideation on Social Media
authors: Yadav Pranathi Sai, Satti Surya Sai Shanmukha Reddy, P.J.Beslin Pajila
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Social media has facilitated users to post their emotional thoughts, and psychological distress symptoms which can contribute to suicidal ideation. To prevent suicides, individuals need to be identified early to receive necessary mental treatment. This paper proposes an approach with deep learning and machine learning that is able to identify suicidal text on social media automatically.The base models taken from baseline are Random Forest, Naive Bayes, Gradient Boosting Decision Tree, and XGBoost. These models have been applied broadly to text classification with features. But, the above models do not completely capture compound meanings and sentiments in the text. To overcome this issue, this paper adds support vector machine and Bi directional long short term memory. For pre processing of data we are using Natural language processing techniques such as Tokenization, removal of stop words, lemmatization, and stemming it normalizes and cleans the text. Feature extraction can be done through Frequency Inverse Document Frequency and word embedding techniques such as Word2Vec. These allow the system to understand contextual relationships and meaning. The SVM model maximizes the separation of suicidal and non-suicidal messages. This allows classification even if it is dealing with high-dimensional data. However, BiLSTM is able to capture long-distance relationships and context in both directions. It excels in capturing context of future and past sentences. The feature is particularly beneficial when it comes to identifying emotional texts and suicidal intentions of the individual. The models are trained and evaluated against a big social media corpus. Their performance is exhibited in terms of Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score. Experimental results demonstrates that BiLSTM performs better than all the base algorithms with maximum accuracy. In conclusion, this paper offers a robust method for the identification of early suicidal text by leveraging the collaborative abilities of deep learning and machine learning in social media messages. By detecting such texts early, the system prevents suicide and promotes mental well-being with online communities.

title: Detection and Evaluation of Lung Cancer in Histology Slides using Deep Transfer Learning
authors: T. Babu, R. Sanjay, S. Sathish Kumar, Muhammad Attique Khan, S. Prabha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The happening rate of the cancer is globally rising due to several reasons, in which the Lung-Cancer (LC) is emerged as one of the top cancers in humans. To reduce its impact and save the human life, several methods are recommended and the biomedical image-based approach is a common practice. Invasive procedure, which uses the histopathology database is widely adopted for clinical evaluation of the LC and this work aims to develop deep-learning architecture (DLA) to classify benign/malignant class images. The developed DLA uses the following sections; (i) collection of images and modifying its size based on the need, (ii) implementing DLA for feature extraction and categorization, (iii) best features identification and generation of fused-features, and (iv) classification and performance verification. This research used softmax-classifier for detecting the LC based on the considered features and the developed system with fused-features help to get an accuracy of >99%.

title: Smoldering Simulation of Incense Burning Considering Ash Deformation
authors: Reio Echigo, Makoto Fujisawa, Masahiko Mikawa
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: We propose a visual simulation method for incense burning that considers the transition from a solid stick to deformable ash for computer graphics animation. Our method simulates two phenomena essential for plausible results: the smoldering process and the deformation of the ash. First, we achieve a chemically-aware simulation by calculating thermal energy based on experimental data and physical principles, which allows for the reproduction of realistic temperature changes in the burning incense. Second, for the ash deformation, we employ a multi-layer XPBD solver that enables a fast and stable simulation while providing detailed control over the ash's stiffness. By coupling these two simulations, our method realistically simulates the entire smoldering process. We demonstrate the robustness of our method by applying it to a complex shape, showing its extensibility to other combustion phenomena.

title: An Intelligent PIN Code Validation System based on Semantic Address Analysis and Random Forest Classification
authors: L.Hemalatha, R. Amutha, A. Aparna, P. Evangelin Angel, A. Evangeline
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Accurate usage of postal PIN codes plays a crucial role in ensuring reliable mail delivery and efficient logistics operations. In the current scenario, incorrect or mismatched PIN codes frequently occur due to manual entry errors, lack of address standardization, spelling variations, and poor readability of handwritten or scanned postal documents. Such inconsistencies lead to delivery delays, misrouting, and increased manual verification effort within postal and logistics systems. Existing address verification approaches largely rely on exact string matching or static lookup mechanisms, which fail to handle unstructured address formats and noisy text extracted from images. To address these challenges, a Smart PIN Code Validation and Recognition System is proposed that combines semantic address understanding with ensemble-based machine learning. Postal address text is extracted from uploaded images using optical character recognition and refined through rule-based normalization and fuzzy string matching to correct common errors. Semantic interpretation of address components is achieved using a transformer-based language model, enabling contextual understanding of locality, district, and regional information despite variations in spelling or structure. A supervised Random Forest classifier is employed to validate the correctness of the provided PIN code against the extracted address and to predict the most suitable PIN in cases of mismatch. Confidence scoring derived from ensemble probability estimates supports reliable validation decisions. The proposed methodology effectively overcomes limitations of traditional rule-based systems by handling unstructured inputs, OCR noise, and address ambiguity. Key features of the system include automated image-based address processing, semantic text representation, robust PIN validation, intelligent correction suggestions, and confidence-driven reliability assessment. Experimental outcomes demonstrate consistent and accurate PIN code validation across diverse address formats, significantly reducing manual intervention and delivery errors.

title: Real-Time Traffic Accident Detection Using YOLOv11 Based on the Derivative of Traffic Parameters
authors: K G Nandhika, A Priyadharshini, S Mithuna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The rapid growth of urban traffic has increased the frequency and severity of road accidents, highlighting the need for intelligent and real-time monitoring systems. This work presents an Enhanced Real-Time Traffic Accident Detection and Emergency Alert System that integrates deep learning, trajectory analysis, and automated communication to improve safety and response efficiency. The system employs the YOLOv11 object detection model to identify accident-related events such as collisions, overspeeding, lane deviations, and signal violations from live surveillance video streams. A dedicated trajectory and causality analysis module further interprets abnormal motion patterns to differentiate true accident scenarios from false positives. Upon incident confirmation, a structured alert—containing location, severity, and event details—is automatically transmitted to emergency responders through a Gemini API–based communication layer. A Flask web interface provides real-time visualization, historical analytics, and automated report generation for planners and enforcement agencies. Experimental evaluation across high- density urban sites demonstrated high detection accuracy (>96%), reduced emergency response time (<7 seconds end-to- end), and improved operational efficiency through automated reporting and hotspot analytics. The results confirm the system’s effectiveness in enhancing situational awareness and enabling faster decision-making, offering a scalable and intelligent solution for future smart-city traffic management.

title: Characterizing Domain Shift in PROFINET Intrusion Detection: A Multi-Site Ground-Truth Evaluation
authors: Jaafer Rahmani, Emiliano Sisinni, Paolo Ferrari, Kai Oliver Detken, Axel Sikora
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: Machine learning-based intrusion detection for industrial PROFINET networks suffers from a deployment gap: models trained on one site often fail on another because normal timing characteristics vary with factory layout and PLC programs. We present a ground-truth evaluation using 158 packet captures containing 53 confirmed ring-open anomalies from automotive manufacturing facilities across two continents and 53 production lines. We compare eleven detection methods (eight supervised, three unsupervised) using Leave-One-Line-Out cross-validation. ExtraTrees achieves AUROC 0.967 [0.935-0.991] with 88.7% true positive rate at 5% false positive rate. Feature importance analysis shows that all top- 20 features derive from inter-arrival time (IAT) statistics; cycle counters and status fields contribute negligibly. Cross-site transfer experiments reveal false positive rate drift up to 30% (6× the 5% target), demonstrating that domain-aware threshold calibration is essential for deployment.

title: A Unified Multimodal Perception and Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for Autonomous Operation of Bionic Robotic Arms
authors: Sheng Guo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: To address the comprehensive requirements of bionic robotic arms for high precision, autonomy, and safe collaboration in complex dynamic environments, this study proposes a unified and efficient multimodal perception and deep reinforcement learning collaborative control framework. This framework is used to achieve autonomous operation of the bionic robotic arm. The framework constructs a unified representation network with cross-modal semantic-geometric consistency, performing high-dimensional collaborative encoding of multisource heterogeneous information such as vision, depth, force, touch, electrophysiological signals, and language commands. Simultaneously, the model introduces a polarity-aware cross-modal fusion mechanism. At the control layer, this paper integrates continuum mechanical constraints and safe reinforcement learning strategies based on a continuous action space Markov decision process. This model uses a physically feasible region projection operator to dynamically constrain joint torques, tension, strain, and contact forces through the policy output. Furthermore, this paper introduces a multimodal-physical coupled state prediction model. This model embeds dynamic priors into the policy update process. At the experimental level, this study constructed a 7-DOF hybrid bionic robotic arm system, integrating rigid joints, a parallel wrist mechanism, and a continuous flexible forearm. Through multitask simulation and real-system testing, the system was evaluated for five complex tasks: multi-target dynamic grasping, compliant insertion, path replanning, language-guided assembly, and multimodal cooperative transportation. Results show that compared to various baseline methods, the proposed framework improves the average task success rate by approximately 18.4 percentage points on real-world systems. Simultaneously, the average task completion time is reduced by approximately 27.3%, and energy consumption is reduced by approximately 18.1%. Regarding policy convergence, the method achieves stable convergence within approximately 1.25 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup> steps. In a 72-hour continuous industrial operation test, the overall system success rate reached 93.6%. Furthermore, the perception-control closed-loop latency was 24.3 milliseconds, and the system interruption rate was reduced by 42.6% compared to the control methods.

title: Predicting Yaw System Faults in Wind Turbines Using SCADA Data and Deep Learning Models
authors: Yifan Li, Nuoxi Deng, Dan Du, Shiyun Peng
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: With the rapid development of the wind power industry, fault diagnosis and prediction of wind turbines have become crucial for ensuring their efficient operation. Yaw system faults, in particular, have a significant impact on the operating efficiency and power generation capacity of wind turbines; therefore, early fault identification and prediction are of great practical importance. To address this issue, this paper proposes a wind turbine yaw system fault prediction method that combines Pearson correlation coefficient feature selection with a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) +Transformer deep learning model. By performing feature selection on wind turbine Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) data, the Pearson correlation coefficient method is used to select variables significantly correlated with the fault occurrence time. Then, TCN is used to capture local features in the time-series data, and a Transformer model is combined to model global dependencies, ultimately achieving accurate prediction of the fault occurrence time. Experimental results show that the proposed method has higher prediction accuracy and stability compared to traditional models, especially in its ability to predict in advance.

title: Evaluating Deep Neural Network Architectures for Devanagari Script Recognition
authors: Madhurya Kumar Bhattacharya, Pratyush Kumar Rabha, Karan Buragohain, Saurabh Bilgaiyan, Usha Desai, Mahendra Kumar Gourisaria
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: It is difficult to identify handwritten characters in the Devanagari script because of its substantial visual resemblance among letters, intricate stroke patterns and the recurrent occurrence of the "Shirorekha", which hinders segmentation. In this study, eight deep learning architectures were compared on the UCI Devanagari dataset to examine how different modeling strategies perform under the same training conditions. This paper also analyzes various synthetic disturbances such as Gaussian noise and motion blur. As per the results, CRNN was found to be the best performing model with an accuracy of 99.43% and f1-score of 0.99. However, if we look at the 'trainable parameters' metric, SimCLR performed at a slightly lower accuracy of 99.16% than the CRNN model, but with a drastic reduction in 'trainable parameters' which makes this the second-best performing model. Relational designs such as Capsule Networks (CapsNet) and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) excelled in distinguishing characters with minor structural variations. The results of this study might be utilized to build a reliable and powerful OCR system capable of recognizing handwritten Devanagari text.

title: Adaptive Battery Management System for Efficient Integration of Bifacial PV and GaN Power Converters
authors: Kiruthiga Devi V, Veena P, Jeyabharath R, Kalaiselvan S, Dharaneesh R, Dinakar E
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: The aim of this research project is to design an adaptive battery management system (BMS) to enable efficient integration of bifacial photovoltaic (PV) modules and Gallium Nitride (GaN) power converters at irradiance levels of 200-1000 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The performance is compared with conventional monofacial PV-battery system. Set 1 comprises 10 samples of conventional monofacial PV-battery systems and set 2 comprises 10 samples of Adaptive Bifacial PV-GaN BMS. The power value is 80% and the threshold is 0.05 with a confidence interval of 95%. The results indicate that the Adaptive BMS has a higher energy yield than the conventional BMS. Energy yield of the adaptive BMS is 195.3 ± 5.8 Wh and energy yield of the conventional BMS is 159.0 ± 6.1 Wh. Optimal irradiance for maximum efficiency of the adaptive BMS is 1000 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> with a significance of p < 0.001 via t-test. Battery current reduced from 6.87 ± 0.32 A to 5.38 ± 0.28 A, power loss from 100% to 72%, and capacity retention from 83% to 88%. Efficiency of the system varies from 94.6% to 98.1% with SOC variation <2%. From the experimental results, it can be concluded that the adaptive BMS has superior performance to the conventional monofacial PV-silicon BMS.

title: NeuroTwin: Multimodal Cognitive Fatigue Estimation using Blink Dynamics and Keystroke Behaviour
authors: Varshini BM, V Shriya, Kanchana M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Cognitive fatigue builds up gradually during long periods of computer use. This condition can quietly decrease attention, response speed, and coordination. It is important to identify this state early, but many current systems rely on only one type of signal, limiting their reliability in different situations. In this study, we propose NeuroTwin, a framework that combines eye-blink behavior and typing patterns to estimate cognitive fatigue in a non-intrusive way. The visual part tracks eyelid movement to derive features like blink duration and eye closure patterns. The behavioral part looks at variations in keystroke timing. Since typing speed varies from person to person, we normalize the data to reduce individual bias. Each set of features is modeled separately, and their outputs are combined to create a unified cognitive index that reflects the user’s state over time. We include a smoothing step to prevent abrupt changes during real-time monitoring. Experimental evaluation shows that using both visual and behavioral cues provides more stable and accurate predictions than relying on just one source. The results suggest that combining complementary signals can be a practical way to monitor fatigue continuously in everyday human-computer interaction.

title: Brain Abnormality Detection using Radiomics and Attention-based Segmentation
authors: Sunil Kumar Talluri, N.Sunanda, Sri Surya Praneeth Bonthu, Krishna Thanmai Danda, Sai Preetham Reddy Donapati, Sathvik Konda
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The utilization of deep learning and radiomics to illustrate regional pathogenetic abnormalities and clustering for the brain has been an important focus in the imaging medicine arenas in the last few years. In this paper we propose a deep ensemble-based system that can integrate radiomics features and segmentation-based attention for accurate and robust detection of abnormal brain structures. The input takes the full strength of convolutional neural networks (CNN) to detect high-level structures and shapes in given brain MRI images, as well as higher resolution, and even more substantial segmentation models such as UNet and SegNet which might be useful to locate regions of interest. With attention mechanisms, the model will be able to concentrate more on the regions of interest resulting in better classification as well as localization. They trained it using a database of brain scans, marked with abnormal areas, and designed experiments which showed it is far superior to other traditional approaches. The suggested method seems to be one of the possible solutions to the automated detection of brain irregularities, which could be applied in clinical practice (diagnostics) and planning individual treatment plans.

title: Sustainable Agriculture for Crop Prediction Using Deep Learning Parameter
authors: Sankara Rao Allada, Divya Sarabudla, K. Kishore Kumar, Banoth. Janaki, Chitri Rami Naidu, Amit Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Accurate crop forecasting is vital for increasing output and process massive data sets has recently ushered in a new era of sustainability in agriculture, which is heavily reliant on improved accuracy in crop production prediction. The techniques environmental factors and soil types. The goal of this machine rely on a variety of data sets gathered from various sources, such as learning project is to develop a model that can determine the optimal aerial photography, weather and soil sensors, records of farm. In order to identify important variables, we apply feature selection methods to a dataset that contains 22 crop classes. To improve sample precision correlations in the data, allowing for finer-grained and more accurate while deleting extra features, feature selection techniques like filter, prediction models. Machine learning models' attractiveness lies in wrapper, and embedding approaches are used.The measures used for their capacity to learn and grow over time. If new data were available, training and testing classifiers include F1 score, accuracy, precision, models might be fine-tuned to ensure high accuracy over the long and decision trees, random forests, support vector machines (SVMs), term by adjusting their forecasts. They are able to respond and boosting approaches (XGBoost). With a 99% success rate, interactively and quickly to the ever-changing agricultural. In response to input parameters, the algorithm returns a prediction of the most likely and practically viable crop for the farmer. Therefore, this study contributes to precision agriculture by enhancing sustainable agriculture through better soil utilization and resource management decisions

title: Web-Enabled Affective Speech Analytics System for Real-World Human Interaction
authors: Nagendra Kumar V V, Kavya Seelam, Seelam Ajay Kumar Reddy, Sai Krishna Sandilya Avadhanam, Nayakula Roopesh Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Speech emotion recognition plays a vital role in human–computer interaction (HCI) by enabling machines to understand and respond to human emotions effectively. This study proposes a deep learning-based approach for classifying emotions from speech signals using the CREMA-D dataset, which includes six emotional categories: anger, disgust, fear, happiness, neutral, and sadness. The system incorporates comprehensive preprocessing and feature extraction techniques, including Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC), Zero Crossing Rate (ZCR), and Root Mean Square Energy (RMSE), along with data augmentation methods such as noise injection, time stretching, pitch shifting, and time shifting to enhance model robustness. Multiple machine learning and deep learning models, including KNN, SVM, DNN, and CNN, were trained and evaluated. Among these, the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) achieved the best performance, with an accuracy of 97.35%, precision of 97.35%, and recall of 97.36%. Additionally, a Flask-based web application was developed to enable real-time emotion prediction from recorded or uploaded audio, providing visual outputs such as waveform and spectrogram representations. The results demonstrate that CNN-based models significantly improve emotion recognition accuracy and are highly effective for real-world speech-based human–computer interaction applications.

title: SwiftLedger: A Hybrid 3 Layer Decentralized Payments System
authors: Ashirwad Gupta, Nikhil Yadav, Raj Shekhar Yadav, Pranjal Jain, Anupama Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Decentralized payment systems have demonstrated robust security guarantees, they still face challenges related to user-facing complexity and throughput constraints. While current Layer-2 (L2) Payment Channel Networks (PCNs) achieve higher performance at the expense of an increased operational burden on end users, public blockchains are still limited by consensus overhead. This paper introduces SwiftLedger, a hybrid decentralized payment architecture that combines: (i) a high- performance Layer-2 scaling layer built on off-chain channels; (ii) a Layer-1 settlement layer that offers security and finality; and (iii) a unique Layer-3 abstraction layer that automates routing, channel management, and liquidity provisioning. The design allows for customer experiences that match the already successful centralized payment systems by separating protocol logic and the user experience. The approach in this paper is to provide a hybrid evaluation framework that combines user metrics (e.g., how long it takes to complete a transaction) and technical metrics (e.g., transactions per second (TPS), transaction latency, transaction fees). Through an analysis comparing the established L2 wallet providers with SwiftLedger, it is evident that SwiftLedger supports transaction rate upwards of 10K TPS with confirmation delay of less than 1 second while at the same time reducing user effort significantly. The findings demonstrate that decentralized payments can achieve both scalability and usability through architectural abstraction.

title: Optimized Diabetes Prediction Using a Combined Random Forest–AdaBoost Classifier
authors: Pulivarthi Bindumadhavi, Selvanathan N, S J Vivekanandan, Seeniappan Kaliappan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Diabetes is a long-term metabolic condition associated with high glucose in the bloodstream, and this raises the chances of encountering serious complications like heart disease, stroke, and kidney failure. Although research has been conducted extensively, developing credible datasets and predictive models that are highly accurate in predicting early diagnosis remains a challenge.The hybrid ensemble of the Random Forest (RF) and AdaBoost are suggested in the proposed study to improve the precision of diabetes prediction. RF is efficient in feature selection and managing large volume of clinical data (high-dimensional) and AdaBoost reinforces the entire classifier by reducing and increasing errors and weak learners respectively. They are combined in a hybrid classification framework that is stable, efficient, and high-performing.The test data indicates that RF + AdaBoost model is better than the classical machine learning classifiers, such as Support Vector Machines (SVM) and Logistic Regression (LR) in accuracy, precision, and calculation efficiency.This hybrid ensemble model has a great potential to be deployed in the context of the early disease diagnosis and preventative healthcare environment, providing great assistance to clinicians and researchers who strive to enhance diabetes risk assessment, management, and treatment plans.

title: Electrical Machine Load Estimation Based on Thermal Imaging and Deep Learning Approach
authors: Abrar Ali Al-Kenani, Hilal Abbood Al-Libawy
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Estimating the load on an electric motor is essential for determining the reliability and energy efficiency of the unit. Previous load estimation approaches which untangle the complexities of the electrical parameters i.e. current, voltage, power factor, have encountered challenges ranging from noise interferences, complicated measuring equipment, and varying environmental factors. The current study aims to tackle these challenges using deep learning in combination with infrared thermography (IRT) to provide contactless load estimation. Thermal imaging provides unique diagnostic information by revealing distinct patterns in temperature of the motor surface. Previous work utilizing real time heat patterning (IRT) along with machine learning in the motor's electrical parameters have focused solely on detection of faults and therefore load analysis and classification in relation to varying parameters for the motor have been largely unexplored. The current study aimed to address this by conducting experiments with a single DC motor under eight different load conditions. Thermal images and electrical parameters for each load were recorded, and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) were trained on the electrical parameters of the samples (an estimate, although little, was given to the thermal images). The proposed model achieved 99 % accuracy, with an F1 score near 1 indicating a strong ability to generalize. Following data augmentation, the complete dataset consisted of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 8, 7 6 8}$</tex> thermal images pertaining to the DC motor. These thermal images were utilized for training, validating, and testing the model. The high level of accuracy achieved in the study confirms the ability of CNN and IRT to accurately estimate the load of an industrial machine in a more operationally viable timeframe.

title: Analysis of Machine Learning Algorithms for Electrical Fault Detection in Photovoltaic Panels
authors: Karuna Gangaram Choudhari, Saba Fatima
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The usage of Solar energy has increased recently because of its sustainable, cost-efficient, low-maintenance, and pollution-free nature. However, the efficiency of the solar farms is limited due to various mechanical and electrical faults in the solar panels. This paper presents an analysis of an automatic solar panel fault detection system using machine learning (ML) techniques, including K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Naïve Bayes (NB), Random Forest (RF), and Decision Tree (DT). The classifiers use various electrical parameters, including string current, current range, DC voltage, DC power, temperature, radiation, and fault resistance. The decision tree classifier achieves recall of 95.83%, precision of 95%, F1-score of 94.94%, and accuracy of 95% for four-class fault detection.

title: Estimating Depth in Omnidirectional Images for a Mixed Reality Remote Teaching Support System
authors: Ryotaro Hoshi, Masaki Ishii, Teppei Fukuda, Hidekatsu Ito, Kohji Dohsaka
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: In the agricultural field, there is a demand for more efficient learning methods that use remote instructions. However, conventional techniques are not necessarily suitable for learning approaches that involve interactions with the surrounding environment. In this study, we propose a remote instruction support system that uses panoramic images with a mixed reality (MR) representation to facilitate smooth communication by allowing users to experience an immersive virtual learning environment that replicates a real worksite with instructional messages or indicators overlaid on an MR display as spatial graphics. The proposed approach is designed to help experienced agricultural workers train new workers in a telepresence setting suitable for specific local areas.

title: TABSUNet: Task-Aware Band Selection for Lightweight Hyperspectral Road Scene Segmentation
authors: Nguyen Hai Duong, Daisuke Miyazaki, Chao Wang
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Hyperspectral imaging (HSI) offers fine-grained spectral cues for road-scene understanding in autonomous driving. However, the high spectral dimensionality of HSI introduces substantial computational overhead, posing challenges for real-time, on-device deployment. We propose TABS-UNet, a lightweight segmentation framework that integrates task-aware band selection with compact spatial modeling. Specifically, a Gumbel-Softmax-based selector dynamically identifies the top-<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex> most task-relevant spectral bands from a 25-channel input, enabling early-stage pruning of redundant spectral information. The reduced spectral cube is processed by a U-Net backbone enhanced with a bottleneck spatial attention module to preserve critical scene structures under tight computational budgets. Evaluations on a real-world automotive on-chip HSI dataset demonstrate that TABS-UNet achieves a superior accuracy–efficiency trade-off compared to both full-spectrum and fixed-band baselines, with up to 95% reduction in FLOPs and parameter count. Our results highlight the promise of task-adaptive spectral reduction for efficient HSI perception in intelligent transportation systems.

title: Design and Analysis of a Low-Energy Ternary Half-Adder Using GNRFET-Based Unary Operators
authors: S. Mubeena Tasneen, Y. Mallikarjuna Rao, Shaik Javid Basha, C. Venkataiah, Erfan Abbasian, Ashish Sachdeva
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Ultra low-power operation is essential for enabling energy harvesting techniques allowing the embedded systems to become self-sustaining and independent of grid power. A key strategy for reducing the critical power consumption in modern embedded design is the adoption of multi-valued logic (MVL). The MVL enables the data compression on physical wires, minimizes interconnections complexity and achieves a lower power consumption over the binary logic. Leveraging the power reducing potential of MVL, this paper introduces a ternary half-adder circuit that achieves reduced power consumption that directly addressing the challenge of maintaining long battery life in embedded and IoT systems. The proposed half adder utilizes dual supply voltage (i.e., 0.5V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf> and V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf>, respectively) and unary operators to improve delay, power and power delay product (PDP). The presented unary operators and half adder circuits are designed and simulated in HSPICE tool to obtain transient responses. The performances in terms of GNRFET count, delay, power and PDP are analyzed for presented adder circuits and compared to conventional works. The presented half adder showed the performance improvements on average up to 86.99% over existing works.

title: Analysis and Processing of Non-Contact Multimodal Physiological Signals for Sleep Monitoring
authors: Jinfeng Huang, Xin'an Wang, Jinpeng Shen
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics

title: Causality-aware Dual Graph Approach for Multi-cloud Networking Forecasting
authors: Yongcan Luo, Jiahao Zheng, Shuxin Qin, Tianyi Chen, Dapeng Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Multi-cloud networking enables enterprises to orchestrate applications across heterogeneous cloud platforms, offering improved scalability, resilience, and cost-effectiveness. However, complex interactions among distributed cloud resources introduce intricate interdependencies, particularly through inter-VM traffic flows and shared network pathways. Current forecasting methods for system metrics often overlook these fine-grained traffic dynamics and the causal relationships inherent in multi-cloud environments, failing to capture how resource usage in one VM can influence others. In this paper, we propose a causality-aware dual-graph neural network designed explicitly for multivariate system metric forecasting in multi-cloud settings. Our framework employs an adaptive dual-graph architecture to simultaneously model spatial dependencies among metrics across different VMs and temporal dependencies within individual VM metrics. To explicitly encode directional causal relationships, we construct a Transfer Entropy-based causality matrix optimized through Bayesian search. A causally guided fusion module integrates these representations using contrastive and triplet learning, aligning latent spaces to emphasize causality-aware structures. Extensive experiments on real-world multi-cloud traces demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms strong spatio-temporal and Transformer-based forecasting baselines, achieving relative error reductions of at least 8% (across MAE/RMSE/MAPE) in the 15-VM setting.

title: Physics-Constrained Pose Refinement of Stacked Objects via a Hierarchical Support Graph
authors: Daohui Liu, Chen Sun, Zheng Gong, Jiong Yang, Xingjian Liu, Yu Sun
abstract: Physics-constrained refinement is a technique for correcting inaccurate 6-D pose estimates. A key challenge, however, is to identify which estimates to refine when a physically implausible state, such as inter-penetration, occurs between multiple objects. This is because the implausible state may stem from a single inaccurate estimate or from all of them. To resolve the ambiguity of which pose to refine, existing methods anchor the process to a single, predefined support, such as a tabletop. However, this method does not apply to stacked scenarios, where objects lack direct contact with this predefined support. To address this limitation, we introduce a method that infers physical supports for each object by constructing a hierarchical support graph. Specifically, this method first validates pose estimates against a primary support, such as a tabletop, and then uses the validated estimates as second-level supports for objects stacked on them. Based on the support relations defined in this graph, a joint cost function is formulated to drive the joint refinement of all estimated poses to physically plausible states. To prevent errors in the hierarchy from propagating, a visual consistency term is included in the cost function, allowing the refined poses to remain grounded in visual information. Experimental results confirmed that our approach is capable of refining pose estimates in various stacked scenes, with its performance validated on the T-LESS benchmark and a custom dataset.

title: Robust Manipulation of Deformable Linear Objects_supp1-3692099.mp4
authors: Jimmy Envall
abstract: Robotic manipulation is a fundamental challenge in the pursuit of automating household tasks and advancing robotic manufacturing. Manipulation planning for deformable objects is particularly challenging due to the associated large action spaces and complex dynamics. For deformable objects, some actions are sensitive to noise in the model, which can significantly degrade the accuracy of predicted trajectories. In this letter, we present a motion planning algorithm for arranging deformable linear objects (DLOs) into user-provided goal configurations. Using a novel problem formulation as well as a combination of sampling and gradient based optimization, the algorithm finds sequences of grasps and control inputs that are robust. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the new algorithm using numerical experiments and manipulation examples both in simulation and in real world environments.

title: Dual-Port DC Wind Turbines Based Offshore Wind Power Integration System Embedded With Power Balance and Fault Ride-Through Capability
authors: Mingrui Yang, Wang Xiang, Jinyu Wen
abstract: In offshore wind farm (OWF) integration systems, emerging DC collection and transmission schemes (all-DC) necessitate the series-parallel connection of DC wind turbines (WTs) to boost the DC collection voltage. However, power imbalance among DC WTs impairs the overall efficiency of the integration system. To address the inherent limitations of the all-DC approach, this paper proposes a dual-port DC wind turbine-based DC collection and transmission topology for OWF integration. Where the DC ports of each DC WT are connected in series to elevate the DC collection voltage, while the AC ports of adjacent DC WTs are connected to decouple the power of DC WTs. Firstly, the topology, operating principle and control strategy of the all-DC integration system incorporating the proposed DC WT are introduced, and the power balance mechanism is analyzed. Then, a comprehensive fault ride-through (FRT) method is proposed to handle various fault types. Furthermore, the parameter design method and overall evaluation of the proposed integration system are conducted. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed all-DC integration system under different operation scenarios is validated in PSCAD/EMTDC.

title: System Design and Analysis of 2.5 MW Flywheel Energy Storage System
authors: Qiang Fan, Xiankui Wen, Yi Yang, Lingrong Pang, BoJun Ai, Huayang Ye, Xinyi Fu, Zhengjun Shi, Xiaoyin Zhang
abstract: With the increasing proportion of renewable energy connected to the grid and the increasing demand for grid frequency modulation, the energy storage technology with high power, fast response and long life has become the key to support the stable operation of smart grid. Flywheel energy storage system has shown great potential in the field of power grid frequency regulation due to its advantages of high power density, fast response, no chemical pollution and long cycle life. At the same time, the introduction of magnetic bearing technology provides a feasible path for the realization of high-speed, efficient and low-loss flywheel energy storage system. In this article, a 2.5MW flywheel energy storage system is designed for the demand of grid-level high-power energy storage, and the overall system parameters design, magnetic bearing structure optimization, Hybrid Magnetic Bearing (HMB) modeling and suspension force analysis are mainly carried out. The designed structure is verified by electromagnetic simulation and finite element analysis of rotor modal stress, and the rationality of the designed structure is proved.

title: Adaptive Dividing Factor and Reference Voltage Overmodulation Strategy for PMSM Field-Weakening Control
authors: Ziyu Wang, Xiaolin Wang, Xucong Bao
abstract: Overmodulation (OVM) techniques improve dc-link voltage utilization, extending the speed-torque envelope in permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) field-weakening (FW) operation. Adaptive OVM techniques are widely studied to mitigate resulting elevated current harmonics in the OVM region. However, deep OVM operation significantly exacerbates harmonic effects on the control loop, complicating harmonic suppression. This article investigates distortion amplification phenomena in the deep OVM region, revealing sharp increases in FW reference voltage ripple and current harmonics with modulation index (MI). To suppress such amplification, we propose an OVM algorithm featuring real-time adaptive control of both the dividing factor kOVM and FW reference voltage. This approach intrinsically reduces peak phase voltage THD in deep OVM by dynamically regulating reference voltage ripple, thereby attenuating current harmonics. Experimental comparisons with conventional and adaptive OVM methods confirm that the proposed algorithm substantially reduces current THD while maintaining fundamental current amplitude and voltage utilization.

title: Joint Trajectory-Resource Optimization for UAV Semantic Communication Systems
authors: Yue Sun, Shuang Du, Haibo Mei, Qi Wang, Chuanjie Liu, Huan Ye
abstract: Nowadays, semantic communication (SC) has attracted intensive attention. By adopting an “Understand-before-transmit” approach supported by Deep Learning (DL), SC is capable of only sending semantic information (SI) rather than raw data, thereby conserving data transmission and communication costs without any degradation in performance. This makes SC particularly suitable for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) communications, given that UAVs' limitations in size, weight, and power (SWaP). The SWaP constraint limits UAVs' airborne data collection capabilities, and affects the efficiency of large file transmissions, such as images and videos. This work explores the application of SC in UAV communications and proposes a UAV Semantic Communication (UAV-SC) system designed to expedite the wireless image transmission tasks of UAVs. Utilizing a Joint Source-Channel Coding (JSCC) method, we achieve image transmission based on SC between the UAV and the ground vehicles (GV). Subsequently, we develop a joint optimization algorithm for UAV trajectory and resource allocation to identify the near-optimal network configuration for UAV-SC, thereby enhancing overall communication performance. According to the numerical results from simulation, the joint algorithm supports the UAV-SC system working in an energy-efficient way and outperforms benchmark solutions.

title: Automated Spatial-Temporal Graph Neural Network Search for Skeleton-based Human Action Recognition on Edge Devices
authors: Xiuwen Li, Weiwei Fang, Liang Yue, Fanjie Shi, Neal N. Xiong
abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have attracted significant attention in human action recognition (HAR) tasks due to their ability to model spatial-temporal relationships between body joints in skeletal graphs. However, prior solutions typically suffer from high computational overhead and significant inference latency, particularly on resource-constrained platforms like edge devices. To mitigate this, we propose Edge-STGNN, a speed-optimized neural architecture search (NAS) framework, for the automated design of spatial-temporal GNNs (STGNNs) tailored for action recognition on edge devices. Specifically, Edge-STGNN defines a search space that includes diverse temporal convolutional layers, attention mechanisms, and network depth options. Based on this search space, it constructs the supernet using a single-path training approach. Subsequently, an evolutionary algorithm-based search strategy is applied to identify optimal architectures. Furthermore, Edge-STGNN integrates an efficient speed predictor to reduce evaluation time, and employs a small-scale proxy dataset to lower search costs. Comprehensive experiments on multiple datasets and edge devices demonstrate that Edge-STGNN effectively identifies architectures with fewer parameters, reduced computational complexity, and faster inference speeds, while preserving acceptable recognition accuracy. Additionally, by incorporating speed constraints, Edge-STGNN enables a flexible trade-off between accuracy and speed tailored to specific application demands. For example, on the NTU RGB+D 60 xview60 benchmark, compared to all baseline methods with reported speeds, Edge-STGNN achieves a 2.4× to 9.4× inference speedup while maintaining accuracy within a ±1% margin.

title: Probability of Positioning Failure for UAVs in Multiple Authorized European Airspace Regions
authors: Sebastian Ciuban, Chengyu Yin, Peter J.G. Teunissen, Christian C.J.M. Tiberius
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) support, or are planned to support, a wide range of operations, including emergency response, environmental research, urban air mobility, and (commercial) air transportation, where positioning safety is paramount. This contribution presents a framework for assessing positioning safety of UAVs by computing the probability of positioning failure, rather than conservative upper bounds, while accounting for time-varying positioning models. In contrast to existing studies, we (i) explicitly adopt UAV safety regions and target probability of positioning failure requirements as specified by the European Union Agency for the Space Programme (EUSPA) for Specific Assurance and Integrity Levels (SAIL) 3 (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-4}$</tex-math></inline-formula>/hour) and 4 (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-5}$</tex-math></inline-formula>/hour), and (ii) use representative positioning models for the UAV GPS receiver which are consistent with Technical Standard Order (TSO) specifications. For the computation of the probability of positioning failure, we use a method based on rare event simulation techniques while accounting for the dependence between parameter estimation and statistical hypothesis testing. We apply the framework to simulation-based positioning safety analysis across authorized European airspace regions in eight countries using real GPS satellite orbit data. The probability of positioning failure is computed over a 24-hour period, then connected to per-hour requirements using one-hour moving averages, and compared against the EUSPA SAIL 3 and 4 requirements. The time-dependent analysis further reports best-case and worst-case probabilities of positioning failure and quantifies sensitivity to key hypothesis-testing design parameters, such as the level of significance. This analysis can help UAV operators and regulators verify compliance with EUSPA safety standards, supporting management of safe UAV operations.

title: Area-Efficient Low-Power Self-Biased Three-Stage OTA Fabricated in 0.6- μ m IGZO-on-Plastic Technology
authors: Muhammad Omer Shah, Marco Privitera, Muhammad Zahid Naveed, Andrea Ballo, Alfio Dario Grasso, Gianluca Giustolisi, Salvatore Pennisi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This work presents a high-efficiency three-stage operational transconductance amplifier (OTA) fabricated in a commercial 0.6-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m Indium Gallium Zinc Oxide (IGZO) thin-film transistor (TFT) technology on a flexible plastic substrate. The architecture leverages a fully self-biased differential input stage combined with a reverse nested Miller compensation with nulling resistor scheme to ensure robust stability under up to 40-pF loads. Operating from a 3-V supply, the OTA consumes only <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$60~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W while driving a 40-pF load, achieving a measured DC gain of 41.2 dB, a unity-gain bandwidth of 200 kHz, and a phase margin of 60.7°. Time-domain measurements demonstrate a 1%-settling time of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$21~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s and a slew rate of 94 V/ms, with an output swing exceeding 83%. The self-biasing scheme and compensation strategy provide high resilience against process, voltage, and multi-die variations. This design occupies an active area of only 0.042 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, and achieves a power consumption of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$60~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W with a small-signal Figure of Merit of 133.3 kHz<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot $</tex-math> </inline-formula>pF/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W, establishing a new performance benchmark for plastic electronics.

title: Open-Vocabulary SAM3D: Towards Training-free Open-Vocabulary 3D Scene Understanding_supp1-3680794.mp4
authors: Yong Liu
abstract: Open-vocabulary 3D scene understanding presents a significant challenge in the field. Recent works have sought to transfer knowledge embedded in vision-language models from 2D to 3D domains. However, these approaches often require prior knowledge from specific 3D scene datasets, limiting their applicability in open-world scenarios. The Segment Anything Model (SAM) has demonstrated remarkable zero-shot segmentation capabilities, prompting us to investigate its potential for comprehending 3D scenes without training. In this paper, we introduce OV-SAM3D, a training-free method that contains a universal framework for understanding open-vocabulary 3D scenes. This framework is designed to perform understanding tasks for any 3D scene without requiring prior knowledge of the scene. Specifically, our method is composed of two key sub-modules: First, we initiate the process by generating superpoints as the initial 3D prompts and refine these prompts using segment masks derived from SAM. Moreover, we then integrate a specially designed overlapping score table with open tags from the Recognize Anything Model (RAM) to produce final 3D instances with open-world labels. Empirical evaluations on the ScanNet200 and nuScenes datasets demonstrate that our approach surpasses existing open-vocabulary methods in unknown open-world environments. Code is available at https://github.com/HanchenTai/OV-SAM3D.git.

title: a Novel Lyapunov-Based Online Learning Predictive Control Method for Power Converters Via Residual Network
authors: Chenghao Liu, Jien Ma, Xiangyu Wang, Xing Liu, Lin Qiu, Jose Rodriguez, Youtong Fang
abstract: This paper focuses on introducing the online learning mechanism and residual network into the finite control-set model predictive control (FCS-MPC) framework to enhance robustness. More precisely, a novel Lyapunov-based online learning pipeline is established within the proposed framework. It enables the approximation of streaming input/output data without relying on physical system information or auxiliary information from other control methods, and achieves online learning with provable stability. This design not only mitigates the performance degradation caused by parameter variations or unmodeled dynamics, but also lays a foundation for more reliable artificial intelligence-based solutions in power converter control. Furthermore, an optimal switching state selection strategy is designed based on the residual network. It helps mitigate unnecessary switching operations of semiconductors and balance the capacitor voltage of neutral-point-clamped converter, while avoiding adjusting the weighting factors with different physical magnitudes in the conventional cost function. In summary, an intelligent online residual network predictive control (ORNPC) paradigm for power converters is proposed, and its superior performance has been validated through simulations and experiments on a digital signal processor (DSP) platform.

title: Understanding Variable Length Arrays In The C Language
authors: Zhiwei Sun, Wenge Rong, Nanxiang Jiang, Yuxi Bai, Jing Li, Zhang Xiong
abstract: The C language has been a mainstream programming language and widely used since its inception. Arrays represent one of the most fundamental constructs in C, yet they have long been considered a challenging topic to fully master. It has been observed that significant ambiguities persist in the interpretation of variable length arrays (VLAs). Key challenges include the precise understanding of the VLA definition, particularly the distinction between integer constants and integer constant expressions, as well as the behavioral differences between VLAs and ordinary arrays when used as operands in the sizeof operator. Through practical examples, this paper illustrates the underlying mechanisms of VLAs and underscores the importance of avoiding side effects in their use. We hope that this work will offer clearer guidance for understanding principles related to VLAs and contribute to more accurate and effective usage.
