title: Recursive Bounds and Explicit Constructions for Error Coefficients of Optimal Linear Codes
authors: Tingting Tong, Shitao Li, Sihuang Hu
abstract: The error coefficient of a linear code, defined as the number of minimum-weight codewords, plays a central role in evaluating the performance of the code. In this paper, we establish two recursive bounds on the minimum possible error coefficient among optimal linear codes with prescribed parameters. We prove that these bounds are tight in infinitely many cases by constructing two explicit infinite families of optimal linear codes that attain them with equality, and we further show that MDS codes also meet one of the proposed bounds with equality. Beyond the recursive-bound framework, we determine the minimum possible error coefficient for three explicit families of optimal linear codes: two families arising from simplex codes and one family associated with MacDonald codes. Moreover, employing tools from combinatorial design theory, we solve a problem proposed by Guan et al. [12] on the eventual constancy of the minimum error coefficient of optimal codes.

title: Antiferromagnetic Néel Vector Readout in Co/MgO/FeMn/Co Tunnel Junction For Artificial Synapse Application: An Ab Initio DFT–NEGF Study
authors: Prayash Dutta, Greg Carman, Tanmoy Pramanik, Sanjukta Bhanja
abstract: We present an ab initio quantum transport study for electrical readout of the Nèel vector orientation in an antiferromagnetic FeMn layer embedded in a Co/MgO/FeMn/Co tunnel junction. The transport properties are investigated using density functional theory combined with nonequilibrium Green’s function (DFT–NEGF) calculations to analyze symmetry-dependent tunneling across the MgO barrier. Transport through the MgO barrier is controlled by Δ1 symmetry filtering, and the direction of the FeMn N´eel vector affects how easily electrons can pass. The ideal Co/MgO/FeMn/Co junction exhibits a tunneling magnetoresistance ratio (TMR) between FeMn aligned along ±X and ±Z of 8729%, which decreases to approximately 526% under realistic process variations such as interfacial oxidation. Analysis of intermediate magnetic configurations corresponding to partial FeMn domain reorientation reveals distinct resistance levels suitable for synaptic weight representation. For a 80×40 nm2 device area, a resistance gap of 2.418 kΩ is obtained, corresponding to the condition where at least one-third of the FeMn Nèel vectors align along the X direction. These results demonstrate that symmetry-controlled transport in antiferromagnetic tunnel junctions enables reliable electrical readout and provides a promising pathway for robust spintronic synapses in neuromorphic computing architectures.

title: Talbot effect of non-coaxial Bessel beams
authors: Yizheng Yao, Siqin Yan, Xuhui Sun, Chenglong Wang, Zhenyu Zhao, Xiao Lei, Jiaxin Fu, Chao Zhang, Yiqiang Qin
abstract: In recent years, the superposition of nondiffracting beams has been employed to construct Talbot effects with novel propagation properties. Here we discover that non-coaxial Bessel beams can also be used to construct the Talbot effect, which is a more general case that encompasses the coaxial one. The realization of the Talbot effect is determined only by the longitudinal wave vectors (the components of wave vectors in the propagating direction), other parameters like the amplitude coefficients, mode orders and the spatial arrangement of these Bessel beams can be chosen arbitrarily, providing more degrees of freedom for light field construction. We also find that rotating self-imaging effect may occur at fractional Talbot distances under certain circumstances. The results are further verified by numerical simulations with multi-parameter control. These results considerably expand the scope of traditional Talbot imaging theory.

title: Ensuring Reliability of Autonomous Cars Through Standards: Status, Gaps, and Future Directions
authors: Muhammad Awais Javed, Sherali Zeadally
abstract: Autonomous cars are emerging as a key technology in modern transportation, with growing use in personal mobility and shared ride services. Their safe and reliable operation depends on well-defined standards. We review the current status of autonomous car standards, outlining the roles of major standardization bodies and classifying relevant standards into four main categories, namely those that cover safety, communication, cybersecurity, and data management. We identify reliability-related challenges such as regulatory differences, validation of artificial intelligence (AI)-based functions, inadequate simulation frameworks, evolving cybersecurity risks, and the lack of unified performance metrics. We also identify gaps in existing efforts and discuss research and policy directions needed to achieve global harmonization. The results will provide a clear reference for stakeholders working toward the safe, reliable, interoperable, and scalable deployment of autonomous cars.

title: A Non-Isolated Modified Multiport Bridgeless Zeta Converter for EV Charging with V2X Compatibility
authors: Vishvanath Murugesan, Venkadesan Arunachalam, Jojin Thomas, Allamsetty Hema Chander, Thangavel Subbaiyan
abstract: This article presents a non-isolated Modified Multiport Bridgeless (MMBL) Zeta Converter architecture for electric vehicle (EV) charging applications with Vehicle-to-Home/Load/Grid (V2X) compatibility. The proposed architecture accommodates dual-input operation, accepting both AC and DC sources as well as AC and DC loads. The converter facilitates bidirectional power flow and supports multiple operating modes, namely AC Grid-to-Vehicle (AC G2V), DC Grid-to-Vehicle (DC G2V), Vehicle-to-AC Grid/Load (V2AC-X), and Vehicle-to-DC Grid/Load (V2DC-X), ensuring flexible energy transfer between EV batteries and AC/DC grids. A comprehensive analysis of the converter’s operating principle and design considerations across all power flow scenarios is included, along with a brief explanation of the control strategy. The effectiveness of the proposed topology is validated through experimental evaluation conducted on a 400 W laboratory prototype. The results demonstrate a near-unity input power factor of 0.9909 and a source current total harmonic distortion (THD) of 4.34%, in line with the relevant power quality standards. The results confirm that the proposed MMBL Zeta converter offers an efficient, cost-effective, and reliable solution for low- to medium-power bidirectional EV charging applications with V2X functionality.

title: Low‑Sidelobe Series‑Fed Microstrip Antenna Array with Defected Ground Structure for Vehicular Communications
authors: Aradhana Singh Kaintura, Rajib Kumar Panigrahi
abstract: Series-fed microstrip antenna (MSA) arrays are widely used in integrated RF modules for vehicular communications due to their low feed-network loss and high radiation efficiency. However, simultaneously achieving wide bandwidth and low sidelobe level (SLL) in such arrays remains challenging, particularly when asymmetric patch elements are employed for bandwidth enhancement. This work presents a defected ground structure (DGS) integration approach for SLL suppression in series-fed linear MSA arrays without requiring the feed network modification or increasing antenna aperture. An analytical method is used to identify optimal DGS placement locations, followed by experimental validation using symmetric and asymmetric patch array case studies. Measurements demonstrate up to 6 dB SLL reduction in symmetric patch arrays and about 2 dB in wideband asymmetric patch arrays, while preserving gain, efficiency, and impedance bandwidth. The results confirm that DGS integration provides a practical solution for achieving low-interference antenna arrays suitable for compact RF front-end modules.

title: Robust MPC for Obstacle Avoidance With Tightened Dual Constraints and Reachability-Guided Planner
authors: Fang-Jiao Zhao, Yong-Feng Gao, Heng Luo, Xi-Ming Sun
abstract: Autonomous navigation in tight and cluttered spaces remains challenging due to two core issues. Disturbances and actuator saturation shrink the feasible set and may make real-time control infeasible, while conservative planning underuses the collision-free configuration space and can lead to planner deadlocks. To address these issues, this article proposes a robust model predictive control (RMPC) framework for three-wheeled omnidirectional mobile robots that couples tightened dual collision (DC) constraints with a reachability-guided planner (RGP). First, the DC constraints enforce separation between full-dimensional convex polytopes for the robot and obstacles, yielding a high-fidelity geometric model that reduces conservatism in collision avoidance. Combined with a constraint-tightening scheme, the RMPC-DC algorithm is developed to maintain safety margins for closed-loop control under bounded disturbances. A local controller and an explicit upper bound on admissible disturbances are further derived to guarantee recursive feasibility of the RMPC-DC algorithm and input-to-state stability of the closed-loop system. Second, the RGP avoids deadlocks by generating subgoals within a reachability circle rather than complete paths. Moreover, the RGP culls obstacles outside a forward obstacle detection sector before passing them to RMPC-DC to reduce computational complexity. Simulation and hardware experiments show that the proposed framework navigates narrow passages and sharp corners without deadlocks. It also achieves higher success rates, shorter solve times, and smoother paths than an MPC baseline with control barrier functions.

title: Networked Spatiotemporal Optimal Control and DC-PPO Reinforcement Learning for Combating Malware Spread in IoUT
authors: Guiyun Liu, Jipeng Zhu, Qinxue Li, Caixia Zhang, Wenming Jiang, Xiaojing Zhong, Zhongwei Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Facing the challenges of spatiotemporally coupled malware propagation and limited defense resources in the Internet of Underwater Things (IoUT), this paper proposes an integrated defense framework combining networked modeling, optimal control, and reinforcement learning. A networked spatiotemporal propagation model is established using the graph Laplacian, incorporating spatial heterogeneity to overcome the limitations of traditional ODE/PDE approaches. A regionalized optimal control framework is then derived, enabling dynamic, risk-based resource allocation. To address environmental uncertainties, a model-free Dual-Clip PPO (DC-PPO) algorithm is designed, which learns near-optimal control policies without a precise system model. Simulations show that our model captures realistic propagation dynamics, the control strategy significantly reduces infection rates and cost, and DC-PPO demonstrates superior generalization and robustness across various network topologies, achieving an error of less than 3% compared with that of model-based optimal control. This work provides a rigorous and practical intelligent control solution for IoUT malware defense.

title: Efficient Machine Learning Framework for Detecting e-Commerce Fraud Transactions
authors: Bhulakshmi Makkena
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The fast pace of e-commerce has also come with a high risk of fraudulent activities that require sophisticated detection systems that balances accuracy, efficiency, and flexibility. This analysis describes a machine-learning-based approach to fraudulent online purchases with the aid of the E-commerce Fraud Dataset. The methodology uses the data preprocessing by removing inconsistencies, feature selection, categorical encoding, and the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) to address the class imbalance. Two suggested models, Linear Regression (LiR) and Decision Tree (DT), were applied and tested in comparison to baseline classifier, i.e., Support Vector Machine (SVM) and k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN). The accuracy (Acc), precision (Prec), recall (Rec), F1-score as well as ROC analysis were used to determine performance. The experimental results indicate that LiR performed better with 96% Acc, 96% Prec, 96% Rec, and a 95% F1score whereas DT scored 89% Acc, 89% Prec, 89% Rec, and 89% F1score. Conversely, SVM and KNN had a lower accuracy of 68% and 50.8, respectively, which validates the high effectiveness of the proposed models. LiR model is robust, scalable and efficient to real time applications whereas DT is efficient in the interpretation aspect which is based on a rule-based structure. This study adds a valid and viable pipeline of detection of frauds, enhancing trust and safety in e-commerce structures.

title: A Unified Deep Learning and Web Application Framework for Automated Heart Disease Prediction and Dietary Guidance
authors: Lithin S, Pranav J, Rajeshkannan. S, G. D. Vignesh, Hasan Dhanish J, Aieshwarya R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Accurate predictions are essential for effective management of cardiovascular health. In this project, we propose a feature-specialized deep multi-branch neural network, or DMNN in short, would analyze clinical data by splitting features into specialized branches. Each branch learns different information on its own. The model relies on residual connections, Batch normalization and dropout have improved stability. and generalization. To make things more interpretable we apply SHAP values: They explain the model’s predictions clearly. We Also include an OCR module. This will allow users to upload scanned medical reports. It automatically extracts clinical data. That reduces manual entry and enhances usability. The entire system runs as a web application built with the MERN stack. Users can input data manually or upload files. It gives binary prediction for heart disease risk. In addition, it offers personalized dietary advice based on the result. Experimental results show the accuracy of the model. and reliability, the design is user-friendly too. All in all, it serves the purpose as a practical tool to assess and manage the risk of heart disease.

title: Latency-Aware YOLOv26 with EMA-Stabilized Training and a P2 Head for Potato Blight Detection
authors: Ajith Nagaraj, Parthasarathy Velusamy, Saravana Kumar Ganesan, Santhosh Rajendran, Manikandan Bose, Ranjithkumar Sakthivel
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Early detection of foliar diseases is critical for precision agriculture, yield monitoring, and crop protection in potato- based production systems. This work presents a YOLOv26 based framework for three-class potato disease classification and localization of Early Blight, Late Blight, and Healthy foliage in field acquired RGB imagery. The method leverages a curated Roboflow dataset of 7,172 images, resized to 416 × 416 and split into 80/10/10 train/validation/test partitions, and introduces several training and architecture level enhancements. System level modifications include an EMA of weights, cosine learning-rate scheduling with warmup, automatic mixed precision training, gradient clipping, and latency aware channel slimming under a real time constraint. Architecturally, a P2 small object head is added to better resolve fine, early-stage lesions, and geometry aware Intersection of Union (IoU) with focal style classification losses addresses class imbalance and inter class similarity. On the held-out test proposed detector achieves 98.98% Precision, 94.31% Recall, 96.59% F1-score, and strong mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95, while maintaining practical inference latency on an RTX-class GPU. Qualitative evaluation of the system demonstrated a high degree of robustness to varying degrees of background clutter and lighting conditions; however, it was noted that some smaller lesions (less than 20 pixels) and those which were significantly occluded remained a challenge for accurate identification. Overall, this study demonstrates the capability to use real time object detection systems as reliable signals for providing operational decision-making input to support both yield monitoring and crop protection activities.

title: Proposal of a Lateral Emergency Avoidance Simulator for Cooperative Strategy-Based Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ryuto Masuda, Yasuhiro Kawanishi, Kenji Mizutani
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: This study reports a successful learning case that achieved lateral emergency avoidance, which was difficult with conventional Advanced Driver Assistance Systems: ADAS (FCW, AEB). Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (RL) was conducted in an environment featuring slopes, curves, and narrow road using Unity ML-Agents. By manipulating sensor angles and reward design, the agents acquired lateral emergency avoidance behavior. Specifically, at a 45 degrees setting, optimizing both forward and lateral monitoring achieved a 100 percent success rate and 0 percent collision rate. Therefore, one of the design principles for next-generation ADAS should include proactive and cooperative hazard avoidance achieved through short-range sensors and reward-based design.

title: From Clicks to Conversions: Predicting Online Purchase Intent Using Supervised Machine Learning
authors: Samuel-Soma M. Ajibade, Evans Oteng, Muhammed Basheer Jasser, David Olayemi Alebiosu, Adedotun O. Adetunla, Olumide Simeon Ogunnusi, Almighty Cortezo Tabuena Tabuena, Olufunke Olupero Ajibade
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: The rise of the E-Commerce sector has transformed the brick-and-mortar businesses to a new paradigm by enabling businesses to gain access to a wider local and international market, reach out to diversified customer groups and allow effective sales and distribution channels for their products and services. Despite the benefits e-commerce brought, many businesses struggle to extract meaningful insights from users data to improve business strategies and increase customers' conversion rates. Hence, this study aims to analyze customers browsing behaviours to predict whether a user session could potentially lead to a successful purchase. A comparative analysis will be performed using the “Online Shoppers Purchasing Intention” dataset on Decision Tree, SVC, Random Forest and XGBoost to identify the best classifier in making predictions. The findings indicated that Random Forest and XGBoost had achieved F1-score of 0.66 and AUPR of 0.71 and 0.7, indicating a strong predictive balance and ability in identifying likely purchasers. PageValues, BounceRates, ProductRelatedDuration and ExitRates were found to be strong predictor variables. The findings are vital in helping businesses to design better business strategies that could offer promotions to the target group that has high probability in purchasing and hence increase conversion rates and business revenue.

title: Unified Intelligent Optimization and Sensing-Driven Coupling Framework for Rain Garden Filler Layer Material Design and Infiltration–Storage Performance
authors: Yang Wang, Jinfu Zuo, Fangwei Huang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The core challenge of this study lies in the multi-objective coupling conflict and long-term degradation between the material structure of the filler layer and its infiltration-storage performance in stormwater storage facilities. This paper proposes a Unified Intelligent Optimization and Sensing-Driven Coupling Framework (UIO-SDCF). This theory achieves synergistic optimization of material microstructure design, sensor fusion modeling, and dynamic performance control at the function space level. The framework constructs a high-dimensional state space using a multi-source sensing system, and achieves simultaneous identification of the hydraulic state and structural parameters of the filler layer through covariance adaptive Bayesian fusion and joint state-parameter estimation. Simultaneously, this research characterizes the uncertain dynamics of aquifer migration and pore evolution based on stochastic partial differential equations. At the structural design level, this paper constructs a multi-scale mapping model between particle scale, pore scale, and macroscopic hydraulic response. This model embeds parameters such as pore connectivity, specific surface area, pore size distribution, and pore tortuosity into the expression for unsaturated hydraulic conductivity. As a method for in-depth modeling, this study proposes a unified performance functional system encompassing infiltration efficiency, effective storage capacity, structural stability, and anti-clogging degradation performance. Based on this performance function space, this study establishes a multi-objective robust optimization model with perturbation uncertainty constraints. Furthermore, this paper introduces a structural adaptive regulation law to achieve performance maintenance and degradation suppression during long-term operation. In the experimental section, nine multi-scale composite filler schemes (M1–M9) were constructed on a 1.2 m × 0.8 m × 0.8 m rain garden physical test platform. Simultaneously, 132 comparative experiments were conducted under conditions including multi-intensity rainfall (30–120 mm·h⁻<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>), continuous pollution load (30 d), sensor noise amplification (1.5–2.5 times), model bias (±15%), and sudden heavy rainfall disturbance. The results show that the proposed framework improves the comprehensive performance functional value under steady-state conditions by approximately 14.1% compared to the physical constraint data fusion method. This result is approximately 31.2% higher than the rule-based empirical design method. Under dynamic rainfall conditions, its infiltration peak deviation and system response time are significantly reduced. In long-term clogging evolution experiments, its anti-clogging attenuation coefficient is reduced by approximately 48.6% compared to traditional methods. One-way ANOVA results show that the differences between different methods are highly significant (F = 19.47, p < 0.001).

authors: Sharelone Juliane Damole, Joemarie Guion, Kristine Joyce Ortiz
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics

title: Low-Complexity Phase-Shift Configuration in RIS-Aided Multiuser Cell-Free maMIMO Networks
authors: Zhihan Ren, Angela Doufexi, Mark A. Beach
abstract: This paper proposes a closed-form low-complexity method for RIS configuration in a multi-user cell-free massive MIMO (maMIMO) network. Leveraging the large-scale fading (LSF) distribution and spatial diversity of the distributed network architecture, the proposed method jointly enhances the end-to-end channel gain and the degrees of freedom for interference suppression. An LSF-based algorithm is introduced to maintain fairness among user equipment (UEs). We have analysed the proposed method under short-term and long-term RIS configuration schemes and different CSI acquisition strategies. A closed-form channel estimation error variance for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$b$ </tex-math></inline-formula>-bit digital RIS is derived, and the non-negligible impact of digital RIS on pilot contamination is quantified. To explore different trade-offs between spectral efficiency (SE) maximisation and fairness, we also propose an element-selection based RIS configuration method. To verify the performance, we derive three achievable SE equations for perfect CSI and imperfect CSI scenarios with a fully centralised cell-free maMIMO operation. Numerical results show that the proposed schemes outperform the baseline under three linear uplink combining methods and non-linear successive interference cancellation (SIC).

title: AI-Driven Hybrid Decision Support Framework for Heart Disease Prediction and Personalized Treatment Recommendation
authors: K.S.Ranjith, Appoji Gari Yasasree, Kancham Haritha, S MD Nizamuddin, M Dhanush Shivaji, Naveen Kumar Akula
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Heart sickness remains a main cause of global mortality, yet maximum present computational fashions are confined to binary prediction and lack clinically actionable steering or interpretability. To deal with these boundaries, this paper proposes HPER – Heart (Hybrid Ensemble ML Models for Predictions with Explainability and AI-Driven Recommendations) because the middle predictive set of rules within an AI-pushed hybrid selection help framework. HPER integrates a supervised hybrid ensemble with Generative AI to provide accurate cardiovascular illness prediction and custom designed healing hints. The predictive module leverages a hybrid ensemble of XGBoost, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Logistic Regression, and Support Vector Classifier, making sure robust overall performance at some point of numerous affected individual profiles. Dataset imbalance is mitigated the usage of SMOTE to decorate fairness and enhance sensitivity for minority classes. A complementary Generative AI module synthesizes individualized drug, life-style, and preventive pointers primarily based on clinical information. Explainable AI (XAI) strategies are incorporated to interpret model conduct, making sure transparency in medical choice-making. The framework is verified the usage of the UCI Heart Disease dataset and a private scientific dataset, demonstrating advanced accuracy, adaptability, and actual-international applicability. By combining excessive-precision prediction with interpretable outputs and actionable pointers, HPER – Heart bridges an essential gap in current studies and supports greater knowledgeable cardiovascular care.

title: Analysis on Small-Signal Stability for Hybrid System Integrating PMSG Wind Farm and Grid-Forming Energy Storage
authors: Bin Li, Jiayang Lamu, Yaoxia Du, Wenxiao Gao, Qingxi Liao, Jian Wu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: High penetration of wind turbine generators (WTGs) introduces volatility and stability challenges power systems. To address this, grid-forming energy storage systems (GFM-ESS) are integrated to smooth fluctuations and provide grid support, forming a wind-storage hybrid system. However, control-grid interactions can induce broadband oscillations. This paper concentrates on the small-signal stability evaluation of a combined system composed of a PMSG wind farm and a grid-forming energy storage unit. By adopting the negative resistance criterion and open-loop modal resonance approach, the impacts of controller parameters, grid-connection topologies, and PMSG capacity scale on system stability are fully investigated. It is found that unreasonable parameter settings will induce system instability. Meanwhile, GFM-ESS can enhance system stability under certain conditions, but more PMSG modules and series topological structures will remarkably aggravate the stability risk. The mutual verification of the two methods ensures the reliability of the results, laying a theoretical foundation for the stable planning and design of wind-storage combined systems.

title: Smart Break Recommendation System Based on Student Mental Fatigue and Task Type
authors: Swathi Vinesh, Rakshitha G, Skandapriya V, Bhoomika G M, Hemalatha J D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Digital fatigue has become a major concern in occupational health, as extended use of computers can lead to decreased productivity, physical discomfort, and cognitive issues. Traditional reminder systems are not personalized and do not consider individual variations in fatigue patterns and work environments. This paper introduces an intelligent break recommendation system that uses machine learning for detecting fatigue and reinforcement learning for customizing break schedules. The proposed system continuously adjusts to user behavior by passively monitoring keystroke dynamics, mouse movements, and window activity. It consists of four main components: real-time data acquisition through behavioral monitoring, task classification using a Random Forest model, fatigue detection with an XGBoost classifier, and an Actor-Critic reinforcement learning agent that optimizes break recommendations based on user feedback. Experimental results show task classification accuracy between 85–92% and fatigue detection accuracy between 78– 86%, with the reinforcement learning agent effectively tailoring recommendations using a reward function weighted by fatigue reduction (50%), productivity improvements (30%), and user compliance (20%). The system operates efficiently (<2% CPU, ∼150 MB RAM) while ensuring privacy through fully local data processing. This study confirms the feasibility of integrating supervised and reinforcement learning for personalized digital well-being interventions, providing an adaptive alternative to static break scheduling systems. Future developments will include physiological sensing and long-term evaluation to enhance robustness and generalization.

title: Comparison of Analysis Using Acquired Machine Data for Machine and Tool Life Optimization
authors: ZHE WEI LEE, HUI EN LEE, HONG SIANG CHUA, Chie Siang Lee, Almon Wei-Yen Chai
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: In the field of Industry 4.0 and smart manufacturing, intelligent Tool Condition Monitoring (TCM) is essential for enabling predictive maintenance and ensuring product quality in machining operations. This research presents the development of a data-driven TCM system capable of acquiring and analyzing machine data for tool life optimization. The study utilizes hybrid data acquisition architecture, integrating internal machine data (Spindle Load) with external sensing technologies, including vibration sensors and a threephase smart energy meter, combined via an (internet of things) IoT gateway and Node-Red platform. Experimental testbeds were conducted on a FANUC ROBODRILL <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\alpha}$</tex>-D21MiB Plus equipped with 5-Axis Rotary Table using SAE 1020 carbon steel to investigate tool wear progression under Conventional and High-Speed Machining (HSM) strategies. Direct offline measurements were performed using a Vision Measuring Machine (VMM) to categorize tool conditions for preexperiment and post-experiment. Three Machine Learning classification models were evaluated: Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Support Vector Machine (SVM). The Random Forest classifier achieved the highest reliability with an accuracy of 73.82 %, effectively distinguishing between operational modes and wear states. The poor performance of SVM model (34.70 %) highlighted the high non-linearity and scatter in the dataset.

title: Explainable AI for Predicting and Understanding Teen Cyber Risks
authors: Alma Mae B. Auxtero, Ariel Roy L. Reyes
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Adolescents nowadays have increasingly engaged with complex digital environments. This phenomenon resulted in heightened exposure to cyber risks such as cyberbullying, online scams, and privacy breaches, among others. Previous studies have explored machine learning and deep learning models to address these cybersecurity problems. However, explainable models that specifically address teen online behavior remain limited. This study applies an explainable machine learning approach to analyze cyber-risk patterns in adolescent online behavior using Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) and a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP). To enhance model interpretability, Shapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) were used to provide both global and local explanations of model predictions. Indicators of safe practices, risk tolerance, self-exposure, and rate-based online interactions were incorporated through domain-driven feature engineering to enhance the behavioral dataset that describes teen online practices. The results indicate that XGBoost provides more consistent and interpretable feature attribution patterns compared to the MLP model, while the neural network demonstrates more diffuse feature contributions. The findings revealed that time spent online, rate-based activity features, and firewall-related events are key predictors associated with cyber risks among teens. Local SHAP explanations further illustrate the capacity of the XGBoost model to generate individualized cyber risk profiles. Overall, the findings highlight the value of applying explainable machine learning techniques to understand behavioral cyber risks among adolescents, and provide insights that may support future research and data-driven safety interventions.

title: Brain Tumour Detection and Size Estimation Using YOLOv10 and Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: G. Samuelraj Chrysolite, R Mokshagna, Aashish Samuel
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: The brain tumour has become one of the most dreadful neurological disorders where timely diagnosis and proper evaluation are crucial in successful clinical management. The Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is well-known in the diagnosis of brain tumour but post-processing with MRI scan is a holistic process that takes a long time and the results are subject to inter-observer variation. This paper will provide an automated brain tumour detection and size estimation system based on the YOLOv10 and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to tackle these challenges. The proposed system will use the latest competitive framework of object detection, YOLOv10, to detect tumour areas in MRI images through the creation of accurate bounding elements. A CNN-based module is then used to analyze the detected region of interest in order to detect a tumour and assist in subsequent analysis. The pixel-based estimates of the tumour size are calculated based on the identified tumour region and that allows objective and reproducible outcome of the estimation. Data used to train and validate the system are obtained and processed through the Roboflow system that guarantees anonymity of annotations and matching the network based on the YOLO architecture. The proposed framework will reinvent radiologists by processing the tumour location, confirmation, and size measurement as one single pipeline, and therefore help them relieve diagnostic tasks, increase consistency, and speed up the process of determining the clinical action. The present work also leads to the creation of reliable computer aided diagnosis systems of MRI based brain tumour analysis.

title: Intelligent Testing System for Multimodal Data Fusion: Learning Behavior Analysis based on Graph Neural Network
authors: Anfeng Xu, Rui Xue, Xuebin Huang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The behavioral data generated during the learning process exhibits significant multimodal characteristics and complex spatiotemporal dependency structures. Traditional methods based on single data sources or sequence modeling struggle to fully characterize the potential relationships between different learning behaviors. This limitation restricts the effectiveness of learning behavior analysis and intelligent testing strategy optimization. To address this issue, this paper proposes an intelligent testing system model (CMGNN-ITS) based on multimodal data fusion and graph neural networks. The model first performs quality optimization on text, video behavior trajectories, and voice interaction data. Simultaneously, it achieves spatiotemporal semantic joint alignment through Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) and cross-modal semantic mapping. In the feature learning stage, BERT, ResNet, and CNN-LSTM structures are used for deep encoding of different modalities. Optimization employs a cross-modal attention mechanism to achieve unified feature space fusion. For behavioral relationship modeling, a causal graph structure of learning behaviors is constructed, and a personalized PageRank subgraph sampling strategy is combined to improve the graph structure representation efficiency. Subsequently, graph neural networks are used to propagate and learn the representation of learning behavior dependencies. Simultaneously, a particle swarm optimization algorithm is introduced to perform a global search of the testing strategy space. This innovation enables adaptive optimization of question difficulty allocation, question order, and testing path. Experimental results show that in the learning behavior recognition task, CMGNN-ITS improves accuracy and F1 score by approximately 4.2%–10.2% and 4.0%– 13.0%, respectively, compared to CNN-LSTM, BiLSTMAttention, Multimodal Transformer, and GCN-BehaviorNet models. Ablation experiments further demonstrate that the multimodal fusion mechanism can bring performance improvements of approximately 4%–7%. In the intelligent testing strategy optimization experiment, the system's testing accuracy improved by approximately 7.3%–14.1%. Simultaneously, the average testing time decreased by approximately 8 minutes, and the question utilization efficiency improved by approximately 12%. Furthermore, behavioral causal graph analysis results show that the probability of "repeated video viewing → retrying the question" reaches 0.37, and the probability of "voice question → knowledge point jump" is 0.34. This result reflects a significant structural dependency between learning behaviors. System scalability experiments show that when the data scale reaches 1 million samples, the model's training efficiency outperforms several baseline methods.

title: Analysis of Machine Learning Algorithms for Credit Card Fraud Detection based on Resampling Methods
authors: Aditya Dudhagi, Nupoor Joshi, Sukanya Gupta, Anupama Phakatkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Identifying fraudulent activities in the financial sector is difficult because the data is significantly imbalanced, with legitimate transactions vastly outnumbering criminal ones. This disparity causes traditional models to favor the majority class, often overlooking rare but costly fraud events. This imbalance often biases conventional machine learning models toward the majority class, reducing their effectiveness in identifying rare but financially critical events. This research systematically evaluates multiple imbalance-handling strategies, including Random Oversampling, Random Undersampling, SMOTE, SMOTE combined with Tomek Links, and Conditional Tabular Generative Adversarial Networks (CTGAN). Five supervised learning models - Decision Tree, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, and XGBoost are trained on both original and resampled datasets to assess performance variations. Since accuracy is inadequate for skewed distributions, evaluation is conducted using Precision, Recall, F1-score, Balanced Accuracy, ROC-AUC, and PR-AUC. Experimental findings indicate that naïve resampling approaches either discard valuable majority information or introduce noise, while hybrid and generative techniques significantly enhance minority detection. In particular, CTGAN-based augmentation combined with XGBoost achieves the most stable balance between recall and precision across datasets. The study demonstrates that integrating generative modeling with imbalance-aware evaluation metrics substantially strengthens fraud detection systems in practical financial environments.

title: Investigation of Weight Illusion Induced by Multisensory Integration in a Virtual Reality Environment
authors: Takahisa Toyama, Kenji Mizutani
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Previous studies have primarily focused on impact sounds based on material differences (e.g., metal vs. wood) to investigate the influence of auditory stimuli in VR on perceived weight. This study expanded the scope by integrating visual volume manipulation with auditory pitch manipulation to induce weight illusions. Using Meta Quest 3, participants lifted virtual dumbbell-shaped objects of varying sizes while listening to impact sounds of different pitches. Results showed that perceived weight increased significantly with larger object volume and lower sound pitch. Unlike previous studies emphasizing timbre or emotional impression, this research demonstrates that multisensory integration of vision and hearing can replicate weight-bearing training. This approach offers a VR rehabilitation platform that flexibly adapts to individual physical conditions, reduces burden, and enhances motivation for treatment.

title: Design and Analysis of Novel Power-Optimized ReRAM Array Using 1D1R
authors: Jamuna S, Prathik Chandrapal, Kavyashree G M, Dhruva R, Jhanavi M, Anagha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Non-volatile memory (NVM) or non-volatile storage is a type of computer memory that can retain stored information even after power is removed. This project presents the modeling, design, and analysis of a non-volatile memory array based on the 1D1R (1 Diode–1 ReRAM) architecture. The growing need for scalable, energy-efficient, and high-speed memory has brought ReRAM (Resistive Random Access Memory) into focus due to its simple structure, low power operation, and compatibility with CMOS processes. However, when deployed in crossbar arrays, ReRAM cells suffer from the problem of sneak path currents—unwanted conduction paths that distort the read operation and degrade the integrity of stored data. To mitigate this, the 1D1R approach employs a diode in series with each ReRAM cell, enabling unidirectional conduction and effectively blocking leakage paths from unselected cells. In this work, we develop a Verilog-A model to capture the electrical behavior of both the ReRAM switching element and the diode. The ReRAM model supports bipolar resistive switching between high resistance (RESET) and low resistance (SET) states, while the diode model reflects nonlinear forward-biased conduction and reverse blocking. This project demonstrates the practical benefits of selector devices in resistive memory architectures and lays the groundwork for further exploration of non-volatile memory integration at large scale.

title: StreamED: Real-Time Event Detection over Big Data Streams
authors: Amrutha Sivakumar, G Bhavya Reddy, Srinidhi M, Niharika Panda
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Real-time event detection from large-scale social media streams requires scalable systems capable of efficiently handling high-velocity data while achieving accurate detection performance. This research presents an end-to-end Big Data pipeline using Apache Kafka and Spark Streaming to process massive amounts of tweets in real time, from initial large-scale preprocessing tasks of cleaning, hashing, and performing power-law analysis using MapReduce-style operations, to near-duplicate tweet detection at scale using shingling, Jaccard similarity, and Locality-Sensitive Hashing (LSH) strategies, to identifying burst patterns with low memory overhead using the Count-Min Sketch. In addition, we will compare traditional machine learning models with deep learning architectures to evaluate event-classification accuracy and the efficiency of the models’ computational resources. Our scalable, high-performance experiments demonstrate the capability of the system to sustain high ingestion rates and maintain good performance with increasing loads, providing end-to-end real-time event detection using social media in Big Data environments.

title: PitchPal-AI Startup Pitch Deck & Idea Validator
authors: M.Rajasekaran, Sai Vinay Shyam Rajith, Y. Krishna Sai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The startup world which is full of disorder hardly leads to any other problems besides those entrepreneurs face in the areas of structuring, validating, and pitching their business ideas to investors. Apart from being a very lengthy procedure, a pitch deck in the traditional manner also heavily relies on a subjective point of view of a researcher, manual research, and very limited market insights, thus the process becomes inefficient while the results are inconsistent. We, therefore, introduce PitchPal, a revolutionary AI-powered framework for spectacularly automatic idea validation and pitch deck generation leveraging cutting-edge technologies from NLP and Transformer-based models. PitchPal is an ensemble of three essential components: Firstly, an Idea Intelligence Engine, which performs semantic analysis and assesses market relevance. Secondly, a Generative Pitch Deck Creator, which produces uniform storytelling investor-ready slides. At last, but not least, a Validation Scoring System, which scores the innovation potential, market fit, and feasibility of the idea. Through extensive experimental evaluations on a startup ideas dataset comprising over 1,000 instances, PitchPal has shown to significantly enhance the quality of the content, reduce the preparation time, and increase the communication power with investors. As such, this system is a milestone in the journey of AI-powered entrepreneurship support, as it makes startup development and presentation based on data and insights, not only fast, but also accurate.

title: NyayamGPT: Indian Intelligent Legal Assistant
authors: S. Amutha, Arni Vinay, T. Jaffar Hussain, Jack V, Mohammed Ashfaq
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The Indian judicial system is currently experiencing a tremendous backlog of more than half a crore pending cases, which has resulted in delays, inefficiency, and difficulties in researching and decision making of choices in legal research. Despite improving India in terms of Legal Artificial Intelligence (Legal-AI), The direct application of Large Language Models (LLMs) is still uncommon because of incompatibility with jurisdiction, common hallucinations, and the challenge of extending these models to the India-specific hybrid legal system. This paper presents the project, NyayamGPT, as a legal intelligence system that helps to reinforce the legal and judicial system in India. It applies a Multi-Agent Retrieval-Augmented Generation (agentic-RAG) design in which various agents are involved in query comprehension, retrieval in huge Indian legal collections, and cross-checking and multi-casting facts. They will help a lot in reducing the effects of hallucinations, provide verifiable outputs, and practice privacy-by-design. NyayamGPT facilitates multilingual computer use English and popular Indian languages, facilitating legal knowledge through the use of a computer and its use. According to burden, it surpasses the conventional LLM and RAG frescoes, and is 15.8 percent more uncritically loyal and being 40 percent less factually empty pockets. All in all, NyayamGPT is a highly dependable and scalable solution to enhancing judicial research, supporting legal professionals, improving the general access of people to the legal knowledge, and, eventually, helping to decrease cases backlog within the Indian judicial system.

title: Deep Learning, Optimization Techniques, and Explainability Approaches in Automated Skin Cancer Detection: A Survey
authors: G.Kiran Kumar, N.Suhasini, M. Anila
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Skin cancer is a disease in the world that is rapidly expanding and early diagnosis is essential to good treatment and better survival of patients. However, it is challenging to classify skin lesions properly because of differences in the appearance of lesions and the imbalance in classes in available datasets. In this paper, we have described a deep learning-based model to classify skin cancer automatically with the HAM10000 dataset. The suggested solution combines image preprocessing, convolutional neural networks and optimization methods to improve the classification performance. Moreover, the Grad-Cam is used to produce a visual explanation to enhance interpretability of the model and raise clinical confidence. The experimental outcomes prove that the proposed model is more accurate and robust than the existing methods. Moreover, the strategy has high prospects of real-time clinical applications and decision support systems. Altogether, the presented research paper demonstrates the efficiency of deep learning and explainable AI in helping dermatologists with efficient and correct diagnosis of skin cancer.

title: Cryptoanalysis of Caesar Cipher Using Brute Force and Frequency Analysis Techniques
authors: Manchu Alekhya, Vangavolu Sai, Yeddu Sujith Sai, Sharvan Kumar, Madapati Hema Sri, J. Vijitha Ananthi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The Caesar Cipher is one of the earliest substitution ciphers used in classical cryptography. Although historically important, it provides extremely weak security due to its small key space and predictable structure. This paper presents a complete implementation of Caesar cipher encryption and decryption supporting shift keys from 1 to 25. Two cryptanalysis techniques, namely brute-force attack and frequency analysis attack, are implemented to automatically recover the plaintext without prior knowledge of the key. Experimental analysis evaluates attack time with respect to message length, compares success rates, and identifies vulnerable key characteristics. Results demonstrate that brute-force guarantees 100% key recovery, while frequency analysis becomes increasingly accurate as message length increases. This work highlights the insecurity of classical substitution ciphers in modern communication systems.

title: A Study of Reducing Vibration of Aircraft in Turbulence Using Variable Gain Feedback Controller With Variable Gain Feedforward Controller
authors: Hiroto Yokoyama, Kazuki Odate, Kazuhiro Morita
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Turbulence is dangerous for an aircraft due to inducing severe vibration. It not only worsens passenger comfort but also causes serious incidents. Thus, this study aims to control the attitude of an aircraft even in case of severe turbulence collision. Moreover, this study particularly focuses on clear-air turbulence (hereinafter called CAT) which rapidly changes wind speed and direction. To achieve the purpose, this study proposes a control method to reduce the vibration of an aircraft by using variable gain feedback (hereinafter called FB) control in addition to variable gain feedforward (hereinafter called FF) control. The variable gain FB control improves the stability of an aircraft because it reduces the influence of FF control. As a result, the proposed method has ensured the stability of an aircraft even in turbulence collisions. In addition, proposed method has reduced the fluctuations of Z-axis acceleration by 81% compared to the conventional method that consists of fixed gain FB control. Therefore, this study offers a prospect for preventing turbulence accidents and improving passenger comfort.

title: Connecting Talent with Opportunity: Design and Implementation of an Integrated Alumni Job Portal
authors: Susheelamma K H, Nivedita V S, Aditya Narayan, Amit Tiwari, Asif Mulla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: In this research, we present "Alumni Connect," a specialized job portal designed to bridge the gap between students, university alumni, and industry recruiters by leveraging a trusted institutional network rather than large, impersonal job platforms. The system aims to improve student placement outcomes, enable mentorship, and provide companies with access to verified talent, addressing the challenges graduates face in today’s fragmented job market. The proposed web-based platform uses a modern technology stack and implements a Hybrid Recommender System that integrates content-based and collaborative filtering to effectively match student profiles with relevant alumni- and industry-posted job opportunities while reducing the cold-start problem. Experiments conducted on anonymized student datasets and alumni job listings resulted in a recommendation accuracy of 86.7%, with the hybrid model outperforming a standard content-based matching system in precision, recall, and F1-score. These findings highlight the superiority of hybrid approaches in delivering personalized, high-quality recommendations and demonstrate the system’s strong potential for real-world use, particularly in automating and enhancing the campus recruitment process.

title: Radiomics and Artificial Intelligence in Thyroid Cancer Staging and Prognosis
authors: B Srivalli, Kummari Venkatesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Accurate preoperative staging and prognosis prediction of thyroid cancer (TC) is crucial for personalised treatment planning and, most importantly, determining if an aggressive surgical intervention is necessary, such as total thyroidectomy (TT), or prophylactic central neck dissection (pCND). Most traditional diagnostic modalities (i.e., based on ultrasound and/or fine needle aspiration cytology (FNAC)) often lack sufficient sensitivity for an accurate non-invasive assessment of tumour aggressiveness and micro metastasis. In this study, we propose a novel integrated framework that combines Radiomics and Deep Learning (DL) to enhance prediction performance for TC preoperative staging and prognosis prediction. Using preoperative CT and MRI scans that are performed in all patients, we performed high-throughput quantitative feature extraction and, in parallel, built a multimodal DL model that predicts clinically impactful prognostic indicators associated with TC preoperative staging/prognosis, specifically lymph node metastasis (LNM) and extrathyroidal extension (ETE). We develop a custom Attention-based Feature Fusion Network (AFFN) that best weighs each data modality. The results show that the integrated Radiomics-DL model outperformed both models that used only clinical data alone and those that used only single-modality features alone, detecting LNM and ETE with AUCs of 0.92 and 0.88, respectively. In conclusion, this non-invasive, AI-based framework has the potential to provide meaningful and practical clinical decision support, minimising unnecessary surgeries and effectively stratifying patients for targeted therapies. The proposed Radiomics-DL framework sets a high standard in precision oncology for managing patients with thyroid cancer.

title: Language-Independent Fake News Detection using GP-ADE Hybrid Evolutionary Approach
authors: S.Md.RiyazNaik, K.Charitha, A.Surekha, A.Alekhya, E.Mounika, K.Deepthi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Digital media platforms have experienced rapid growth which has led to increased dissemination of false information and fabricated news stories. This phenomenon creates major obstacles which undermine public confidence and social order and the ability to trust information sources. The process of fake news detection is extremely challenging when researchers try to analyze news articles in multiple languages because of different linguistic systems and the scarcity of available training materials. The research develops a framework for detecting fake news across various languages through its system which combines Genetic Programming (GP) with Adaptive Differential Evolution (ADE) to function as a language-independent detection solution. The proposed GP-ADE model is designed to optimize feature selection and improve classification performance by evolving candidate solutions through evolutionary search strategies. The hybrid optimization framework uses textual features from news content to conduct processing operations which help establish patterns that link to deceptive content. The proposed approach demonstrates superior classification performance and system reliability through its experimental evaluations against traditional machine learning approaches. The research demonstrates that evolutionary optimization methods can successfully improve fake news detection performance when applied to multiple languages. This research develops misinformation detection systems which operate across multiple languages and use scalable methods to detect false information which enables digital platforms to distribute trustworthy information.

title: Effects of Graphic Recording Layout Styles on Information Comprehension
authors: Rin Kawahisa, Sno Ooi.
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: This paper aims to investigate the information aggregated by the layout style of graphic recording. Currently, graphic recording lacks consistent standards for layout, including structure and style, and the relationship between layout and the content of discussions has not been sufficiently investigated. Accordingly, this study examines the effects of different layout styles (three-part, four-part, and radial) on comprehension and perceived impressions.

title: Design Optimization of Feedforward and Recurrent Neural Networks for Process Variation Classification Pattern Recognition
authors: Alluri Prathima, Muhammed Abraar S A, V T Ram Pavan Kumar, B Nagaiah, Praveena Narayanan, Nouluri Venkata Phaneeswar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Variation in processes in industrial, electronic systems can greatly affect performance, reliability and yield. Good pattern identification of process variation is imperative in quality control and fault identification. The current paper focuses on the issue of the optimization of hidden layer settings in feedforward networks and recurrent neural networks (RNNs) to enhance the accuracy of pattern recognition of variations in processes. We offer a formal design of optimization in the number of neurons, the activation, and the configuration of layers in both designs. We present and compare a piece-wise linear activation function to the more widely used ReLU and sigmoid activation functions, as a way to expand the learning ability of the networks. The proposed framework was trained with PyTorch, and tested on benchmark variability data of different processes. The findings indicate that the optimum parameters of the hidden layer with both feedforward and RNN networks will yield better recognition accuracy, less training period, and the generalization of the model. The method outperforms typical architectures utilizing comparative analysis, confirming the methodology is a viable option for monitoring industrial processes.

title: Improved VGGNet Architecture for Accurate Identification of Influenza and Cold Infections from CT and MRI Images
authors: N. Deepa
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Approaches of machine learning that are used in existing systems suffer from various challenges while classifying influenza as well as cold infections. These challenges are addressed by proposing a framework of Improved VGGNet algorithms which is a deep learning framework for classifying along with segmenting these medical images. This model utilizes CT as well as images of MRI for enhancing the precision of diagnosis in order to overcome the challenges faced by existing systems. Intricate patterns along with features in these examined images are identified by the proposed system assisting the process of understanding the infection. Deep learning helps in distinguishing these infections and the presence of CT along with MRI images in the dataset makes the system robust. By this way, the system identifies the infection accurately 93.063% along with precision of 0.92 and recall values as 0.94. The usage of these metrics shows the effectiveness of the model as false prediction positives as well as negatives are reduced. The proposed study helps in identifying these diseases in an effective way thereby being a supportive healthcare tool for making decisions in healthcare practices in image analysis.

title: A Study of the Removability of Speaker-Adversarial Perturbations
authors: Liping Chen, Chenyang Guo, Kong Aik Lee, Zhen-Hua Ling, Wu Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Recent advancements in adversarial attacks have demonstrated their effectiveness in misleading speaker recognition models, making wrong predictions about speaker identities. On the other hand, defense techniques against speaker-adversarial attacks focus on reducing the effects of speaker-adversarial perturbations on speaker attribute extraction. These techniques do not seek to fully remove the perturbations and restore the original speech. To this end, this paper studies the removability of speaker-adversarial perturbations. Specifically, the investigation is conducted assuming various degrees of awareness of the perturbation generator across three scenarios: ignorant, semi-informed, and well-informed. Besides, we consider both the optimization-based and feedforward perturbation generation methods. Experiments conducted on the LibriSpeech dataset demonstrated that: 1) in the ignorant scenario, speaker-adversarial perturbations cannot be eliminated, although their impact on speaker attribute extraction is reduced, 2) in the semi-informed scenario, the speaker-adversarial perturbations cannot be fully removed, while those generated by the feedforward model can be considerably reduced, and 3) in the well-informed scenario, speaker-adversarial perturbations are nearly eliminated, allowing for the restoration of the original speech.

title: Efficient NoC for Embedded Heterogeneous Multi-Core DSPs
authors: Wei Chen, Dake Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: At present, most networks on chips (NoCs) are designed for symmetric multiprocessing architectures for general-purpose computing. While currently designed NoCs provide considerable flexibility, they are accompanied by substantial routing delays and a relatively high cost associated with reorder buffer memory. The significant difference in data transmission length is a characteristic of heterogeneous multi-core systems. We propose an innovative NoC architecture for embedded heterogeneous multi-core digital signal processor (DSP) systems, which includes the design of top-level architecture, router, network interface (NI), and NoC pipeline. Our designed NoC architecture has the advantages of flexibility and low latency. It can not only significantly improve the transmission performance of long data packets but also improve the transmission efficiency of short data packets. Moreover, we propose efficient routing methods, which include an efficient routing algorithm, a hybrid transmission method, a packet-connected circuit transmission method, a short packet transmission method, and multicast and broadcast transmission methods. We also propose fault-tolerant data transmission mechanisms, which include a fault-tolerant data transmission algorithm and a deadlock avoidance method. Our proposed method and mechanism can improve the performance and reliability of NoC, mitigate the impact of router failures, eliminate the need for reorder buffer memory, reduce power consumption, and improve the robustness of NoC. The experiments show the performance advantages of our design in terms of latency, throughput, and silicon overhead in comparison with state-of-the-art NoCs. Our design reduced the latency, area, and power consumption by 47%, 92.6%, and 78.9%, respectively, and improved the throughput by 27%. This verifies its effectiveness in high-end applications.

title: EMFQA: Enhancing Multi-Dimensional Facial Quality Assessment via Multi-Scale Perception and Prompt-Conditioned Adaptation
authors: Sha Wang, Jia yu Ding, Xin ran Yuan, Feng Gao, Yu Yao
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 18th International Conference on Computer Research and Development
abstract: None

title: Real-Time Classroom Engagement and Interaction Monitoring Using YOLOv5
authors: M. Gokuldhev, Muhammed Sinan P, Krishna Pratap Dwivedi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This research demonstrates a highly developed vision-based monitoring framework of the classroom that com-bines YOLOv5 object detection with DeepSORT for tracking multiple objects in monitoring student interaction and engagement in real time. The general idea is to develop a non-intrusive, computerized system that would automatically analyze the behavior of the class from behavioral as well as emotional clues obtained from live video streams. The developed model detects each student, follows the activity of each student during the session, and categorizes the level of engagement based on visual cues such as the direction of attention, facial expressions, and participation gestures.To facilitate interpretability, an interactive analytics dashboard is created that presents engagement measures, student and group performance, and temporal sequences of classroom attentions. The visualization allows educators to derive actionable insights on detecting disengaged students and adapting their teaching strategy in real time. The architectural approach is focused on **edge computing deployment** such that real-time computing can be done locally on devices with efficient execution without using any cloud facility, ensuring data confidentiality and minimal latency.A custom-annotated classroom dataset was developed and collected with great care in order to train and test the model, emulating various student behaviors, learning patterns, and classroom conditions through realistic but controlled scenarios. Experimental results show that the new system can reach high accuracy, strong robustness, and real-time responsiveness and can be a realistic solution to current smart classrooms as well as educational analytics applications.

title: New Approach to Small-Signal Parameter Extraction for GaN HEMTs up to Ku-Band
authors: Deepti Mongia, Dhananjay Rana, Kirti Bansal, Subhash Chander, Devendra Verma
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: A new small-signal modeling approach applied to GaN HEMTs is presented. This approach addresses the challenges associated with numerical optimization of equivalent small-signal parameters using broadband S-parameter data, namely sensitivity to initial conditions, selection of starting values for extrinsic pad capacitances, problem of local minima in optimization algorithms and non-uniqueness of extracted small-signal parameters. The validity of the proposed small-signal modeling approach and equivalent circuit is verified by comparing the simulated wide-band small-signal S-parameters with measured S-parameters of indigenous 0.25 µm AlGaN/GaN HEMTs of 2×150 µm and 10×175 µm sizes.

title: RTL to GDS Implementation of a RISC-V SoC with Integrated Timer using Openlane Framework
authors: Gavaskar K, Shivappriya S N, Ishwerya V, Kanagadharshan V, Elango S, Anbumani V
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: This paper describes the development and physical execution of a 32-bit RISC-V System-on-Chip (SoC) that incorporates a timer with programming unit utilizing a fully open-source RTL-to-GDSII design pipeline. Due to its open, modular, and license-free nature, the RISC-V architecture has emerged as a viable alternative to proprietary CPU designs. In order to solve these problems, a memory-mapped clock that can produce frequent signals for real-time management is combined with a tiny RISC-V core that supports the RV32I core instruction set. The entire SoC is built with the Open Lane streamlining structure and the Sky130 130 nm CMOS process design kit. Operational validity is tested using cycle-precise simulations, which ensure suitable command processing and dependable interrupt management. Logic synthesis generates an area-saving gate-level architecture translated to Sky130 standard cells. The final GDSII output shows that a fully operational RISC-V SoC can be built with only freeware tools, demonstrating the potential of open hardware design for embedded and industrial applications.

title: Machine Learning-based Stroke Detection using CT and MRI Imaging
authors: Hariharan R, Ruthrapathi M, Sanjay K, Srikesh S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Differentiating between ischemic and hemorrhagic stroke is important, as both are major causes of death and disability. With the development of medical imaging, machine learning (ML) is also applied to stroke detection and triage. This work focuses on recent advances in the machine learning methods used for stroke detection that appeared from 2020 till 2025 in CT/MRI images. In this work, we analyze and compare the evolution of deep-learning-based architectures, such as CNN, U-Net, transformers, and hybrid ensembles, from classical classifiers with handcrafted modalities. This review is based on how to categorize lesion types, localize hemorrhage, and isolate ischemic infarctions, as well as combined imaging strategies. Some recent advances like transfer learning, low-power CNNs for edge applications, uncertainty-aware ideas, and explainable AI for improved clinical interpretation are also discussed. But there still exist many challenges, including domain shifts across scanners, multi-center validation limited in scale, interpretability lacking in a model, and easy association with patient records under the privacy constraint. This review details methodological advancements and unresolved challenges associated with the above-mentioned methodologies. We view the ability to recognize strokes with ML as a key enabler that can help emerging needs like real-time, robust, and equitable diagnostic support systems toward ultimately beginning to deploy AI in acute stroke management.

title: Development of an Information-based Supply Chain Optimization System for Abaca Farmers and Traders
authors: Nancy P. Jacosalem, Jocelyn O. Balolot, Jade G. Cuartero, Michelle C. Elape, Jeanette M. Eborda, James Cloyd M. Bustillo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The abaca fiber industry in Barangay Culiram, Talacogon, Agusan del Sur, is a vital economic driver for local farmers and traders. However, the sector is currently hindered by fragmented supply chain processes, reliance on manual record-keeping, and restricted market access. This study addresses these challenges through the development of the E.A.S.Y. ABACA system, an information-based web optimization platform designed to automate farmer profiling, enhance production monitoring, and improve market transparency. Utilizing the Waterfall Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), the system was engineered using ReactJS, Node.js, and MySQL. Data were gathered through a mixed-methods approach involving 150 stakeholders, including farmers, traders, and agricultural officers. Evaluation results yielded an average weighted mean of 4.28 ("Very Good") on a five-point Likert scale across all system objectives. The findings demonstrate that the platform significantly improves data accuracy, facilitates informed decision-making, and fosters a more equitable and competitive agricultural economy by providing real-time market pricing and efficient supply chain oversight.

title: Image Encryption Technology Based on Fractal Image Compression Algorithm
authors: Karan A, Karthickkameshwar S, Dhandapani Samiappan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Image security and efficient storage have become critical challenges with the exponential growth of digital multimedia. The computational cost for traditional methods of encryption, especially with large image datasets, is high and significantly affects storage efficiency. In this work, fractal image compression is integrated with encryption techniques to make images secure and compact. First, a fractal image compression algorithm is applied to the proposed system for reducing the size of an image without significant quality degradation, followed by a robust encryption mechanism in order to protect sensitive information. The foremost aim is to ensure high-level security with the view of providing quick, efficient storage and faster transmission of images across networks. Its novelty lies in that fractals could leverage the self-similarity property to support simultaneous compression and encryption with very minimal processing overhead compared to conventional methods. Experimental results demonstrated that the system achieved superior compression ratios alongside strong cryptographic protection, suitable for secure multimedia transmission over bandwidth-constrained environments.

title: Low Latency Drowning Prevention: Design and Implementation of a Smart Safety Belt with Autonomous, Sensor-Fusion-Triggered Inflation
authors: Parimelazhagan S, Thirumoorthy S M, Karthikeyan M V, Tephillah S, Silva Deena J
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Drowning has been one of the leading causes of accidental death among the aquatic environments, especially in unsupervised or open-water areas. The paper describes the design and execution of a wearable smart safety belt, called Rescue Air, which is a detection system of possible drowning incidents, and it is composed of a water-immersion sensor, an MPU6050 inertial measurement unit (IMU), and GPS positioning. Detection algorithm is decided by rule driven decision logic and prolonged immersion time and abnormal movement patterns. When the distress conditions are confirmed, a solenoid operated 33g CO 2 cartridge inflates an airbag of 16.8L to offer about 160N of buoyancy. The system was tested in controlled experimental conditions in order to quantify latency of detection and reliability of its functioning. Findings show that the suggested prototype is capable of deploying the airbag in less than 2 seconds of the distress signal being received. The system is developed on a low-power microcontroller platform, and also provides the manual override features together with the inherent safety protection of the system to ensure safe functioning.

title: Sketch-Based 3D Anime-Style Character Generation with Geometry Guidance
authors: Kai Niu, Bofei Huang, Haoran Xie
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Multi-view image generation is crucial for 3D reconstruction, yet diffusion methods often lack strict perspective and tend toward near-orthographic views. This may lead to inaccurate camera pose estimation with severe artifacts and misalignment. Especially for anime-style images, geometry details cannot be faithfully reconstructed, such as faces, hair, and clothing. We propose a novel sketch-based 3D generation framework for 3D anime-style characters from a sketch using a feed-forward reconstruction stage built on synthesized multi-view images. A coarse geometry produced by a 3D generation model constrains the reconstruction stage to recover accurate geometry while preserving texture quality. For our constructed anime character dataset, the proposed method outperforms single-model baselines with high user satisfaction, highlighting the promise of combining generation with reconstruction for anime-style 3D reconstruction.

title: Design and Testing of Anti-Pumping Circuit for High-Voltage Circuit Breaker
authors: Chaoyu Chen, Qingbiao Zhan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: High-voltage circuit breakers play a critical role in the power system, and their anti-pumping circuit is an important protective measure to prevent the circuit breaker from repeatedly “closing opening” fault cycles due to the continuous existence of closing commands or contact adhesion. On the basis of analyzing the design requirements of circuit breaker control circuits, this article compares and studies the working principles, structural characteristics, and engineering applicability of traditional circuit breaker mechanism box antipumping circuits and innovative design protection device antipumping circuits. Through practical cases and experimental data analysis, the key impact of the matching between the action time of anti-pumping relays and the opening and closing time of circuit breakers on circuit reliability was revealed, and engineering suggestions were proposed to follow the principle of “single anti-pumping” and avoid using two sets of anti-pumping circuits together. At the same time, the system elaborates on the testing methods and common problem handling of antipumping circuits, providing technical reference and practical guidance for improving the operational reliability of highvoltage circuit breakers.

title: When AI Manages Memory: Q-Learning Based Adaptive Page Replacement
authors: Rehan George Mathew, S L Richris, Rajesh Kannan Megalingam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: As modern computing workloads have drastically increased in data-intensiveness, heterogeneity, and latency-sensitivity, memory management has become the primary source of bottlenecks in the operating system’s performance. Although page replacement algorithms such as First-In-First-Out (FIFO), Least Recently Used (LRU), and CLOCK are considered outdated, they are still mostly used because of their simplicity and predictability. Nevertheless, the predetermined decision logic of these algorithms render them unable to manage the dynamically changing access patterns which, for instance, could be normal for cloud analytics, containerized microservices, and machine learning inference systems.The present study is a clear and understandable RL system for adaptive page replacement based on Q-Learning. The system keeps metrics—page-fault rate, reuse distance, and working set size—performing one single run from the three algorithms FIFO, LRU, and CLOCK it chooses the best policy. Through online temporal-difference learning, it gradually adjusts its decision to a workload variation and hence, it supports the operation in a self-adaptive mode that is not affected by kernel latency constraints.The Q-Learning model introduced is a good example of a fully transparent system as compared to deep neural models. This is because it has a short, human-friendly Q-table that clearly shows the connection between workload states and the replacement actions.
