title: On the Availability Risks of Production LLM Services under Unbounded Inference_supp1-3689535.pdf
authors: Hongzi Zhu
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become foundational components in a wide range of applications, including natural language understanding and generation, embodied intelligence, and scientific discovery. As their computational requirements continue to grow, these models are increasingly deployed as cloud-based services, allowing users to access powerful LLMs via the Internet. However, this deployment model introduces a new class of threat: denial-of-service (DoS) attacks via unbounded reasoning, where adversaries craft specially designed inputs that cause the model to enter excessively long or infinite generation loops. These attacks can exhaust backend compute resources, degrading or denying service to legitimate users. To mitigate such risks, many LLM providers adopt a closed-source, black box setting to obscure model internals. In this paper, we propose ThinkTrap, a novel input-space optimization framework for DoS attacks against LLM services even in black-box environments. The core idea of ThinkTrap is to first map discrete tokens into a continuous embedding space, then undertake efficient black-box optimization in a low-dimensional subspace exploiting input sparsity. The goal of this optimization is to identify adversarial prompts that induce extended or non-terminating generation across several state-of-the-art LLMs, achieving DoS with minimal token overhead. We evaluate ThinkTrap across multiple commercial, closed-source LLM services and observe that it can consistently induce abnormally long outputs and noticeable response-side degradation under black-box access. To further quantify the system-level impact of the attack, we conduct controlled experiments on private LLM deployments, where ThinkTrap reduces service throughput to as low as 1% of its original capacity and, in extreme cases, induces complete service failure due to resource exhaustion.

title: Measured Energy Consumption for AI-based Precoding
authors: Pengyu Cong, Chenyang Yang, Shengqian Han, Nan Li, Qi Sun
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper presents the first measurement-based study on the energy consumption of artificial intelligence (AI) algorithms for radio access networks (RAN), aiming to investigate whether the integration of AI leads to higher energy consumption than traditional non-AI algorithms. We use the multiuser precoding problem as a representative example, where AI is widely recognized for its potential in supporting the growing deployment of antennas for 6G systems. Our assessment examines three AI algorithms using the convolutional neural network (CNN), edge graph neural network (EGNN), and communication model-based graph neural network (MGNN), in comparison with two non-AI algorithms, specifically the weighted minimum mean square error (WMMSE) and regularized zero-forcing (RZF) algorithms. Through extensive measurements of energy and power consumption, we examine how efficient architecture designs of deep neural networks (DNNs) affect energy consumptions, and provide a quantitative comparison between measured results and estimates from existing power estimation models. The results indicate that the MGNN, which is the most efficient architecture among the three DNNs, consumes substantially less energy than the non-AI algorithms for large-scale systems, while the CNN consumes significantly more energy for both training and inference compared to the MGNN. Moreover, utilizing GPUs does not always lead to higher power consumption compared to CPUs, the power consumption of CPUs should be taken into account when GPUs are used, and DRAM contributes little to total power consumption. We demonstrate the shortcomings of existing utilization-based and FLOPs-based models in estimating energy consumption, calling for the development of more accurate models to facilitate green AI designs for RAN.

title: A 13-bit 70-MS/s SAR-Assisted Cyclic ADC With Charge-Reused 2-bit/Cycle Operation
authors: Rucheng Jiang, Han Wu, Kian Ann Ng, Chne-Wuen Tsai, Jerald Yoo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This article presents an energy-efficient 13-bit high-speed SAR-assisted cyclic ADC. The <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1^{\text {st}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> stage 6-bit SAR ADC coarsely quantizes the input signal. The proposed charge-reused 2-bit/cycle cyclic ADC doubles the conversion speed with 50% reduction in capacitor number. Owing to the comparator noise suppression circuit and mismatch robustness of the cyclic ADC, an interstage gain of only <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> is required, which significantly relaxes the amplifier design and improves its total harmonic distortion (THD). Fabricated in 40nm 1P8M CMOS, the proposed SAR-assisted cyclic ADC achieves 13b resolution with SNDR and SFDR of 66.5dB and 80.1dB, respectively, with a Nyquist-rate input at 70MS/s without calibration while consuming 0.88mW power, yielding FoM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</sub> and FoM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">W</sub> of 175dB and 6.9fJ/conv., respectively. The ADC demonstrates a sub-1dB SNDR variation across the measured 9 chips, presenting the robustness against variations.

title: Crash-Risk-Aware Integrated Predictive Control in Emergency Conditions for Intelligent Vehicles
authors: Yu Zhang, Fuwei Wu, Yechen Qin, Mingming Dong, Shaoyang Shi, Ke Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Crash-avoidance systems in current intelligent vehicles (IVs) require accurate crash risk estimation and optimization of multiple control inputs. However, the uncertainties in vehicle states caused by the inherent noise in onboard sensors lead to increased errors in spatiotemporal crash risk calculations, which, in turn, affect the optimization of control inputs. To address these challenges, this article introduces a crash-risk-aware integrated predictive control (CRIPC), which computes crash positions and time-to-collision with uncertainties (UTTC) between vehicles under nonideal conditions by constructing an extensive elliptical vehicle geometry model to quantify the uncertainties arising from sensor noise. Additionally, CRIPC utilizes UTTC and the crash-point position between vehicles to dynamically adjust the crash-risk-aware repulsive field, which is subsequently used to define the objective function for CRIPC optimization. Validation results from real-vehicle experiments and driver-in-the-loop (DiL) simulations demonstrate that CRIPC effectively quantifies the motion uncertainties of surrounding vehicles, significantly enhancing crash avoidance capabilities while maintaining vehicle stability. Furthermore, CRIPC shows robustness to variations in road conditions and system uncertainties, meeting the real-time requirements of practical applications.

title: Nanomaterial-Based Capacitive Sensor for Dust Type Differentiation on Surfaces_supp1-3690478.pdf
authors: Abdullah Abdulhameed
abstract: Current surface dust sensors offer advanced measurement capabilities, especially in determining dust concentration and accumulation ratio using light-scattering mechanisms. However, these sensors are limited in their ability to identify the type or geographical origin of dust particles. Leveraging advances in nanomaterials, we report the fabrication of nanomaterial-based dust sensors capable of differentiating dust samples from different sources under controlled dust loading on the sensor surface. Furthermore, a readout circuit is designed and implemented on a PCB to form an end-to-end sensing platform. Dust samples collected from sand dunes, house roofs, construction sites, and desertified soil are characterized using SEM, XRF, FTIR, and XRD. The results revealed that the dust samples have different morphological structures and elemental distributions, which contribute to their dielectric constants. The sensor capacitance ranged from 1.75 pF to 4.08 pF, and the sensor sensitivity ranged from 8.12% to 118.57%, respectively, as a function of dust type and accumulation level, which varied from 20 mg to 100 mg. In addition, the readout circuit reported a voltage sensitivity of 26-99% at a low dust loading of 20 mg, depending on the dust type. The proposed surface dust sensor has the potential to be integrated into solar panel systems to optimize their performance by enabling automated cleaning initiation when critical dust accumulation thresholds are reached.

title: A Real-time Detection Method of Spark Discharge in High Voltage Switchgear Based on Improved YOLOv10 and Multi-scale Feature Fusion
authors: Liming Li, Qianwen Chen, Wentao Ma, Ying Li, Yongyu Wang, Bo Liu, Liwei Song
abstract: Spark discharge is liable to occur during the long-term operation of high-voltage switchgear, which often induces electrical failures and fires. Traditional detection methods suffer from limitations including low detection accuracy, high false alarm rate, and poor real-time performance. To address these challenges, this study proposes a novel spark discharge detection framework integrating an improved YOLOv10 algorithm and a High-Resolution Network (HRNet), with a key innovation of a global-local attention fusion module. By integrating HRNet’s four-stage bidirectional cross-connection architecture, the framework realizes continuous interaction and complementarity between multi-scale features. A comprehensive spark discharge dataset was constructed using a multi-sensor array, providing reliable data support for model training and generalization verification. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves 98.9% accuracy on the validation dataset. At a resolution of 1920×1080, the false alarm rate is as low as 0.02, and the frame rate reaches 90 frames per second, realizing the synchronous improvement of detection precision and real-time performance. In practical deployment on 300 high-voltage switchgear samples under actual operating conditions, the model achieves an average detection accuracy of 99.2% with a 3D positioning error of 3.5 cm. This method significantly improves the detection accuracy and real-time performance of spark discharge, providing reliable technical support for the safety monitoring of high-voltage switchgear. The proposed method not only offers a reliable technical solution for the active safety protection of high-voltage switchgear, but also provides theoretical reference and engineering insights for the algorithm design of weak-feature target detection in similar industrial scenarios.

title: A Lightweight Multi-Head Attention Network with Hierarchical Cross-Modal Fusion for Planetary Gearbox Fault Diagnosis
authors: Qingyuan Zheng, Haitao Zhuang, Zuo Wang
abstract: As critical components in transmission systems, planetary gearboxes require precise intelligent fault diagnosis to ensure optimal performance. Most existing methods rely solely on individual vibration signals for training, a practice that leads to the dual problems of insufficient data diversity and over-parameterized models. This paper presents a novel framework that integrates multi-modal sensor fusion with a lightweight multi-head attention mechanism to effectively address these challenges. Firstly, to enhance discriminative capability, multi-axis vibration measurements (x and y-direction) from the planetary gearbox are fused with encoder data to capture complementary dynamic characteristics. Then, a hierarchical cross-modal fusion method with a gating mechanism is introduced to enhance feature reliability and richness while reducing dimensionality. Within the proposed hierarchical architecture, we first perform intra-vibration feature consolidation to remove spectral redundancy, followed by cross-domain fusion with encoder signals to amplify discriminative characteristics. Finally, to reduce computational complexity, redundant information is reduced by applying average pooling to the multi-head attention inputs. Additionally, the value vector in the conventional attention mechanism is replaced with the input itself to decrease the number of parameters. Extensive experimental validation under varying operating conditions demonstrates that the proposed method simultaneously enhances fault diagnosis accuracy and significantly reduces computational demands.

title: Dynamic Whole-Body Dancing With Humanoid Robots: A Model-Based Control Approach
authors: Shibowen Zhang, Jiayang Wu, Guannan Liu, Helin Zhu, Junjie Liu, Zhehan Li, Junhong Guo, Xiaokun Leng, Hangxin Liu, Jingwen Zhang, Jikai Wang, Zonghai Chen, Zhicheng He, Jiayi Wang, Yao Su
abstract: This paper presents an integrated model-based framework for generating and executing dynamic whole-body dance motions on humanoid robots. The framework operates in two stages: offline motion generation and online motion execution, both leveraging future state prediction to enable robust and dynamic dance motions in real-world environments. In the offline motion generation stage, human dance demonstrations are captured via a motion capture (MoCap) system, retargeted to the robot by solving a Quadratic Programming (QP) problem, and further refined using Trajectory Optimization (TO) to ensure dynamic feasibility. In the online motion execution stage, a centroidal dynamics-based Model Predictive Control (MPC) framework tracks the planned motions in real time and proactively adjusts swing foot placement to adapt to real-world disturbances. We validate our framework on the full-size humanoid robot Kuavo 4Pro, demonstrating the dynamic dance motions both in simulation and in a four-minute live public performance with a team of four robots. Experimental results show that longer prediction horizons improve both motion expressiveness in planning and stability in execution.

title: Eddy-Current Losses Analysis of Edge Burr-Affected Laminations in Single-Phase Wound Core of Transformer
authors: Chenqingyu Zhang
abstract: The interlaminar short circuit under the impact of edge burr is one of major fault of transformer core, which might locally damage the core and make the transformer operate with defection, further affect the service performance. The correlative research primarily focuses on fault simulation or experiment to laminated core at various short-circuit positions, burr lengths, material types and so on. However, the numerical method to evaluate the eddy-current losses with local interlaminar fault is relatively rare, especially for transformer core of larger capacity. Therefore, in our study, an analytical formula to estimate the eddy-current losses by respective resistance of lamination is proposed, where the non-uniform distribution of electromagnetic quantity is sufficiently considered to determine the boundary condition of eddy-current field. Meanwhile, the contact coefficient measurement of electrical steel is adequately prepared for losses computation of contact resistance. Subsequently, the losses experiment to defective samples affected by artificial burr is developed to validate the effectiveness of proposed method, where the result shows a good precision in a variety of excitations.

title: HFedAR: Resource Allocation for Adaptive Retransmission-Based Hierarchical Federated Learning in Open RAN_supp1-3682775.pdf
authors: Hongchao Wang
abstract: The flexible architecture of the open radio access network (O-RAN) provides effective support for the deployment of federated learning (FL). However, existing FL schemes in wireless networks often suffer from packet errors, which lead to reduced model test accuracy and increased training delay. In this paper, we propose a robust FL scheme in O-RAN, named adaptive retransmission-based hierarchical FL (HFedAR), which improves model test accuracy and reduces training delay. Specifically, HFedAR first analyzes the similarity of local gradients using a clustering algorithm to associate users with edge servers. Subsequently, we employ an adaptive retransmission mechanism for both edge and global aggregation, thereby facilitating rapid convergence of FL on non-independent and identically distributed data. Considering the limitations of spectrum resources and user energy, we formulate a multi-objective optimization problem to minimize FL training loss and delay. Due to the implicit nature of the objective function, the coupling of decision variables, and network dynamics, it is difficult to solve the problem through traditional convex optimization and machine learning algorithms. Therefore, we derive a convergence upper bound for HFedAR and present a two-stage resource allocation algorithm. The algorithm can jointly make optimal user power and computing resource allocation decisions in the first stage, and user scheduling, retransmission selection, and radio resource block allocation decisions in the second stage. Extensive simulation results on the MNIST and CIFAR-10 datasets demonstrate that the HFedAR scheme significantly improves model test accuracy by 3.5% and 6.5% and reduces training delay by 40.1% and 34.2% compared with existing FL benchmarks.

title: Novel Bushing With Adaptively Field Grading Structure: Simulation and Mechanism
authors: Zhiwen Huang, Gang Sun, Yifan Zhou, Ya Sun, Zhikang Yuan, Jun Hu, Jinliang He
abstract: Bushing has become a critical component endangering the safe functioning of power transmission and limiting its development to higher voltage and power levels. The main technical bottleneck that restricts high voltage bushing is the uneven distribution of electric fields due to its “plug-in” structure. In this paper, a novel adaptive field grading (AFG) bushing with a simple multi-layered structure based on nonlinear materials is introduced. A frequency-domain steady-state simulation method considering the field-dependent dielectric and loss characteristics of nonlinear materials to design the AFG bushing is proposed. Referring to the size of the AC transformer capacitive bushing, the finite element models of the 40.5 kV AFG bushing are established, and the electric field distributions are obtained. Optical electric field sensors are used to test the surface electric field distribution of the bushing samples, validating the accuracy of the simulation method. Combining simulation and experimental test results, the influence of doping concentrations and lengths of the field grading layer on the electric field distribution is studied, and the grading mechanism of nonlinear material is summarized. The results demonstrate the potential of nonlinear materials to relieve electric field concentration in bushings and provide a solid basis for its design and application.

title: 6G-Enabled Quantum-Energy-Aware Multi-Agent Multi-Modal Scheme for Underwater Acoustic–RF Networks
authors: Altaf Hussain, Shuaiyong Li, Tariq Hussain, Muhammad Salman Pathan, Theyazn H.H. Aldhyani, Eatedal Alabdulkreem
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Wireless networks with limited deployment constraints are critical for emergency response, marine monitoring, and mission-centric operations. Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks (UASNs) communications are severely constrained by high propagation delay, limited bandwidth, and energy scarcity. This paper proposes a 6G-Enabled Quantum-Aware Energy-Efficient Cooperation (6G-QAEEC) multi-agent underwater acoustic-radio frequency (RF) network framework for reliable multimodal underwater communication. The proposed architecture enables cooperative intelligence among submerged sensor nodes, Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs) relays, and 6G-enabled surface/Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) gateways. A Nearest Node Verification (NNV) discovery protocol is introduced to prune unstable links during route formation, reducing redundant flooding and improving routing stability. To further enhance network efficiency, three cooperation mechanisms are developed: adaptive relay rotation with signal amplification, duty-cycled neighbor re-verification, and opportunistic optical burst communication for local high-rate exchanges. Quantum-aware optimization at the 6G edge accelerates routing and resource scheduling decisions under dynamic channel conditions. Simulation results show that the proposed framework improves packet delivery ratio, reduces energy consumption per bit, lowers routing overhead, and decreases end-to-end delay compared with baseline schemes. Overall, the proposed multi-agent multimodal quantum-aware framework enhances reliability, energy efficiency, and spectral utilization in next-generation underwater networks.

title: FAS-MBCA: Face Anti-Spoofing using Multi-path Bi-directional Cross Attention Fusion
authors: Dhimas Arief Dharmawan, Made Vidyatma Adhi Krisna, Anto Satriyo Nugroho, Andrey Ferriyan, Bambang Yuwono, Jaconiah Widiwasita, Aqsha Jauzaarafa’ Setya Hadi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science
abstract: Face Anti-Spoofing (FAS) is a critical component for securing face recognition systems against diverse presentation attacks. multi-modal FAS approaches leverage complementary cues from multiple sensors; however, existing methods often adopt single-path fusion or uni-directional interaction schemes, which may introduce modality hierarchy and limit comprehensive cross-modal feature refinement. In this paper, we propose FAS-MBCA, a structured multi-path bi-directional cross-attention framework for tri-modal face anti-spoofing. FAS-MBCA explicitly constructs a complete pairwise interaction topology among RGB, depth, and infrared modalities, enabling reciprocal feature-map refinement across all modality pairs. By modeling six-direction cross-modal interactions within a lightweight CNN-based architecture, the proposed framework enhances multi-modal synergy while maintaining computational efficiency. We evaluate FAS-MBCA on multiple widely used benchmarks, including CASIA-SURF (MmFA), CASIA-CeFA, and WMCA, under both intra-dataset and cross-dataset protocols. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FAS-MBCA consistently outperforms single-modal baselines and na¨ıve fusion strategies, and achieves competitive or superior performance compared to recent multi-modal methods. Efficiency analysis in terms of model parameters and GFLOPs further indicates a favorable accuracy–efficiency trade-off. These results validate that explicitly modeling complete bi-directional cross-modal interaction at the feature-map level provides an effective and practical solution for multi-modal face anti-spoofing. The source code of FAS-MBCA is publicly available at https://github.com/nascafas2025/FAS-MBCA.

title: Zone-Based Demographic Mapping of Stray Dogs and Cats Using YOLOv10
authors: Vince Yuan C. Lucero, Ethan Sean Angelo S. Labi, Arianne M. Salcedo, Amb Justine O. Silang, Alvin Destajo
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Traditional methods for managing the increase in stray animals in residential areas often yield limited results due to the lack of real-time surveillance, underscoring the need for automated tracking. This study developed a system for zone-based demographic mapping of stray dogs and cats using the YOLOv10 object detection algorithm. The framework includes a dashboard that displays a heat map with predictive analytics, improving data exploration and the user experience. The methodology involved collecting synthetic and real images, then preprocessing and augmenting them. The YOLOv10 model was trained and validated on a split dataset to distinguish cats and dogs within designated zones. Evaluation used Mean Average Precision (mAP), precision, recall, and F1-Score to measure system accuracy. Results showed over 86% accuracy in identifying stray animals and reliable performance in varied settings. Findings indicate that integrating CCTV with YOLOv10 significantly improves detection, reduces manual work, and increases resident satisfaction. Future work may address system scalability and hardware improvements.

title: A Dual-Branch Vision Transformer–CNN Model with Explainable Attention for Accurate Knee Osteoarthritis Grading
authors: Neha Kumari, Eram Khan, Gurprem kaur, Tanya Gupta, Sargam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Knee osteoarthritis (KOA) is a progressive degenerative joint disease and one of the most prevalent causes of pain and mobility alteration on the global level. Radiographic images provide important information on the accuracy and timely severity; nevertheless, manual Kellgren-Lawrence (KL) grading is subjective and is likely to suffer inter-observer variability, especially in the young and middle stages of the disease. To overcome these issues, the proposed paper will use an explainable hybrid Vision Transformer-Convolutional Neural Network (ViT-CNN) model to diagnose knee x-ray images with KOA. The model suggested uses a dual-branch design, where a CNN backbone is used to encode fine-grained local radiographic texture, including edges and texture pattern, whereas a branch is a Vision Transformer that is used to encode global structural relationships using self-attention. The representations of the complementary features are merged with a concatenation-based strategy and the representatives are categorized with the help of a Multi-Layer Perceptron. Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) is incorporated to learn the parts of the anatomy that contribute to model predictions to increase clinical interpretability and trust. A 5-fold cross-validation protocol was used to conduct experiments to make the results robust and statistically reliable. The suggested hybrid model was able to classify with a mean accuracy of 96.2% with standard deviation of 0.8 and macro-precision of 95.4, macro-recall of 95.1 and macro-F1 of 95.2. In addition, the model also exhibited excellent separability of classes with a macro-Area Under the Curve (macro-AUC) of 0.981. The performance of both the normal (KL-0) and severe (KL-4) cases in terms of classes demonstrated performance with F1-scores of more than 97, whereas drastically lowering the misclassification between the adjacent KL grades. Altogether, the findings suggest that the suggestive explainable hybrid framework ViT-CNN is an effective, precise, and explainable KOA severity judger that can be recommended as a clinical decision-support framework to orthopedic practice.

title: AI-Epicast: Intelligent Forecasting of Epidemic Dynamics using Population Data
authors: Gajjala Ramya Sri, Bokka Sakeerthana Reddy, Ragpani Sowmya Rupa, Tamtam Jagadeshwar, Mohammed Nadeem
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: In order to trace the active dynamics of the spread of the diseases and in order to define the useful intervention and preparedness strategies, predictive algorithms of the epidemic outburst must be present. This complicated, nonlinear, and spatiotemporal nature of the disease transmission across various viral outbreaks may not be adequately well-represented by the deterministic nature of most of the mathematical models, which have thus far been used to examine epidemics. The state-of-the-art hybrid predictive framework, which will be proposed in the current work, will be able to incorporate the most recent machine learning and deep learning models, including Deep Neural Networks (DNN), Graph Neural Networks (GNN), Logistic Regression, and the rest of them, to expand the stochastic modeling method LSMEKF-SIRD-V. XGBoost approach, Ensemble Learning, and Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) With the processing of a large number of heterogeneous data gathered in various sources, the proposed system will Though the integrated learning models can reflect temporal, spatial and non-linear effects of real time and previous data, the model dynamic estimation of epidemiological parameters using the integration of Susceptible-Infected-Recovered-Deceased- Vaccinated (SIRD-V) compartments model with the Least Square Method (LSM) and the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) to provide a region specific prediction which is helpful in decision support in planning vaccinations, medication distribution and allocation of healthcare resources, the framework still lacks the ability to provide a region specific prediction. To support the governments and health authorities in timely making data-driven choices concerning the public health, the results of the experiments and simulations suggest that the suggested hybrid ensemble model can considerably enhance the forecasting capabilities, and can forecast the future development of any of the viral outbreaks with the help of previous and current epidemiological data.

title: Quantifying Wet-Antenna Detuning at 868 MHz for LoRa Nodes and a Path to Adaptive Matching
authors: Mohammed Jajere Adamu, Niels Neumann
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: Outdoor industrial LoRa nodes operating at 868 MHz are susceptible to moisture-induced antenna detuning that degrades link margin. We quantify this effect using a repeatable layering method to apply controlled water films in an estimated range of 0–1000 µm on three commercial antennas: a dipole and two low-profile patch antennas. While the exact film thickness could not be measured precisely, the layering approach ensured reproducible and monotonic wetting conditions. Calibrated VNA measurements reveal substantial resonance shifts towards lower frequencies (up to 130 MHz) and significant impedance mismatch. We demonstrate that simple board-level retuning substantially improves matching with return loss from 5 dB to over 15 dB near 868 MHz for a chosen wet state. We propose an adaptive-matching approach using lightweight device-side feedback to maintain acceptable match across varying wet-dry conditions. The paper outlines a validation plan targeting minimal added loss and link-level margin recovery in industrial IoT scenarios, providing practical guidance for robust 868 MHz node design in moisture-prone environments.

title: AI-Driven Adaptive DDoS Mitigation in Cloud Security: A System Implementation Using GNN and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Rajani K C, Kamaakshi Kapoor, Aneesha Mishra, Shivangi Singh, Vaibhav Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks continue to threaten cloud-hosted services by saturating network or compute resources using distributed botnets. Traditional mitigation approaches rely heavily on static configurations or manual tuning, making them unsuitable against dynamic and low-rate multidimensional attacks. To address this, we present an implemented defense pipeline combining Graph Neural Network (GNN) anomaly detection, Deep Q-Network (DQN)-based mitigation decisions, and Software Defined Networking (SDN)-enabled enforcement. A prototype setup using CIC-DDoS2019 datasets, Ryu controller, and a simulated Mininet topology demonstrates the feasibility of the approach and highlights key deployment considerations. Experimental observations indicate improved adaptability and decreased false-positive activations when compared to threshold-only defense methods.

title: Anonymous, Explainable Videos, and Accessible AI for Women’s Genital Disease Diagnosis
authors: Chintala Krishi, V. Ebenezer, S. Stewart Kirubakaran, E. Bijolin Edwin, M. Roshni Thanka, Mohammed Minhaj Manzur
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Women are still lacking easy access to simplified, dependable health information about issues related to their genitalia. These disorders are frequently misdiagnosed and misunderstood in the absence of clinical expertise. To fill this gap, we are conducting a research project to create an online information tool on Streamlit that will maintain confidentiality, as no registration is required. Our system is designed as a Random Forest Classifier that will classify 47 different types of female genital disorders by correlating symptoms and demographic variables along with contributing factors to determine which category best describes each woman's health concerns. Then, based on the classification results, we will provide two types of recommendations: (1) general advice for non-healthcare professionals; (2) specific information for healthcare providers. An analysis of the 47 conditions reveals that a significant number of them share very similar symptom patterns, which complicates the process of differentiating between them. This supports the need for the use of explainable artificial intelligence to produce clear, interpretable, and condition-specific information. Our data structure provides a consistent format across all conditions to avoid confusion and enhance patient education. These results also demonstrate the need for continual addition of new data sets to keep up with the ever-changing nature of clinical data, so that the system can improve accuracy and eventually reliability.

title: Urban House Price Forecasting in Malaysia Using Tree-Based Machine Learning Models
authors: Zheng Yin Chin, Hui Shen Choo, Yoke Leng Yong, Umi Najiah Ahmad Razimi, Muhammed Basheer Jasser, Sina Abdipoor
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Nowadays, it is essential to perform accurate predictions of urban house prices for housing market analysis and policy planning. Malaysia has traditionally used conventional valuation methods such as ARIMA models, Hedonic Price Model, and Linear Regression, but these methods often struggle to represent complex market dynamics. Now, recent work increasingly explores tree-based or neural machine learning (ML) models. This study seeks to implement tree-based models such as Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Extreme Gradient Boosting for urban house price forecasting in Malaysia, to examine the effectiveness of ML on house price predictions. In terms of data, structural, locational, and socioeconomic features were compiled from governmental portals, preprocessed, and explored before modelling. During evaluation, metrics such as Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Root Mean Squared Percentage Error (RMSPE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) were applied. Out of the three models, Random Forest outperformed the other models when predicting house prices, achieving a MAE value of 0.2189 after optimization and cross-validation. Furthermore, tree-based models also provide additional feature importance insights to stakeholders. As a result, the main influential features affecting house price prediction included house type, population and state. Overall, this study solidifies the effectiveness of tree-based models for urban house price forecasting in terms of accuracy and interpretability.

title: Adaptive AI-Powered Firewall with Integrated Deception and Automated Countermeasures for Proactive Cyber Threat Neutralization
authors: S Sandhya, S Sakthi Vinayagam, V Krithika, V Kaviyalakshmi, S Kowsalya, S Ahamed Zayan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: In the current era of cyber-attacks, the traditional firewalls have been compromised because of the dramatic increase in advanced and AI-based attacks. To counter this in this paper, a proposal is made that combines intelligent detection, deception, and automated countermeasures in one that is an AI-Powered Firewall with Active Counterattack. The created system uses pre-trained machine learning models on standard intrusion datasets that is 99.7 percent accurate in classification of malicious and normal traffic with zero latency. It is innovative in its combination of real-time, AI-based detection along with synthetic firewalls, IP spoofing, and honeypot-based counterattacks to confuse and stall the intruders and gain threat intelligence on which to develop adaptive learning. Over 90 percent of automated attack tools were fooled and an average of 9.4 minutes were added to interact with the attacker. The firewall has been tested in both enterprise and cloud systems, and has demonstrated similar scalability, robustness and resilience. The proposed system prevents, misleads and counterintelligence effectively and presents a powerful, intelligent and resounding cybersecurity platform that is proactive.

title: An Enhanced Physical Design Analysis of a RISC-V CPU using Skywater 130 nm PDK
authors: Gavaskar K, Shivappriya S N, Deepika K, Naveen Kumar S, Thushara Hameed, Elango S
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: This paper presents the physical design and power analysis of a 32 bit RISC-V processor implemented using the openlane RTL-to-GDSII flow with the SkyWater 130nm PDK. The open-source and modular nature of RISC-V enables flexible customization and simplifies verification compared to proprietary architecture ASIC flow, including synthesis, floorplanning, placement, clock tree synthesis, routing and signoff verification is carried out. The results indicate that dynamic power particularly switching and internal components dominate over leakage power. It is also observed that area and utilization increases after clock tree synthesis and routing due to buffer insertion and added interconnects. The study highlights that early design stages especially floorplanning and placement significantly impact overall power, performance and area. The final implementation demonstrates that the proposed design is suitable for low power embedded applications.

title: Application of Counter-Malaria Techniques Using UAV Aerial Imagery: A Case Study of the Osaka Expo
authors: Daichi Ishido, JuHoe Kim, Katsuya Hasegawa
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Mosquito-borne diseases pose a significant global public health challenge. Particularly in Africa, Larval Source Management (LSM)-the identification and treatment of Anopheles mosquito breeding sites using Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) imagery-has emerged as a prominent strategy for malaria control. SORA Technology has developed an innovative system to enhance the efficiency of LSM by integrating UAV aerial photography with AI-driven analysis. While this methodology has been successfully validated in Africa, its application to rapidly developing urban environments remains unexplored. This empirical study aims to adapt the UAV-based LSM technology and expertise developed in Africa to environments characterized by complex urban structures. Using the site of the 2025 World Exposition in Osaka, where mosquito outbreaks have become a critical concern, as a case study, we demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach through AI analysis of high-resolution UAV imagery. Furthermore, we outline key considerations for deploying this methodology in urban contexts based on the findings of this demonstration.

title: Real-Time Integrated Circuit Defect Classification and Automated Sorting Using YOLOv11 and Robotic Manipulation: An Experimental and Simulation Study
authors: Muhammad Taqiuddin Mat Zahar, Nor Mohd Haziq Norsahperi, Maryam Yasser Elshishini, Muhammad Hasif Azami, Wan Zuha Wan Hasan, Hafiz Rashidi Harun Ramli
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: This study evaluates the viability of integrating the state-of-the-art YOLOv11 deep learning model with a robotic manipulator to detect and sort defective integrated circuits. One of the key benefits and main contributions of this study is the development of a three-tier quality assessment model namely 'good,' 'moderate,' and 'poor' defect categories using the YOLOv11 algorithm. Comprehensive verification through simulations and experiments was conducted to determine the effectiveness of the model. Two datasets were used for training: one containing IC images with backgrounds and another without. The simulation results confirm that the model exhibits a mean average precision (mAP) of 0.977. Furthermore, experimental results confirmed the model's superiority with a 90% success rate. The study concludes that future research could be directed towards fully autonomous manufacturing environments, directly improving the fabrication process and reducing electronic waste.

title: Dual Detection of Fire and Smoke in Forest Using Deep Learning
authors: R. Pari, M. Swarna, D. Nivetha, P. Amareshwaran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Forest fire is one of the most devastating forms of natural disaster that causes colossal damage to human life, vegetation, wildlife, and infrastructure. Following such losses, there is a necessity for systems that can give early warning alerts in a timely and accurate manner. Nevertheless, the existing vision-based fire alarm systems have high false positives, low adaptability to other surroundings, and are not real-time processors. Detecting smoke and fire in forested regions is very challenging because of the changing light conditions and thick plants, smoke, and fog. Therefore, a new vision-oriented strategy is suggested that compromises effectiveness, efficiency, and flexibility. This paper employs a deep learning-based detection algorithm and a set of pre-trained models, utilizing transfer learning, data transformation, and real-time image processing, to facilitate surveillance. Evaluations on images of fire and smoke depict enhanced resilience, real-time functionality, and flexibility. YOLOv11 model attained an accuracy of 98.6 percent, confirming that it is useful in real-world applications.

title: Cross-Cloud Large Language Model Orchestration for Secure DevOps and Data Engineering in Enterprise AI
authors: Gajendra Babu Thokala, Sivaraman Krishnan, Anjali Thaneeru, Sivarama Krishna Akhil Koduri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Enterprises increasingly deploy large language models (LLMs) to automate DevOps workflows and data engineering pipelines, yet practical adoption remains constrained by security, governance, vendor lock-in, and operational complexity across heterogeneous cloud environments. This paper presents a comprehensive study and practical review of cross-cloud LLM orchestration architectures designed to support secure DevOps and enterprise-grade data engineering workloads. We analyze how orchestration layers abstract model execution, policy enforcement, and data flow control across multiple cloud providers while preserving compliance, observability, and scalability. Through architectural decomposition, implementation patterns, and real-world enterprise use cases, the paper demonstrates how LLMdriven automation can be safely embedded into CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure-as-code validation, data quality engineering, and incident response systems. The study further evaluates operational trade-offs related to latency, cost, security boundaries, and model governance in cross-cloud settings. By bridging theoretical orchestration concepts with applied frameworks and deployment practices, this work provides actionable guidance for designing resilient, secure, and vendor-neutral LLM ecosystems for enterprise AI operations.

title: Usability Evaluation of Presentation and Graph Creating Tool using AR
authors: Kaoru Mitsuhashi, Iwao Sekita, Hiroshi Takeshita
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: The advancement of Digital Transformation (DX) necessitates personnel skilled in software and electronic data management; however, the number of individuals struggling with PC operations is on the rise. The complexity of keyboard and mouse commands acts as a barrier, particularly for tasks such as figure creation and text input within graphics. Previous research demonstrated that combining AR technology with physical block manipulation enables quick and intuitive file creation with physical placement and voice input. However, whether users can independently create figures based on their own conceptual design remains unclear. This research aims to elucidate the potential for AR block operation to serve as a viable alternative to PC operation by analyzing user creation tendencies, identifying necessary tool functionalities, and evaluating the effectiveness of a phased operational learning method.

title: Mapping the Landscape of Housing Sustainability Research: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Anthonia Oluwatosin Adediran, Samuel-Soma M. Ajibade, Muhammed Basheer Jasser, Maged Zagow
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive bibliometric analysis of housing sustainability research published between 2014 and 2024 to address gaps in understanding publication patterns and evolving thematic priorities in the field. Using Scopus data from 757 peerreviewed publications, the study employs Microsoft Excel for trend analysis and VOSviewer for co-authorship networks, citation analysis, and keyword co-occurrence mapping to identify influential authors, institutions, funding sources, and high-impact studies. The findings reveal a clear thematic evolution from predominantly technical perspectives, such as green architecture, toward more integrated economic, social, and cultural dimensions of housing sustainability. Emerging research themes include climate resilience, smart housing, circular economy practices, and health-integrated housing. However, substantial gaps persist, particularly within the social sustainability pillar of the Triple Bottom Line framework, highlighting the need for greater emphasis on equity and social justice in housing policies. The study underscores the importance of interdisciplinary collaboration and alignment between academic research and global agendas such as the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, while offering practical insights for policymakers and practitioners to strengthen sustainable housing frameworks. As one of the first comprehensive bibliometric reviews covering a decade of housing sustainability literature, this research maps the field's intellectual structure, identifies critical research gaps, and provides a robust foundation for future scholarly inquiry and policy development.

title: Security in Telehealth: A Comprehensive Framework Combining Watermarking, Blockchain, and Encryption
authors: Dip Patra, Soumyadiptya Dutta, Priyanka Biswas, Nirmalya Kar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Telehealth systems have become increasingly important in modern healthcare delivery, but protecting sensitive patient data remains a critical challenge. Many existing systems struggle with vulnerabilities that compromise data integrity, confidentiality, and the ability to track who accessed what information and when. This framework presents a security approach that combines three key technologies: AES-256 encryption for keeping diagnostic information private, LSB (Least Significant Bit) steganography for embedding encrypted reports directly into medical images, and blockchain records for creating an audit trail. Our method works by first encrypting a diagnostic report, then hiding it within the pixels of a medical image using LSB embedding, and finally storing a digital fingerprint (hash) of the final image on a blockchain ledger. This integrated approach ensures that patient data remains confidential during transmission, the integrity of medical images is preserved and verifiable, and there is a permanent record of data sharing events. Unlike standalone security solutions that address only single aspects of the problem, our framework creates a unified system where encryption, steganography, and blockchain work together to establish a secure and trustworthy environment for telehealth communications.

title: Refill Alert: LPG Cylinder Monitoring and Alert Notification System
authors: Garapati Meghana, Divya Kothapalli, Shaik Fathima Saniya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) is widely used for household and industrial applications, where continuous monitoring and safety are critical. This pa- per presents a LPG monitoring system that measures, analyzes, and communicates the cylinder status in real time. The proposed system uses a load cell sensor with an HX711 amplifier to measure the cylinder weight and calculate the remaining gas level. An Arduino Uno microcontroller processes the sensor data and compares it with a predefined threshold value. When the gas level falls below the threshold, the system activates a buzzer and sends an SMS alert to the user through a GSM module. Experimental results show that the system detects low gas conditions quickly and sends notifications within approximately five seconds. The use of GSM communication enables the system to operate effectively without requiring internet connectivity, making it suitable for both urban and rural environments. Unlike traditional methods that depend on manual inspection, such as tapping or lifting the cylinder, the proposed approach provides accurate and reliable monitoring through weight measurement. Overall, the proposed solution offers a low-cost, efficient, and user-friendly approach for real-time LPG monitor- ing and safety management.

title: A Discrete-Event Simulator for TSCH Networks
authors: Stefano Scanzio, Gianluca Cena
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: This work presents a realistic example of the use of TSCHmodeler, a discrete-event simulator for wireless sensor networks based on time slotted channel hopping. The simulator is written in Python, which makes it easy to use and adapt to different application contexts, including testing new techniques, evaluating scenarios before deploying real devices, or interfacing with the physical environment in a network-digital-twin fashion. In the simple example we present, different network configurations are compared. Results are discussed with particular attention to how the simulator can be used to improve typical network performance indices such as latency and energy consumption.

title: Enhanced Evaluation System for Student Performance Analysis
authors: Ashwini Matange, Rutuja Kalekar, Akshata Mahajan, Pranjali Kotalwar, Sayee Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This study investigates the integration of behavioral metrics into traditional student evaluation frameworks to provide deeper insights into test-taking strategies and overall performance. Metrics such as total time spent on tests, time efficiency, correct-to-incorrect answer ratios, and question revisit proportions are quantified using well-defined formulas. The objective is to complement conventional grading systems by equipping educators with actionable insights into students' time management, decision-making, and confidence levels. This paper presents the methodology for computing these metrics, discusses findings based on simulated datasets, and demonstrates how behavioral analytics can enhance teaching strategies and support personalized feedback mechanisms.

title: Real-Time Leukemia Detection from Blood Smear Images Using AI
authors: Jayashree N, Ashok Kumar K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Leukemia is an aggressive hematological neoplasm that demands early accurate diagnosis for successful treatment. To overcome this issue, this paper suggests a new AI-based framework for real-time leukemia detection and classification from blood smear images. The suggested model incorporates a Swin Transformer for hierarchical feature extraction and Momentum Contrast (MoCo) for self-supervised pretraining, strengthening feature representation and lowering reliance on large annotated datasets. A lightweight YOLOv8 model is used for real-time detection, allowing for fast localization and classification of leukemic cells. It uses preprocessing techniques including stain normalization, contrast enhancement, and noise elimination in addition to segmentation architectures such as DeepLabv3+ to segment white blood cells from intricate smear background. By incorporating global context transformers and real-time object detection, the model demonstrates better performance compared to CNN-based methods in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. This approach guarantees robustness, generalizability, and deployability in clinic environments. The system proposed here presents a scalable and effective method for automated leukemia diagnosis, minimizing human error and speeding up clinical decision- making.

title: An IoT-based Water Management System for Precision Irrigation using AquaNet-S Model
authors: Abinaya. M, Ashwini. T. B, Charithra. P, Jency Ruba. S, Maheswari. A
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Effective water management has been a very important problem in the agricultural industry, especially in water-deficient areas. By combining both IoT-based and data-driven intelligent models (IMs) of irrigation optimization, this paper presents an intelligent model, which is called AquaNet-S, to optimize irrigation and crop productivity. In the system, the field-deployed IoT sensors such as temperature sensor, humidity sensor, soil moisture sensor, and water flow sensor are used in the system to gather real-time data of soil and the environment. The obtained data is sent through the wireless communication devices like Wi-Fi and LoRa to the central processing unit of the AquaNet-S model that processes the data to understand the content of the soil, weather conditions, and requirements of the crop to come up with the best irrigation times. The suggested system automatically manages autopilot irrigation pumps and solenoid valves to distribute the accurate amounts of water using the crop needs. Experimental findings show that there is a 30-40% decrease in the use of water as compared to the traditional irrigation techniques and the soil moisture is also maintained at optimal levels between 60-75% of major crops grown regions. The system was able to achieve an irrigation rate of about 92-96% and latency of real-time response of less than 2 seconds. Also, the field trials showed an increase in crop yield by 15- 20% owing to the fact that water was distributed in a timely manner and evenly. The web and mobile dashboard can also be integrated and farmers can follow irrigation and get alerts, thereby enabling them to make informed decisions. On the whole, the suggested model guarantees sustainable water resource management, reduces the level of water waste, and promotes precision agriculture based on the regional climatic conditions.

title: Fake News and Rumor Classification Using GraphSAGE, GAT, and GCN
authors: Chhotelal Kumar, Naween Kumar, Akshit Naagar, Dron Rawat
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Spreading misleading information via social media platforms has been a significant concern, particularly during emergencies and large-scale events, affecting the general public. It is challenging to determine whether rumors or fake news are accurate due to the informal nature of posts on the Internet, the brevity requirement, and the fact that they are often embedded within a thread of discussions. These interaction patterns are not typically captured using traditional text-based machine learning methods, which primarily rely on lexical signals. In this study, we will be exploring the three Graph Neural Network (GNN) architectures, including GCN, GAT and GraphSAGE, in terms of their ability to detect misinformation on the PHEME dataset. We model every conversation as a graph, where the nodes are tweets (coded with contextual embeddings), and the relations between the tweets are the reply relations. To ensure a fair comparison, we also test models used in the classical context of Logistic Regression, SVM, Random Forest, and Naive Bayes, which are trained on TF-IDF features. We conduct our experiments in a Python environment and demonstrate that GAT and GraphSAGE achieve the best performance, with ROC-AUC scores of 0.930 and 0.924, respectively. Additionally, these GNN models exhibit greater recall for minority rumor classes, indicating that the propagation structure is a crucial factor in reducing misclassification. Overall, the results suggest that graph-based modeling is more reliable and informative approach to misinformation detection compared to traditional methods that rely solely on textual features.

title: Design of Low-Power CMOS Amplifier for Biomedical Applications Using Cadence Virtuoso
authors: Shashi Kant Dargar, Avulapati Giridhar, Gummagatttu Sathya Deepak
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: In modern analog and biomedical electronics, many physiological signals present at very low amplitudes, making them difficult to process without amplification. This challenge is very important particularly in healthcare applications, where signals such as ECG, EEG, and EMG contain vital information to perform diagnostics. For IoT-based wearable and portable medical devices, capturing these weak signals, accurately is crucial. Any distortion at the amplification stage can affect the overall reliability of the system. To address this work focuses on the design of an optimized two-stage CMOS amplifier using 180 nm technology, specifically for the amplification of low-level biomedical signals with high fidelity. The proposed amplifier operates at a 1.8 V supply and maintains ultra-low power consumption below 100 µW, which makes it suitable for battery powered, long term wearable health monitoring devices. It also addresses the requirements of emerging IoT based health monitoring platforms, where compact, energy efficient analog front end circuits are needed for continuous physiological data acquisition.

title: Enhanced Semantic Segmentation of Road Scenes using ConvNeXt-Tiny Blocks with Integrated Dual Attention Mechanisms
authors: Hari Swaminathan M, Suhaas M, Rohith Vishwa S, Bagyammal T, Kannan R V
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Accurate and reliable semantics segmentation through advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) is a necessity as it allows for the understanding of scenes in the real-world environment. While attention-based frameworks have made advancements in the modelling of contextual information, the majority of current frameworks typically apply spatial and channel based attention either sequentially or solely on deeper layers of the network, limiting the refinements and enhancements to early-stage features. This paper proposes a lightweight framework for semantic segmentation using ConvNeXt-Tiny by employing parallel integration of spatial attention and channel based attention at each backbone block in order to generate features that would otherwise be enhanced sequentially. The integration of both branches of attention is performed by using an element-wise summation method allowing for simultaneous enhancement of both outputs and maintaining low parameter complexity. The results of experiments performed on the Cityscapes dataset indicates that the proposed dual attention architecture provides an mIoU of 80.62%, exceeding the performance of models using single attention based methods and also demonstrating computational efficiency. Qualitative results indicate improved delineations of boundaries, improvement of performance in low-level lighting conditions, and the overall suitability of this proposed method for implementation in real-time ADAS systems.

title: Architectural Optimization of a 16-Bit Vedic Multiplier for Power-Efficient High-Speed Computing
authors: V.Muralidharan, E.Pratheeka, C.Saranya, C.Sujith Kumar
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Efficiency of multiplication circuits emerges as one of the most important parameters for a modern-day digital VLSI design environment, particularly for computing systems used for various applications of digital signal processing/image processing. This paper presents the design, implementation, and performance evaluation of a 16-bit low-power, high-speed Vedic multiplier based on the Urdhva-Tiryagbhyam sutra of Vedic Mathematics. In the proposed work, the Vedic multiplier architecture is systematically compared with that of the conventional Wallace Tree multiplier based on key performance metrics, namely propagation delay and power dissipation. Both architectures are designed and simulated under identical operating and environmental conditions to ensure a fair and accurate comparison. Results obtained from the simulation show that even as both multipliers have an equal power dissipation of 0.166 W, the Vedic multiplier is found to be dynamically efficient compared to the Wallace multiplier due to less switching activity involved for the Vedic multiplier. It also show that the Vedic multiplier is found to have less propagation delay compared to the Wallace tree multiplier approach. It was found that the Vedic multiplier takes less delay to be achieved with 6.241ns compared to the Wallace tree approach that takes 6.465ns delay to be achieved. This confirms that the Vedic multiplier provides an efficient balance between speed and efficiency compared to the Wallace tree multiplier approach for high-speed and efficient implementation of digital VLSI circuits with less delay involved for achieving efficiency.

title: Design and Analysis of a 5-Port Router with Enhanced Features for NoC Applications
authors: Ganga Narmada, Subramanya G. Nayak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: With the current generation of System-on-Chip (SoC) designs moving towards having more and more processing cores built into their structures, the demand to have a communication network that can scale up without having to impact timing or predictability has been demanded more and more. Network-on-Chip (NoC) architectures have become a viable option in this sense since they enable parallel transfer of data with reduced interconnect complexity in the whole globe and reduced overall power consumption. The router is at the centre of any NoC, the router controls the movement of flits between nodes and hence contributes significantly to the performance of the network. In this paper, the design and implementation of a five-port QoS-enabled router is proposed in a 2-D mesh NoC. To ensure that traffic with more urgency and that with higher priority than other traffic like real-time data, receives faster access to shared paths when there are conflicts between multiple requests (i.e. not in the same path), the router employs a priority-based arbitration scheme. This assists in addressing latency needs in the busy traffic circumstances. This design is specified in Verilog HDL and its entire design being functionally validated with Cadence Xcelium. In Cadence Genus, synthesis and timing checks are done using typical constraints of SDC. The findings validate proper routing dynamics, one-cycle switching throughput, and significant contention delay reduction in addition to effective utilization of hardware resources. The results of these observations show that the suggested router can be used in performance-sensitive on-chip communication applications in the current SoC platforms.

title: Explainable Multimodal Deep Learning for Early Neurodegenerative Dementia Diagnosis
authors: R. Abarnaswara, S. Aarthi, V. Aiswarya, S. Dheetchitha, R. Dhivyashree
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Early and accurate differentiation of major dementia subtypes remains a critical clinical challenge due to overlapping structural, metabolic, and cognitive manifestations. This study proposes NeuroFusion-FT, a multimodal deep learning framework integrating structural T1-weighted MRI, FDG-PET metabolic imaging, and structured clinical–cognitive variables for early dementia subtype classification. Separate EfficientNet-B3 networks were employed for MRI and PET feature extraction using a 2.5D representation strategy, while clinical features were modeled using a multilayer perceptron. The extracted embeddings were fused through a late-fusion architecture to generate final five-class predictions (Normal, Alzheimer’s disease, Frontotemporal dementia, Lewy body dementia, and Vascular dementia). The proposed system achieved an overall accuracy of 0.921 with a macro F1-score of 0.916. Advanced agreement metrics demonstrated strong reliability, including a Cohen’s Kappa of 0.902 and Quadratic Weighted Kappa of 0.935. Macro Sensitivity reached 0.915, indicating balanced subtype detection, while Macro Specificity of 0.979 reflected excellent false-positive control. Ablation analysis confirmed that multimodal fusion significantly outperformed MRI-only, PET-only, and imaging-only fusion baselines. Explainability analysis using Grad-CAM and lesion-based ROI overlap further validated anatomical plausibility. The proposed framework demonstrates that integrating structural, metabolic, and clinical biomarkers enhances diagnostic precision, robustness, and interpretability, offering a clinically meaningful approach for early dementia detection and differential diagnosis.

title: Quantum-Resistant Two-Party Integrity Framework for Secure Cloud Storage of Medical Videos
authors: M. Lakshmanan, Nilesh Ashtankar, Joshuva Arockia Dhanraj, Sriramkumar R, R. Tamilamuthan, Shreyas Rajendra Hole
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Medical videos could be (like routine endoscopy and echo cine studies) stored in cloud VNAs and PACS for prospects of longitudinal analysis. However, even with their regular operation in classical RSA/ECDSA-based integrity guarantees, they are still, expectedly, vulnerable by an oncoming quantum fleet. Largely shoulder-to-shoulder with traditional ones and file-based, existing cloud-audit systems continue to abound. This paper presents 2P-QIV, a quantum-resistant two-party integrity scheme for medical videos stored in the cloud. 2P-QIV chunks en bloc at GOP borders by enhancing a streaming-aware mechanism, with SHAKE256- hash chains doing this per chunk, where each short "epoch" is handled by dual post-quantum signatures (ML-DSA + daily-rotated SLH-DSA), and an independent auditor cosigns transparency log checkpoints, which ensures verifiability for privacy-preserving challenge audits without revealing pixels. Our prototype was implemented using liboqs v0.10. 1 on an S3- compatible object store and evaluated on Kvasir-Capsule and EchoNet-Dynamic datasets, showing (i) slightly above 11% storage overhead, (ii) higher throughput rates of 26-34% relative to a classical RSA-based single-party baseline, and (iii) scaled compromise windows of ≤24 hours. 2P-QIV anticipates quantum-era needs for an auditable integrity foundation of a prolonged medical-video archive.

title: A Comprehensive Power Quality Evaluation Method Based on Asymmetric Closeness Degree and Improved Cloud Model
authors: Hongbo Yu, Taotao Huang, Yuanguo Luo, Zhou Wu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: In order to address the issues of uncertainty and subjectivity in existing power quality assessment methods, a comprehensive power quality evaluation method based on asymmetric closeness degree and improved cloud model is proposed. Firstly, a power quality assessment system is established based on the cloud model, taking into account the randomness and fuzziness at the boundaries of its classification levels. Secondly, a new cloud entropy value is obtained by a cloud entropy optimization algorithm that considers the characteristics of two different types of cloud entropy, thereby deriving an improved cloud model to calculate the correlation degree of each indicator with the corresponding quality levels. Finally, the closeness degree vector between each indicator and the feature subset corresponding to its quality level is determined through asymmetric closeness degree. The TOPSIS method is used to define the positive and negative ideal levels of the indicators, and the final power quality assessment level is judged by comparing the closeness distance between each closeness degree vector and the positive and negative ideal levels. The effectiveness and accuracy of the proposed method are verified using actual $\mathbf{1 0 k V}$ busbar data from a substation in a practical engineering case.
